# Thread: Interesting hot spring in japan
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-hot-spring-in-japan.21605/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### arwin — 2018-07-10T07:42:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-hot-spring-in-japan.21605/post-245498>

hi, I’m visiting Ibusuki, Kyūshū, Japan. This is a a famous place for “sunamushi” sandbath. It is a volcanic area and hot spring water flow to the beach and heat the sand. A ditch is dug, the person lies down, and the hot sand is shovelled onto the whole body. This lasts for 10/15 minutes and the effect is amazing. Very powerful. The whole body feels like a giant heartbeat. Apart from the effect of the Onsen water and the sweating I think there must be a tremendous earthing effect. If you visit Japan, please try it.i read that the American Indians used to bury people in a similar fashion for some diseases. Afterwards you can eat sashimi and drink green tea - perfect !!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-10T14:22:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-hot-spring-in-japan.21605/post-245510>

Another reason kids love to be buried in the sand on the beach.....it works.

### NADme — 2018-07-10T11:59:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-and-nnemf.21606/post-245507>

Well Jack, you are not as dumb as Janz looks. I am about 3/4 of the way through your vid on 5G. I have many comments and a few intuitive theories that may serve in future exploration since we all cant obviously move to the equator. As John Lennon said, there are no problems, only solutions. This is were I get semi frustrated reading your forums. It seems much of the time you point to the big green monster without a solid means to mitigate.

In a past life I was a classical guitarist who studied with the worlds best. Harmonics and the effects on the human psyche was at the forefront of my attention for years. The reason being is that I had amazing luminaries shape me to understand that performance was not about entertainment.

IT WAS ABOUT BEING A DOCTOR OF ETHOS!...emphasis added

Sidebar: The NAD story is essential to me not for the reasons most assert. The Ca homeostasis is key and the three ribose expressions from that coenzyme creates intracellular homeostasis of Ca2+. It was asserted in commentary on my reads that lack of intracellular Ca homeostasis was at the root of most cancers. I would appreciate your comments on that notion.

Where does this self inflicted idea of being a Doctor of Ethos come from? Pre Renaissance, Medieval, two texts existed. I have not tried to see if there were any available in modern translation, assuredly would have been Greek etymology. They were known as the doctrine of ethos and doctrine of pathos. **Rx for the moving of emotions.**

In our society, with the exception of Jazz, we listen to music in major (Ionian) and minor (Aolian) keys. Keys are also known as modes. There were 5 other modes in addition to the two cited. Think of modes as moving the start point of the octave and centering the start at various root or tonic notes within the octave. By example C to C, all white keys, in the key of C major (Ionian). In the key of G you should have a black key F# (f sharp). However within the modes there were no black keys also known as accidentals, realizing G to G all white keys as the mixolydian mode, based on the 5th, C1 D2 E3 F4 G5 A6 B7.

That interval from the tonic of C to the 5th at G is known as the DOMINANT.

This is where it gets good: They would shape character by listening to certain modes. A warrior would listen to the dominant/mixolydian because of the aggression it would instill. Other modes such as locrian or phyrgian were somber and dark and applied to poets and those to induce the melancholy you hear all through Renaissance music.

**Fast forward:** Here we are confronted with all of the criminal atrocities rendered by a 5G assault and make no mistake Jack they are picking up where Maurice Strong left off regarding eugenics and Agenda 21 published by the UN, restated by the Georgia Stones and since the genome has been mapped seek to reduce population to 500 million globally. They the proverbial do not need us anymore. The world of have and have not as two on different sides of the coin is over now with AI and blockchain instigation.

As I listen to you pointing out the problems as you do so well it makes me reflect on past hypothesis and how it may possibly give us a tool or tools to push back.

One of my projects was to use tactile transducers to be able to sync your resonant signature and then to introduce an inverted wave less 1 less full form (causing near wave form flat line), similar to a helix, to see the effects on the subjects density. I theorized that it would impact density and enable what I was developing as intelligence infusion, where information on any given subject would be infused since water has memory. I also use to float tank extensively.

**Sidebar:** Did you know that when MgSO4 (Epsom salts) has sound projected on it that it will increase in temperature? Sound converted to heat.

With everything going on with all of mans brilliance as we race to assimilation or self destruction, it strikes me that we all can't wear faraday cages.

**POTENTIAL #1
HOWEVER:** As extrapolated from above: What if within the octave as you cite we were able to develop a means to use the modes and tetra modes combined with sound induction to cancel the signal or modify the nnEMF wave form assaults with a self-imposed inverted or cancelling signal in such away that we preserve ourselves?

**POTENTIAL #2**
I had a friend who made a Tesla cloud buster. I tried it Jack, I was literally erasing clouds from the end of his driveway. When Fukushima happened and we witnessed the radiation move in clumps, not waves, via the jet stream that comes in over BC/Vancouver, turns South down through the great plains and the continues East when hitting Texas almost following Interstate 10 as considered the primary path of that jet stream.

**My friend theorized that we could set up a number of these cloud busters and use it as a "Harmonic Fence."**

The G5 map and the metros as you cited may be the explanation for the phenomena I have not been able to explain.

I live in close proximity to the Kennedy Space Center and use to work Shuttle ops and other Space transport systems. This place is turning into a real shit hole. Anyhow, there has been on at least two occasions that I went to TN and DC and started dumping water like crazy, 20 lbs in a few days. I was pissing to the extent that the boy in the Brussels fountain had nothing on me. I return to Florida and it all stacks back on. I tested for mold exposure, negative.

When I backdrop with your theory about cellular dehydration, sodium and potassium wasting come to minds but also the effects of the 5G as you pointed out and how maybe some harmonic shift enabled the water shedding.

As a young man I was able to sense tube TV's, that 60 cycle 3 phase running through tubes, being on in peoples homes from the sidewalks definitely their front doors. It felt like a buzzing or vibration that was distinct to me. I am highly sensitive to sound and harmonics, besides my physical but even more because we were taught as a musician to instill the ethos of the piece. To make people cry over a lament or get up and want to march when playing a march, etc. I am hypersensitive to resonance to the extent I can reproduce 440 cps from memory and can feel overall resonant signatures from people instinctively. It can be a big nuisance at times.

This is a free form rant, I understand it is all over the place, I sense relevance.

**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ossa — 2018-07-10T23:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-am-sun-the-most-important.21611/post-245569>

Just wondering the science behind why am sun is more important than mid day or evening. Do you not get about the same frequencies of light except uvb which you get mid day?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-11T03:56:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-am-sun-the-most-important.21611/post-245585>

Watch the Vermont 2017 and 2018 talks. Full explanation.

### WalterNL — 2018-07-11T11:00:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-am-sun-the-most-important.21611/post-245604>

> [Ossa said:](/goto/post?id=245569)
>
> Just wondering the science behind why am sun is more important than mid day or evening. Do you not get about the same frequencies of light except uvb which you get mid day?

Sunlight frequencies are not balanced the same throughout the day, and biological processes follows a circadian rhythm. Meaning that your body does other things in the morning then it does around noon or in the evening. The AM frequencies are crucial for proper circadian rhythm of every cell in your body.

### Ossa — 2018-07-12T21:17:03+0300

### Sajid Mahmood — 2018-07-12T21:26:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-am-sun-the-most-important.21611/post-245763>

It's about 6,500K around midday. I thought first the colour temperature drops back down after midday but that is incorrect. The colour temperature keeps increasing throughout the day until sunset. From 1600k to 16,000k at sunset. The colour temperature begins dropping after sunset. The frequencies of the sun changes throughout the day to help our eye clock know what time it is. The sun is like a source of wireless data and information to us.

### Sajid Mahmood — 2018-07-18T19:13:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-am-sun-the-most-important.21611/post-246134>

[https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/do-you-want-to-start/1310514499012901/](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/do-you-want-to-start/1310514499012901/):

"So in the AM sunrise the color temperature of light is only 1800 Kelvin, at 10 AM it is 4000 K, at solar noon it is 5500 K, at high noon (12-1:30PM) it rises to 12,000 K, and at dusk it is 16,000K. It builds as the day goes on."

[https://jackkruse.com/reality-2-can-life-skirt-uncertainty-principle-second-law-thermodynamics/](https://jackkruse.com/reality-2-can-life-skirt-uncertainty-principle-second-law-thermodynamics/):

"The photons come in all colors or frequencies because the sun’s light varies by location altitude and time of day. For example, the AM sunrise color temperature is 1800K while the sunset is 16,000K"

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/timing-post-leptin-rx.20347/#post-226798](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/timing-post-leptin-rx.20347/#post-226798)

"realize at sunrise we're at 1600K and 1 hr prior to sunset 16,000 K. Then in 1 hour your at zero K."

Someone asked Dr Jack Kruse on the Vermont 2017 video on the comments section: "Why are sunsets so important to a mitochindriac?"

Dr Jack Kruse replied:
"Because the color temperature of PM light rises and this is the stimulus to the melanopsin system and the activation of melatonin after its aromatic rings were excited by AM IRA and UVA light. This is also a big leptin and amylin trigger too for the same reasons."

### shaneoco — 2019-06-14T23:37:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-am-sun-the-most-important.21611/post-272670>

I have very disturbed sleep issues which I believe are deuterium related. I often lie awake for hours each night. It's the northern European summer where I am now (Ireland) which means it's bright at 4am. Getting up at this time every morning would seem totally counterproductive formme trying to get some quality sleep/rest (as often I'd only be falling back to sleep around this time after being awake for hours), however staying in bed means I miss the am sunlight. Usual wake up time would be 8.30am.
Wonder could anyone comment on this?
Thanks!

### Sue-UK — 2019-06-15T11:16:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-am-sun-the-most-important.21611/post-272684>

> [shaneoco said:](/goto/post?id=272670)
>
> I have very disturbed sleep issues which I believe are deuterium related. I often lie awake for hours each night. It's the northern European summer where I am now (Ireland) which means it's bright at 4am. Getting up at this time every morning would seem totally counterproductive formme trying to get some quality sleep/rest (as often I'd only be falling back to sleep around this time after being awake for hours), however staying in bed means I miss the am sunlight. Usual wake up time would be 8.30am.
> Wonder could anyone comment on this?
> Thanks!

When people talk about deuterium and sleep, I think it could be a case of either too much deuterium, or not enough deuterium. Not enough deuterium sounds a bit counter intuitive but you deplete deuterium during REM sleep, which implies to me that a certain amount may be necessary to enter REM sleep, and tribes who spend months of the year struggling to find enough often deuterium loaded foods, can have less than optimal amounts of sleep, even at the equator. So I've wondered if (for example) potentially, a long term strict keto diet could, in terms of deuterium, be a sleep disrupting equivalent. If someone has too much deuterium, or its in the wrong place, then being anal about getting circadian rhythm on track will help. The enterocytes of the gut turning over in line with their circadian rhythm helps stop excessive deuterium accumulation, get the day-night circadian cycle firm and there's a better chance of melatonin doing its thing and someone going through the sleep cycles, making use of REM to deplete deuterium etc.

Around summer and winter solstices are the most difficult times in terms of sleep for me, but for different reasons. Even if you are blue blocking/ using iris software, your post is timed as being posted at 9.37 - that is just after sunset for me, and with such a short night, its a question of getting offline much earlier, see the sunset and go to bed. If I'm tired earlier its be outside for a while to experience some of the transitioning frequencies, and go a bit earlier if necessary while sleep pressure is strong. If you are awake at 4 a.m. you could try getting outside and seeing the sunrise and experiencing some of the morning transition frequencies for a few minutes, and then going back to bed. If I have to do this, I then leave the curtain and preferably the window open, to let the natural light hit my eyes, even if its through closed lids.

### shaneoco — 2019-06-16T12:44:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-am-sun-the-most-important.21611/post-272724>

Thanks for the reply Sue. Interesting what you say about a lack of Deuterium affecting sleep, potentially.
Your idea of getting up for the am light at 4am and going back to bed may be an option, but that would only be after 4-5 hours of very broken sleep at best. I literally feel like a zombie the next day unless I at least stay in bed until at least 8-9am, which I know means I miss all the am sun. Work at the moment allows me to be outside almost all day after so I think that might be a worthy trade off for now, and hopefully I will see some circadian rebalance happening soon.

### Lahelada — 2019-08-07T18:28:52+0300

### kris90 — 2019-08-07T20:13:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-am-sun-the-most-important.21611/post-275598>

This question gets asked a lot, so I thought I would share my 2 cents.

I really believe light's interaction with water is the key in driving life. Of course the magnetism is critically important as well as the force that drives energy flow. Gerald Pollock's work on the EZ makes understanding AM sunlight critical:

1\. Red light turns water into a battery (EZ) that powers life
2\. Your cells are loaded with water that needs the charge of this red light
3\. Once the EZ is built, it's refraction shifts to 270 nm light which is UV
4\. UVA arrives later in the morning
5\. The combination of UVA and IRA light works a magical dance to create UVC laser light within our blood plasma through a nuclear reaction we learned about in Vermont 2018
6\. That UVC light generated from this AM stimulus is what programs the aromatic amino acids to build dopamine, serotonin, melatonin and probably many more chemicals that control the timing mechanism (circadian rhythm) in our body

So you can see how wise nature is in preparing our body to use the solar stimulus to build wellness. Red light from AM sunlight is step 1 in preparing us for optimal each and everyday. UVA is the next critical transition. Of course the other key is the evening when color temperature peaks while UVA goes away, and melanopsin is programmed to begin melatonin secretion after 4 hours of darkness when color temperature falls off a cliff post-sunset.

That whole rhythm all begins with the AM sunlight stimulus. You cannot jump to step 3 or 4 or 5 when you've missed step 1.

# Thread: Hello!  SO happy to have found this!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-so-happy-to-have-found-this.21612/>

### kellyjwarren — 2018-07-10T23:59:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-so-happy-to-have-found-this.21612/post-245570>

I am in the process of receiving a 20-year-overdue Biotoxin Illness diagnosis, following Lyme disease in 1997 (not properly diagnosed or treated until 2001 when I was bedridden). After trying just about everything to restore my health and relieve pain/dysfunction with extremely limited success, I am hopeful that I finally will have a correct diagnosis and a path forward!

I would like to dive headfirst into a restorative eating plan, and have a few questions about the basics I have read about the Epi-Paleo plan:

1\. Do oysters have to be raw? Cooked? Canned?
2\. I have my own flock of free-range chickens...How do I know if I can/should consume their eggs on this?
3\. I am hoping to receive a copy of the book soon, but in the meantime is there a summary post of all the recommended foods? Is coconut milk and coconut meat part of the diet or just coconut oil?
4\. Other than cilantro, are there suggested or banned greens to consume? Does high-quality cilantro essential oil work as well?

In addition, what are Dr. Kruse's thoughts on the Shoemaker protocol? I have a TGF-beta > 12,000, low CSF glucose, and central diabetes insipidus. Still waiting on other labs (C3, C4, CD profiles, and others...). While I have read that DI can be caused by biotoxins, I have had multiple brain MRIs since 2013 showing a dangling fragment of pituitary. My Cleveland Clinic MD thinks my severe postpartum hemorrhages incited an autoimmune lymphocytic hypophysitis that continues to attack what remains. (No intracranial HTN causing PES...remaining gland is not flattened.)

Any other quick-start suggestions you may have are greatly appreciated! I will be reviewing the Leptin and CT information as recommended in the welcome video.

Fingers crossed and mind open,

Kelly

### caroline — 2018-07-11T02:58:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-so-happy-to-have-found-this.21612/post-245575>

Hi Kelly and welcome....
Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. your Mom's and Gran's health history as well.

Where do you live? work? your post code is important.

Until you get Jack's book which is the first couple of years of the blog....listen to all his podcasts, especially the nourish Vermont ones - there are three now. These are extremely important..... as well as all Jack's other podcasts. They are a source of fast and easier to understand information.

Might I suggest that you become a "gold" member. You will have access to the monthly webinars and the follow up live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything. The June Q&A lasted for 4 hours and 17 minutes! We have a thread about that one - please have a look. The Q&A's alone are worth the price of membership.

Lots of people say they can't afford it ...but ....you have been sick for a long time - and now you have found the right place for healing. It won't be easy or fast .......you will be on a tremendous healing/learning curve.

Jackis all about the three legged stool ......light, water and magnetism. This won't cost you a thing.

Get outside every morning for the sunrise, with as little clothes on as possible, with barefoot on the ground. Make sure to see every sunset too. This is all about resetting your circadian rhythm.

On the home page there are easy start guides. Have a look at the leptin reset. Also read about C/T - it is a game changer.

There is a welcome from Jack on the home page and how to get started.

Dr. Kruse has also started a thread about the importance of newbies - please read.

Don't be afraid to ask questions if you don't know something ...... There are lots of great people here to help ....and Dr. K. reads journals.

Also - there is a little book by John Ott .....Health and light - please read

### WalterNL — 2018-07-11T12:41:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-so-happy-to-have-found-this.21612/post-245610>

Welcome. Check out all the Rx's in my signature.

Thank you, Caroline!

I received the Epi-Paelo Rx book from the library, and am currently in my second reading while taking notes/creating questions. Will try and reserve Ott's book next.

I will look for the optimal journal section online to get my personal one started. I plan on becoming a gold member in the future, but am currently an unemployed single parent trying desperately to make ends meet until I am well enough to return to work.

More lab results are in... elevated MMP-9, lymphocytes, and C4a... and an entire host of parasites, yeast, mold, fungi, and bacteria in my "sterile" spinal fluid, which helps to explain the low CSF glucose result. My best guess is this is secondary to leaky gut caused by CIRS/Biotoxin Illness. My follow-up appt is on Tuesday for the treatment plan.

In the meantime, I am doing the Leptin Rx, have been watching sunrises and sunsets, walking barefoot outdoors (which I often do anyway), and reading all I can about the best daily activities/nutrition for healing. I was pleased to see mention of Shoemaker's protocol in Epi-Paleo Rx. After 20 years of debilitating chronic illness following Lyme disease, I think I need some major detoxing for the lifestyle changes to work best for me. Luckily, progress is being made in the treatment of CIRS based on a person's specific genetics that create the biotoxin accumulation susceptibility. I'm curious if Dr. Kruse is aware of this development and what his thoughts are.

### Dean6789 — 2018-07-12T00:05:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-animals-edible.21620/post-245676>

What about all these nice grass fed animals we're eating -- let's say some good beef is raised 30 miles outside of Colorado Springs airport -- Would you eat it?

If not, since everything is getting zapped with 5G, what's the best sourcing for the land animal part of our diet?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-12T01:01:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-animals-edible.21620/post-245681>

Good question that needs an optical scanner to know for sure.......

### Mhoej — 2018-07-12T13:38:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-animals-edible.21620/post-245716>

I always had second thoughts about cows habitually grazing under powerlines. Would be interesting to see different values in the meat, such as DHA and Deuterium. We know grass-fed vs. grain-fed has an effect on the latter.

Hi everyone,

I'm new to this forum. I just discovered Jack through YouTube and Podcasts. I'm not sure where to post this so I'll start with the beginners area. I had a sudden health issue a few months ago in January and ever since the issues continued. My symptoms are brain fog and dizziness, shortness of breath, chest pain, body feels weak. Went to the doctors office. They found nothing wrong with me in the blood test, urine test, and heart ultra sound.

I just want to know where I should start reading and learning in this forum. I see there are lab sections, what kind of testing I should do to find out my current health issue.

Thanks so much for your help and pointers.

### caroline — 2018-07-14T08:29:57+0300

Hi and welcome.

Please start a journal in the Optimal journal section and give Dr. K. your health history, also your Mom's and Gran's.

Where do you live? work?

Have you watched Jack's Nourish Vermont Podcasts? there are three now and they are hugely important.

Please read the beginners guides etc. and then look thru the list of blogs and see what might be relevant to you.

Do you have Jack's book? The Epi Paleo RX ....it is the first few years of the blog.

If you can manage it - become a Gold member and you will have access to all Jack's monthly blogs and the follow up live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything.

We have a thread on the June Q&A - have a look.

What can we call you? A better pic would be nice - Jack Likes to know who he is talking to.

### caroline — 2018-07-15T09:49:52+0300

### JanSz — 2018-07-14T14:02:26+0300

Knowledge narrows your perspective while experience broadens it by raising your curiosity.
I spent too much of my life gaining knowledge and not focusing on wisdom.......
Your perspective on people comes from the cage your "perspective" is held captive in.
Wise folks understand there no facts, only interpretations.
This is why one person's "crazy" is another person's reality.

### NADme — 2018-07-14T15:12:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/toms-cruising-with-jack-a-totally-excellent-journal.21635/post-245878>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=245877)
>
> Knowledge narrows your perspective while experience broadens it by raising your curiosity.
> I spent too much of my life gaining knowledge and not focusing on wisdom.......
> Your perspective on people comes from the cage your "perspective" is held captive in.
> Wise folks understand there no facts, only interpretations.
> This is why one person's "crazy" is another person's reality.
> This makes intuition and insanity relative.
> It depends on "who" has "who" locked in 'what' cage.

Curiosity killed the cat. Directed analytics wins the day. I hold out that wanting to know something is not a curiosity. Where as curiosity is propelled by desire and wanting to learn predicated by something far less whimsical such as a cause, objective or highly defined modus operandi.

My perspective on people comes from first hand experience, which we are the net sum total of, not a cage. The species is a vile one at best that consumes, excretes and reproduces, all within an entitlement backdrop. A parasite as an individual a virus as a collective. My days of service at the feet of the addict, afflicted, battered or elderly, is blessed by God, challenged by the recipient. Challenged to fit the prosecutor, victim rescuer model in its most classic sense.

I have time and time again laid witness to those from all walks be restored in various capacities from a wide range of bondage and can concisely say that once the receiver perceives no longer a need to be assisted their true colors appear and it is a world of ugly no one wants to talk about. There are exceptions, but at the core the overwhelming majority act and are parasitic, feeding off the tangibles and psychic energy of others, much like social vampires, with indignation an entitlement. Where low carbon footprints have no place in the minds of the locust. Yet, I am required to serve and embrace them. Paradox?........like no tomorrow.

The addiction treatment center I own has been more than a dip into the bowels of humanity it has been full immersion into the cease-pool of life. Having to remain impartial to the recreational user who has trampled over family, their children and everyone in their circle, fortified with lies and acts that are over the pale. Trying to stay on that bow as the waves of deceit break over me has me white knuckle grabbing for the rails.

What is worse?.........the private sector who somehow positions to accreditation (joint commission) , pharmaceuticals, politicians and at the top of the evil, the insurance carriers, who make a vertical market out of others pain and struggles who then exploit to suck every last dime from the poor while boxing them into a world that never allows them to redeem themselves. If anyone reading this ever had a felony conviction for possession or use they know that society is not very fond of them!

My cage is at full 5G bandwidth. Segmentation is a non issue with me. At total sum, as with the classic sonata form of composition, yields a unity via diversity perspective that still renders a ruling of parasite/virus worthy of extermination. These notions, the same as those backed by Agenda 21 and eugenics fans. Those finding exception will find that exception is quickly overruled, zero'ed out and have no place in this swill.

Subjugation to a "higher power" which most do not define, is very defined to me, and not by me, and is the only reason my engagement with humanity is one of humanitarian or servant, most certainly not warranted by the species nature.

My ultimate plight, to get as much yardage, hope to usher original thought, partake in composite knowledge and to see myself, with all of my health contentions, somehow do the hat trick and live an extraordinary longevity. Sounds conflicted, right?

Well I have this notion that this 4th dimensional state has intra and multidimensional access points, some may say portals others riffs. Crowley cited them as riffs in space time. Whatever the label, a state then when combined with honed abilities can tether out and reflex back, affecting this realm to a magnitude that would in the flesh require a full on, blood letting, revolt. As above so below.

Resonance, light, water..........I have been on the cusp for years. Most of what we engage in trying to get our meat wrappers to obey our notion of health is folly. Take a look around, does it look like any of the enlightened are broad banding and causing a paradigm shift in society at large, emphasis on at large? If truth is the broadsword, then it has been given a dull blade these days for if truth by Kruse or any other school of thought was to be so substantial, it would have already had a cause effect that is readily identified by even the moron or dolt.

My presence here is because Jack does come from a varied vantage and deserves at least some time to vett.

# Thread: Klub membership clarification
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/klub-membership-clarification.21636/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### karenr — 2018-07-14T19:03:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/klub-membership-clarification.21636/post-245886>

Hi folks.. Exploring new options, I'm needing some clarification about what's included in Klub membership. Thought I’d start here before going to Support..

1.  At Silver right now, I see on the Klub Dashboard the recent podcast on “Blue Light, Skin and Suicide in Music and Shift Workers: The Melanopsin Link.” Says $9.99 for Silver members. Are these going to be occasional podcasts for an extra charge for all levels of Klub membership?
2.  What does family membership for Titanium and above mean - can it include people in other locations, or just household members?
3.  Each family member has their own login?
4.  Are the monthly webinars not going to be video anymore? I find it much easier to process the info in video or just audio format compared to written. Q&A's must be awesome!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-14T19:26:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/klub-membership-clarification.21636/post-245887>

1\. When we have to hire outside contractors to bring you new content members will get very discounted rates to cover the costs of their travel and editing. The public is paying 80 bucks for the same video. The public is paying most of the freight. I fronted all the costs and all the risks. As the fixed costs vanish, then member prices will fall or go away completely. Right now the cost is quite low because the public has shown a ton of interest.
2\. It means that you and a spouse and kids can do things as a unit. This does not include extended family. Members asked for it so I am amenable to trying it as a trial.
3\. Contact support for details
4\. WE were asked to do more writing and less video so July 2018 was the first time we've done it. We are collecting feedback on this from current members.

### karenr — 2018-07-14T19:36:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/klub-membership-clarification.21636/post-245888>

Terrific, thanks Jack!
I personally get a lot from the conversational or interview format, a very dynamic kind of interaction. But what works for the audience is also about what structure suits the info and the presenter best.

# Thread: New here with skin in the game! (cfs/chronic lyme)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-with-skin-in-the-game-cfs-chronic-lyme.21638/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nancy Hampel — 2018-07-15T02:08:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-with-skin-in-the-game-cfs-chronic-lyme.21638/post-245893>

Hello all! I found Jack K about 2 weeks ago after having a hard and long crash that was unexpected. I am 51 with "chronic CFS/Lyme" for a lot of years.

When I read the adrenal RX post a huge bell went off...none of the holistic docs/clinic/treatments had addressed the brain in that way. I read a ton and started the leptin RX and sunrise watching/sunning/earthing .

That pulled me out of the crash. When I say skin in the game..I mean it..I live in forested country by myself, so I can put all my skin in the sun at any time and I am doing so! Also no shoes outside..barefoot or moccasins, got the blue blocking glasses. Already had turned of breakers at night and no wifi or cell reception at house.

I start coaching with Nathan W next week and started CT yesterday. Just joined the Silver Klub level.

This is just crazy good info. I am so happy to be here.

I have never been able to get up early, and after one day of leptin RX I woke up at sunrise and this has continued every day! No trouble with blood sugar dips either, which astounded me. And extra flabby fat is coming off. All in about two weeks time.

Would love to hear from others with longer term CFS/lyme and hacks.

Cheers...Nancy

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-15T03:53:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-with-skin-in-the-game-cfs-chronic-lyme.21638/post-245895>

**LYME DISEASE** is a manifestation of a poor environment that changes the electric charge in water to decrease the water’s ability to absorb any UV light to build the redox potential of the cell. As such the immune system is poorly powered and loses information to properly program T cells and B cells. When this occurs then disease previously kept under wraps become serious problems. This mimics the webinar I did on Uganda children with typhoid and malaria that my friend and videographer Ben Smith filmed in Africa in 2016. This wisdom was used in helping the lady below with some sage advice harnessed from nature. Mariangela Mancuso reviewed Dr. Jack Kruse — 5 stars. Why did she do this?

Mariangela said in May 2018, “I started with Dr. Jack Kruse’s protocols in August of 2017. I started to improve immediately when I implemented sunrise, sunset, all 4 limbs on the ground and unplugged wifi and no artificial light after dark. I did his leptin reset and cold thermogenesis too and use blue blocker glasses. I continue to do his methods and learn more from him. I am “cured” of a diagnosis of neuropsychiatric Lyme disease. I had seizures, catatonic episodes, tremors, shaking, confusion, speech problems, comprehension problems, severe debilitating pain and fatigue, got horribly sick when I ate, balance and walking problems, hysteria, phobias, double vision, hallucinations- and more, sad to say. I really think I just had a nnEMF issue. I am COMPLETELY symptom-free. Dr. Kruse continues to give me the knowledge of how to live a complete life. Thank you, Dr. Kruse!” When you know better you do better because you have skin in the game. [https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse/posts/10214224366181866](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse/posts/10214224366181866)

# Thread: Sun and breathing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-and-breathing.21640/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### arwin — 2018-07-15T12:59:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-and-breathing.21640/post-245910>

hi, for the first time I took off my shirt and spent some time getting am sun (it’s pretty difficult to find a place to take your shirt off in Japan!). Taking the shirt off made for a completely different experience for me. After 15 minutes, my eyes closed (couldn’t stop them), my breath count dropped and became almost imperceptible. This just happened naturally. I stayed for an hour crossed legged on the beach because it was so “easy”. The simple lesson I took from this is that consciously controlling the breath is no comparison to letting the sun do it for you. I’ll never try to meditate indoors again; it’s too much like hard work!

### drezy — 2018-07-15T22:13:18+0300

Think melatonin is only a sleep story?

More melatonin (from AM sunlight + darkness at night) ups efficiency of respiratory proteins (shortening their distance) in mitochondria.

⚡What does that mean? Better electron tunneling = increased fuel efficiency and a faster spinning ATPase because H+ is in the matrix and deuterium is not allowed there. A breakdown in the melanopsin/retinol systems lowers melatonin and allows deuterium to INFECT KREB's BICYCLE.

# Thread: Nancy's Optimal Journal - cfs/lyme
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nancys-optimal-journal-cfs-lyme.21643/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nancy Hampel — 2018-07-16T00:36:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nancys-optimal-journal-cfs-lyme.21643/post-245941>

Hello! I am 51, live in north central Idaho about 45th parallel. Moved to 20 acres last year in forested area further surrounded by wheat farming. No cell reception at house. In between work and planned to make living working on this place offering small workshops/retreats/airbnb/coaching. Very little exposure to blue light now.

Health history: Late 20s viral onset of CFS.
6 month before onset I had 4 hour surgery on face to check a neuroma. Moody and low energy after.,
Then UTI, then flu with hives on chest and got abx and crashed into CFS. Was in grad school and took medical withdrawal.

I lived in Madison, WI and crashed in spring.

Did MANY holistic protocols, saw many docs, was able to work on my own terms but never got well...crashed in early 40s and got lyme diagnosis, Never did long term abx for lyme.

CCSVI - venous intervention done about 6 years ago...veins were partially blocked due to lyme and blood was backflowing to the brain. Had MRI then. Helped with brain fog to a certain degree.

MRI found my neck is very very straight. Got some oral inserts to help with oxygen flow to brain..retainers..but stopped using them as I got cavities and were very difficult.

Did bio Identical cortisol for about 6 years and bio identical thyroid for about 4. Got of both of them 3.5 years ago.

Major crash this spring. Then found this info. Leptin Rx helped pull me out of crash.

Main sypmtoms: Brain fatigue/scattered thinking/brain fog/fatigue...comes and goes in day or some good days then bad ones.
Not enough brain power to follow through on many things consistently.
Intense fatigue at times for day or weeks.
Exercise intolerance. Hard to tell how much activity I can do without crashing. Right now legs get tired walking up hill.
\-------
Mother: Died of brain tumor at age 62 and had lung cancer 7 years previously. Smoker. I was not breast fed.

Grandmother. Lived in remote Colorado mining area and committed suicide after her husband, my grandfather, died. My mother was about 5 years old and only child. This was about 1930

Great Grandmother: Died in childbirth or shortly after giving birth to only child, my grandmother. age 19. It appears her mother was healthy...long lived and had large family. Something seemed to happen when they moved from the EAst to Guffey Colorado...then later female generations ....my mother, grandmother and great gran...died young.

\-------
Currently doing Leptin rx for about 2 weeks, CT for 3 days, AM sunrise with feet on ground, hour or more of am sun ground with all skin exposed.
90 percent of walking is barefoot or moccasins outside
Blue blocker glasses
bak soda/ac vinegar in water
ordered Davinia water

Question: What labs should I get? I will private pay, so want optimal ones, necessary but not extra.

Question: How can I further optimize given this history?

Thank you!!!
Nancy

> MRI found my neck is very very straight. Got some oral inserts to help with oxygen flow to brain..retainers..but stopped using them as I got cavities and were very difficult.
>
> Did bio Identical cortisol for about 6 years and bio identical thyroid for about 4. Got of both of them 3.5 years ago.
>
> Major crash this spring. Then found this info. Leptin Rx helped pull me out of crash.
>
> Main sypmtoms: Brain fatigue/scattered thinking/brain fog/fatigue...comes and goes in day or some good days then bad ones.
> Not enough brain power to follow through on many things consistently.
> Intense fatigue at times for day or weeks.
> Exercise intolerance. Hard to tell how much activity I can do without crashing. Right now legs get tired walking up hill.
> \-------
> Mother: Died of brain tumor at age 62 and had lung cancer 7 years previously. Smoker. I was not breast fed.
>
> Grandmother. Lived in remote Colorado mining area and committed suicide after her husband, my grandfather, died. My mother was about 5 years old and only child. This was about 1930
>
> Great Grandmother: Died in childbirth or shortly after giving birth to only child, my grandmother. age 19. It appears her mother was healthy...long lived and had large family. Something seemed to happen when they moved from the EAst to Guffey Colorado...then later female generations ....my mother, grandmother and great gran...died young.
>
> \-------
> Currently doing Leptin rx for about 2 weeks, CT for 3 days, AM sunrise with feet on ground, hour or more of am sun ground with all skin exposed.
> 90 percent of walking is barefoot or moccasins outside
> Blue blocker glasses
> bak soda/ac vinegar in water
> ordered Davinia water
>
>
> Question: What labs should I get? I will private pay, so want optimal ones, necessary but not extra.
>
> Question: How can I further optimize given this history?
>
> Thank you!!!
> Nancy

welcome, I'm from Door County, Wisconsin and Brookfield, WIsconsin and also have Lyme disease.

### Nancy Hampel — 2019-03-08T21:24:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nancys-optimal-journal-cfs-lyme.21643/post-264834>

Long time and here is my update: (20 plus years of cfs/lyme)

Started keto/sun cycles, face dunks, leptin reset etc in July 2018

Went to Yucatan for three weeks Jan/feb..felt great.

Now: Lost 2 pant sizes from July to December. Ups and downs with fatigue until going to Yucatan.

Since Yucatan and during (and adding carbs back in..stopping keto) I feel much like a person without cfs! For several weeks now.

Had a blip of couple weeks with head pressure, fatigue, and hot flashes. Added carbs back and all stopped!

I added ezekiel bread and grass fed kefir back in lat week.....but got inflammed and depressed and am taking them back out.

I am a little too thin now..did keto a little too long I think,

I could tell immediately in Yucatan that my body loved the grounding..I am quite sensitive to energies. I have fair skin and worked up to several hours per day in sun.

Went barefoot almost constantly, ate fish and veg and some fruit. Sunrise daily, red flashlight at night.

I also do herbs for CNS inflammation from lyme from Stephen Buhner's protocol. I feel they help.

No meds at all, no hormones. Now doing lugol's and magnesium supplements. Eating lots of seafood and lamb.

Loved Mexico!

Going back March 26 for two months. Considering moving there...not only for health..I think now I could maintain here...but I loved the people and the expat community aspect and culture and nature. I am now without obligations and love to travel. Changed my life, really.

Nancy, you learned something big about redox. GO FURTHER and build your MASTERPIECE with your new wisdom.

Every life I treat as a surgeon is a canvas and every interaction I now have with a person is a brush to me, therefore I've come to be more wise and now consider how I "touch" the paint before I act. # Thread: Losing weight with nutrition
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-nutrition.21644/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shely Propst — 2018-07-16T01:20:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-nutrition.21644/post-245944>

Hey Everyone,

New to the thread and Jack Kruse and I’m hooked. I may not understand all he says but it makes sense!

I’ve listened to all Vermont talks—twice and every podcast I can find. What brought me to this was that I recently came off my antidepressant —and two drugs that I went on because of some of my side effects from my antidepressant (high blood pressure and reflux). My sleep has been even more screwed up and my stomach issue (IBS) is back.

I’m getting my AM and PM sun—and all my Vitamin D from the sun using the dminder app. I live in KS.

My question is about calories in vs calories out and I’m a total believer that it’s more than calories in vs calories out. I’ve been a personal trainer and “nutritionist” for 12 years. I have a nutrition coach as well. I’ve seen many lose weight and many not lose weight. I’ve increased people’s calories and they lose weight. Etc. I’ve lost weight on cuts and trying to lower my body fat for competitions.

My question: (not sure how to phrase it). What is going on in the mitochondria and respiratory protons when someone loses weight—-without altering their environment?

I have people who’ve lost 70lb+ and I’ve watched others plateau—more often than lose weight. So what is going on inside—hormonalky—or whatever else when nutrition is altered?

Again, I do believe nutrition is only 1/3. For many reasons. I also know how to manipulate my own body to lose weight too.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-nutrition.21644/post-245959>

Hi Shely and welcome.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. health history and your Mom's and Gran's too.

where do you live? work?

The Vermont talks answer your questions.

Do you have Jack's book?

### Sue-UK — 2018-07-16T17:08:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-nutrition.21644/post-245983>

> [Shely Propst said:](/goto/post?id=245944)
>
> I have people who’ve lost 70lb+ and I’ve watched others plateau—more often than lose weight. So what is going on inside—hormonalky—or whatever else when nutrition is altered?
>
> Again, I do believe nutrition is only 1/3. For many reasons. I also know how to manipulate my own body to lose weight too.

Hi and welcome to the forum

A good thread to read is this one [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/) Notice that the only mention of food in the list is seafood at number 4. and "the order of the recipe is critical to the success."

A tool that you may find useful too is the quiz at [https://jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/](https://jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/). It will give you an idea of the types of technology to avoid or mitigate. For 70 lb overweight clients the intermittent fasting wouldn't come before they've regained leptin sensitivity (lots of info on leptin resistance on the public blogs - just do a search), but it gives an overview of the issues. Any 5G rollout is a whole new ballgame, but it could help with the basics.

I'd suggest investing in a gold membership. The webinars and Q and A's transformed my understanding of the mitochondrial dynamics, sleep etc and may also help you "fine tune" your own programme and individual advice to clients based on their own n=1 context.

### kris90 — 2018-07-19T22:33:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-nutrition.21644/post-246232>

It's pretty simple. People who lose weight from a calories/nutrition perspective: calorie restriction = more body fat being tapped into. It will always work short term, but long term depends on leptin sensitivity/resistance. It activates part of a metabolic pathway, but needs to be yoked to the environment. Intermittent Fasting WITHOUT calorie restriction activates the same pathway.

Your best blog to read: [https://jackkruse.com/the-holy-trinity-ct-4/](https://jackkruse.com/the-holy-trinity-ct-4/)

The environment is the only way to make calorie restriction occur naturally: Cold and UV/IR light do it. Can you still lose weight by restricting calories in a blue light/nnEMF world? Of course. This is called stem cell depletion, and most will end up binging and gaining the weight back as a protective mechanism, but those who have the "willpower" to keep going will end up looking like a superhero on the outside, while they drop dead of cancer or a heart attack in their 4th decade of life.

### JanSz — 2018-07-21T20:16:50+0300

(high blood pressure and reflux). My sleep has been even more screwed up and my stomach issue (IBS) is back.

# Thread: Grounding
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.21645/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sara — 2018-07-16T03:49:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.21645/post-245961>

I’ve been walking barefoot around my place for about a year. It was difficult at first; my foot hit the ground hard, heel first; I suppose that’s how I walk in shoes. It took a couple of weeks to find a flat and balanced step; now I can walk with silent ease. I ground at sunrise and sunset anywhere from a half hour to an hour. An afternoon grounding is not practical for me; I’m heavily shod, boots in the stirrups, but I do get plenty of sun.

I’ve found that grounding is powerfully up lifting even though I can’t actually feel the pulse of Nature beneath my feet; the land I walk on is bathed in blood and ferociously adversarial. It’s an aggrieved land, taken from the Apache, the Mexican and desecrated from cattle ranching. I’ve never been able to get a foothold here the way I could on Pueblo Lands, which are sorrowful and sweetly nostalgic; where I could feel the thrum of Natures’ heart beat and once, I heard Her voice. I connect more surely with sorrow than with anger; anger makes me mean where sorrow makes me thoughtful. But, you know, the beat of Her heart must be getting through by osmosis because together with the grounding effect of riding horses (ho ho ho), I’m beginning to feel less fragmented and more united; more whole. Grounding is also a time to get out of the mind and cozy up to the heart of the matter. In my case, that would be relinquishing the cares of the world and embracing Nature.

Morning grounding is full of life awakening: the scurryings of small creatures; birds chirping and picking through the fallen shavings of grass hay for seed; jack rabbits hopping across the meadow like kangaroos; skunks hightailing it home from the unmasking of dawn. I usually see at least one herd of elk pass through and the other day, as the sun rose out of the darkness, one of the largest elk herds I’ve ever seen appeared in the suns’ rays through the murmuring pines; there had to be about a hundred head and it took a good half hour for them to pass, bugling to their calves, which were plentiful. Time is not of the essence to elk unless they’re being hunted. It was a magnificent, leisurely elk walk; my grounding session turned into a barefoot photo shoot. And a couple of weeks ago, I was favored with the sight of bear; a rare occurrence. What I’d really like to see is a wolf. In all my travels into the interior of the wilderness and different areas of these environs; all my silent laying in wait, I have never caught a glimpse of wolf. I’ve seen just about everything else, but no wolf.

Evening grounding is more tranquil; it’s an interlude between the day and night life of the wild. Dusk is an amazing time when the condors come home to roost, circling above like airplanes landing in turn; rabbits and other small critters quit their horsing around and head for their nests and warrens; insects settle down their pesterings, leaving the field open for the no-see-ums and snakes have slithered back into their holes; elk and deer await the dark to come back out and browse and the birds quiet down for the night, except for a lone hummer once in awhile. If you’re lucky, you can espy an owl by the draw of its hoot. Last evening I came in with a baby preying mantis on my sleeve. How cool is that!?! And how fortunate to have surroundings to experience this brilliant display of life-giving life as I connect to the Earth in an otherwise artificial world.

### Joyfun — 2018-07-16T13:53:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.21645/post-245975>

> [Sara said:](/goto/post?id=245961)
>
> I’ve been walking barefoot around my place for about a year. It was difficult at first; my foot hit the ground hard, heel first; I suppose that’s how I walk in shoes. It took a couple of weeks to find a flat and balanced step; now I can walk with silent ease. I ground at sunrise and sunset anywhere from a half hour to an hour. I usually see at least one herd of elk pass through and the other day, as the sun rose out of the darkness, one of the largest elk herds I’ve ever seen appeared in the suns’ rays through the murmuring pines; there had to be about a hundred head and it took a good half hour for them to pass, bugling to their calves, which were plentiful. Time is not of the essence to elk unless they’re being hunted. It was a magnificent, leisurely elk walk; my grounding session turned into a barefoot photo shoot. If you’re lucky, you can espy an owl by the draw of its hoot. Last evening I came in with a baby preying mantis on my sleeve. How cool is that!?! Hi [@Sara](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21601/),
I am really into grounding and decided to read your entry. I've seen your picture and did not know what to think. It did look like a case of "fun" to me. What is amazing about you is your writing. I went to your first entry #1 of July 2, 2018 to see where that talent came from and I found the sentence: "I write." I will reply under that entry with other specifics that caught my eye.

### Sara — 2018-07-16T19:02:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.21645/post-245993>

Hello Joyfun in Israel! Your land is historically ancient and not as empty of souls as ours, here in New Mexico. I wonder what sense of Nature you get from beneath your feet? Pray tell!

I like fun. I use my hats as a conversation piece to avoid small talk. It works like a charm in a mad hatter sort of way. As for writing; I'm a poet, only recently refining my prose for the prospect of blogging … or worse. Heheheh

Yep, I write.

Happy to make your acquaintance.

[https://www.washingtonpost.com/life...ory.html?noredirect=on&utm\_term=.761ebbed4e35](https://www.washingtonpost.com/lifestyle/wellness/could-walking-barefoot-on-the-grass-improve-your-health-the-science-behind-grounding/2018/07/05/12de5d64-7be2-11e8-aeee-4d04c8ac6158_story.html?noredirect=on&utm_term=.761ebbed4e35)

> I just to the VCS test with NEGATIVE results for biotoxin! CFS/chronic lyme disease diagnosis here some years ago.

you can have a mold infection and still pass the VCS test. The eye test is more useless than helpful. You need to find a skilled doctor and ask for a Neuroquant MRI test. This will give the doctor a look into your brain and see if it's mold, Lyme, TBI ect…. Plus the long list of labs Dr Shoemaker
asks for on his website will help.

If your ADH levels are at 0 or near 0 - your BUN/Creatinine level will be very high. Common cause of dehydration is mold.

.

# Thread: First Vitamin D Results from UK - 64 mg/ml
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-vitamin-d-results-from-uk-64-mg-ml.21654/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Neil Taylor — 2018-07-17T18:20:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-vitamin-d-results-from-uk-64-mg-ml.21654/post-246074>

Just had my first test around the summer solstice in the UK. It has been a good summer and I've spent a lot of time outside as naked as possible.

**My results are 160 nmol/L or 64 ng/ml** which I believe [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) considers the low end of optimal.

These are completely un-suplemented. I was hoping for closer to 80 ng/ml but I'm pleased with this. My conclusion is that Jack is right - we need to spend as much time in the sun as naked as possible. It is difficult to make vitamin d and it is only going to get more difficult with 5G.

### JanSz — 2018-07-18T02:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-labs-to-begin-with.21655/post-246098>

As far as I can tell dr K does consults to members only.
He also like to see true face on avatar.
The very first advice you will usually get is to work on your Circadian Rhythm.
That involves whole body exposed to outside environment, practically regardless if it is sunny or cloudy day or if it rains.
Best if you can follow that from sunrise to sunset.
You should be naked, no sunscreens or any oils or eye glasses.
All 4 limbs on the ground and unplugged wifi and no artificial light after dark.
No white light after sunset, no tv, no cell phones, candles, very old fashion electric light bulbs are ok.
After certain time, say 3-4 months, you should be ready to take measurements of:
4x saliva cortisol
vitamin D

..........

### Erin Gaiser — 2018-07-19T14:26:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hpu-kpu.21663/post-246177>

I searched and didn't find anything on this topic HPU/KPU.

I'm not sure where it fits in best anyways, so I thought I would put it here because it pertains to my son. He is diagnosed with this and he definitely feels better taking high dose active B6 and Zinc, without it he feels bad within days (bad moods, moods swings, doesn't tan, cries a lot, cannot sleep)

The stuff Dr. Kruses posted on Oxalates was awesome and has worked so far, so I was wondering if Dr. Kruse has another perspective on this!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-19T15:25:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hpu-kpu.21663/post-246183>

The HPU complex is a biochemical marker and neurotoxic substance frequently identified in the urine of patients with autism, learning disabilities, alcoholism, substance abuse, schizophrenia, ADHD, Down syndrome, depression, bipolar disorders, and even criminal behavior. Some estimate the incidence of KPU to be 40-70% in schizophrenia; 50% in autism; 30% in ADHD; and 40-80% in alcoholism and substance abuse.

All are related to the destruction of the melanopsin/retinol mechanism. The reason why HPU/KPU people eventually stall on reversals is that they never fully treat their environmental deficits that cause melanopsin/retinol defects to persist. Their doctors never recommend this because they do not know anything about how melanopsin works to control the peripheral clock gene mechanism linked to the mtDNA defects.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-19T16:02:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hpu-kpu.21663/post-246189>

**TOP TEN SIGNS OF LIGHT TRUMPS FOOD and WHY YOUR LIGHT DECISIONS cause your disease**
1\. You have cataracts, glaucoma, macular degeneration, tinnitus, and early onset neuro-degeneration. Your CT shows a calcified pineal. Your plasma Vitamin A levels are lower than they should be. Your eyes and skin are light-sensitive to the sun. When you look in the direction of the sun your eye hurts or tears excessively. When you go in the sun, you burn excessively. Cataracts are a protective mechanism the brain uses to reduce blue light exposure. If you also procaffeinate, use too many supplements, and have no idea your "light environment" is behind this hazard and you really need to bio-hack your environment. (TIME 4 blog alert)
2\. As a child/infant, you develop atopic dermatitis, eczema, or peanut allergy, need tubes in your ears, excessive caries, or need glasses early, childhood asthma, reactive airway disease, early puberty, have a neuro-immune disease, eating disorder. You were raised on a TV computer or iPad as your main babysitter. Compared to many kids, you lived an INDOOR existence. You are afflicted by the blue light hazard and have a deficit of UV/IR light from the sun. Blue light is just one form of nnEMF, but the light we use to communicate via technology is also a huge offender. For example, immune-modulated diseases are linked to poor T and B cell function due to nnEMF exposure. Think Lyme and mastocytosis. This is why you need to hire EMF engineers or building biologists.
3\. When you changed to a carnivore/paleo diet, you developed rapid onset constipation or an autoimmune disease soon thereafter (Sara Ballantyne reaction). Your CO2 levels on a Chem 7 are below 25, and your BUN/creat ratio is above 10 to one.
4\. You have circadian or cardiac arrhythmia, hypertension, sleep apnea, pseudotumor cerebri, or normal pressure. Migraines, nasal drip, hydrocephalus, or CPM, a history of rhabdomyolysis, alcohol destroys your sleep, have an abnormal T1 or T2 MRI of an organ because your peripheral clock gene mechanism is not yoked to your SCN in proper fashion.
5\. You develop an autoimmune condition with dairy or egg exposure; food allergies dominate your life because of a lack of sun's ability to control the immune system properly. This is especially associated with xerostomia or xerophthalmia, gut sensitivity, altered microbiomes, and leaky gut—MS, Devic syndrome, RA, LADA, Hashimoto’s, fibrocystic disease, or thyroid nodularity.
6\. You have been diagnosed with adrenal fatigue by an alternative provider. AF develops with a meat diet and excessive exercise in an indoor gym. Exercise in a gym inside the power grid hurts you instead of helping you. You gain fat and lose muscle while working out and eating LESS.
7\. Chronic post nasal drip, chronic sinusitis, metallic taste in your mouth, you get fibromyalgia, photosensitivity, and sense of coldness in sunlight. Your sweating pattern is abnormal, and your skin and membranes are more apt to have aphthous ulcers, angular cheilitis, and lichen planus. You get toe and foot fungus way too easily.
8\. Food allergy testing shows you have excessive amounts of allergies, yet you eat those things and have no problem. Many people will report a seafood allergy, yet when they eat it nothing happens. There is a mismatch in your labs and your phenotype.
9\. You suffer from SIBO, IBS, Crohn’s, eosinophilic folliculitis, esophagitis, GERD, acid reflux, seafood allergy, and use chronic nootropics, you get migraines while inside windows or in a car.
10\. You have chronic parasite infections, mastocytosis, mycotoxin exposure, CE, FM, CMV, EBV, or Lyme disease that never seem to go away regardless of what is done for them. You get “mysterious diseases” after jet flights over 3 hours in length. You have bouts of depression that eventually lapse into anxiety, bipolar tendencies, ADHD, and sleep disorders. (all due to endogenous melanin destruction)

TRUTH BOMB: Sir Henry Dale, one-time President of the Royal Society of London, made an important comment in his retirement speech: “Science should not tolerate any lapse of precision, or neglect any anomaly, but give Nature’s answers to the world humbly and with courage.” To do so may not place one in the mainstream of modern science, but at least we will be searching for truth and moving ahead rather than maintaining the scientific status quo.

**PICTURE OVERVIEW:** BOTH MITOCHONDRIAL AND LYSOSOMAL STRESS STIMULATE TFEB NUCLEAR TRANSLOCATION, FOLLOWED BY INCREASED HKDC1 EXPRESSION.

This peptide is a new find in mitochondria. What does it do?

NKDC1 is essential for preserving the integrity of mitochondria and lysosomes, effectively warding off cellular senescence.

If you review the connection of this pathway, you will soon find that many of the exogenous nootropics espoused by the food gurus down-regulate NKDC1. While they get rich, you get older.

HKDC1 STABILIZES PINK1 THROUGH INTERACTION WITH TOM70, THEREBY FACILITATING PINK1/PARKIN-DEPENDENT MITOPHAGY.

Why are exogenous nootropics advocated by many not wise?

ADDITIONALLY, HKDC1 AND THE VDAC PROTEINS WITH WHICH IT INTERACTS ARE IMPORTANT FOR THE REPAIR OF DAMAGED LYSOSOMES AND FOR MAINTAINING MITOCHONDRIA–LYSOSOME CONTACT. HKDC1 PREVENTS DNA DAMAGE–INDUCED CELLULAR SENESCENCE BY MAINTAINING MITOCHONDRIAL AND LYSOSOMAL HOMEOSTASIS.

# Thread: Coffee Junky

### Michalis — 2018-07-19T15:35:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-junky.21664/post-246186>

I am prefacing this post with Jack's facebook post about coffee:

[https://de-de.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/today's-quantum-lesson:-did-you/1936510193079992/](https://de-de.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/today%27s-quantum-lesson:-did-you/1936510193079992/)

I am trying to get the Leptin reset right to sync myself so i started lowering my coffee consumption with the view to stop it asap... All previous attempts ended up in a painful fiasco. But i need to persist!

So i don't have any coffee at home any more , not even a sample. When i get crazy about it i might have it from a coffee shop... this has been going on for a few days now... so my consumption is pretty low compared to what i was doing... so 1 cup per day and it has already causing issues as it doesn't saturate the adenosine receptors fully.

Last couple of days i get extreme pain at my back and it seems that it likes to move all around the back , weird... I had to confirm that it was the coffee and not something else. I had crazy aches night last ... Again this morning i didn't drink any and the pain was just growing... i had to re-confirm that it is coffee and not something else so i went to the coffee shop and had one.

1 hour later, i am now typing this and the pain has vanished!

Coffee Junky.... Guys have you got any tips? I can go pedal to the metal because i know it is only coffee and not something else but it is so painful. Who managed to kick the habit? How long did it take?

### drezy — 2018-07-19T17:27:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-junky.21664/post-246199>

It sounds really bad. Sounds like you are in a hellhole

My last corporate gig was in the worst emf hole I've ever been in. The remarkable parts make sense to me here now after learning a bit more:

-   I started drinking 6-8x the coffee I usually did
-   My boss constantly asked the exact same questions up to 5 times a day on tasks that had weeks long duration (aka a 4 week task doesn't change status 5 times in 8 hours)
-   My feet sweat like crazy and I had to bring two pair of sneakers and a sealing bag
-   I developed massive heel cracks like a senior citizen
-   Everyone around me was using sick days at a rate I'd never seen in 15 years in industry. The younger guys seemed to be hit worse.
-   The ladies that worked there were more aggressive than I'd ever experienced. I try to keep in non-piggish shape and by no stretch have I ever been god's gift to women, but in that office I could have fooled myself.

The benefit is that I now know what it looks like when I'm in an environment that sucks the life out of me. I almost never (talking decades) get sick but with a diabetic mother with atherosclerosis I get to look at my heel cracks through a very different lens. If I choose a zoo human life I have myself modeled as "that guy who was never sick but his heart freakishly just blew up one day".

### Mike David — 2018-07-19T19:30:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-junky.21664/post-246206>

I drank coffee like a junky myself. Three 20oz. From Wawa every day. The only thing that got me to stop was the fear of the consequences of not stopping. I knew my brain was going to be pissed and I had to mentally prepare myself to deal with the consequences head on. The urge does not stop just slowly becomes less frequent. It’s a backyard street fight with yourself you cannot afford to lose. Just drinking one cup can erase weeks of hard fighting and resistance. Embrace the fear it is your friend. Embrace the suck it will make you stronger. Stay committed for the long haul when you get the urge try to occupy your mind with something else to distract you. None of this will be easy but celebrate the fact that your slapping around your inner monkey and overcoming temptation. I just drink water nothing else. Reprogramming your brain is like climbing Mount Everest you miss one foot hold you fall to the bottom. I used to work with an X cocaine addict one day he said to me you know with the secret to quitting is ( here I thought I would get some profound insight into quitting )he said the secret is to not do it. No magic pill or anything like that just the fear of the consequences of failing.

### Mike David — 2018-07-19T19:52:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-junky.21664/post-246208>

In addition to the coffee I smoked a pack of cigarettes a day and smoked weed almost every day. Jack helped me realize how just a small stimulus has a very powerful negative effect on my biology. It truly scared the shit out of me. Smoke free for two months now and coffee free for one month. I have to slap that monkey every day and celebrate each and every victory. Keeping myself active mentally and physically really helps.

> My last corporate gig was in the worst emf hole I've ever been in. The remarkable parts make sense to me here now after learning a bit more:
>
>
> -   I started drinking 6-8x the coffee I usually did
> -   My boss constantly asked the exact same questions up to 5 times a day on tasks that had weeks long duration (aka a 4 week task doesn't change status 5 times in 8 hours)
> -   My feet sweat like crazy and I had to bring two pair of sneakers and a sealing bag
> -   I developed massive heel cracks like a senior citizen
> -   Everyone around me was using sick days at a rate I'd never seen in 15 years in industry. The younger guys seemed to be hit worse.
> -   The ladies that worked there were more aggressive than I'd ever experienced. I try to keep in non-piggish shape and by no stretch have I ever been god's gift to women, but in that office I could have fooled myself.
>
>
> The benefit is that I now know what it looks like when I'm in an environment that sucks the life out of me. I almost never (talking decades) get sick but with a diabetic mother with atherosclerosis I get to look at my heel cracks through a very different lens. If I choose a zoo human life I have myself modeled as "that guy who was never sick but his heart freakishly just blew up one day".

I understand where you come from. Coffee while bathing in nnEFM is a short fix...then de-hydration comes ^2 and short circuits mitochondria.. I have just came back from the park reading the Mitochondrial RX OSF6

My last gig was terrible. Food cravings , especially carbs , coffee cravings , and a bunch of idiots playing politics , pushing projects to each other with massive communication breakdowns.

I am going to tackle the coffee issue - I was more resilient when i attempted in the past... it is more brutal now as i got less muscle mass and lower redox. i am sure about this.

### Michalis — 2018-07-19T22:02:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-junky.21664/post-246229>

[@Jenelle](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18778/) I am currently and officially unemployed so in the morning i open the window and sit there semi naked looking at the sun for the last 3 weeks...I dont think the neighborhood appreciates but who cares..

Before that when i was at work, i was having lunch outside and long breaks too. This has been a great summer in the UK so far.

If i don't have things to do in the morning stay there like 1/2 - 1 hours and have Jack's podcasts playing in the background. In the afternoon i go to the park reading and grounding , taking my shirt off.. I have been in London for far too long and it is the first time i got a TAN in the UK. Like a real tan. It is MAD! .. So people think i went on a holiday...

I cant say that i have been consistent as this weather here in the UK doesn't allow this. But i try my best. As Jack says things are not linear and there are a lot of variables that we need to take into account for each n=1. We all got ups and downs but I think persistence is key.. The worst problem for me is blue light and nnEMF. They might actually negate the time i sit under the sun...

### Jenelle — 2018-07-19T22:22:45+0300

[@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) ~ I gotcha. I still like to hear the experiences of others. I agree that a gradual reduction in coffee makes a lot more sense sometimes than cold turkey ~ which can be brutal for people, and can create problems in other areas of life.

On the other hand, some people just can't do moderation very well.

[@Michalis](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18092/) ~ I'm sorry you're unemployed, but it sounds like you are making some great changes during this time. I'd encourage you to keep it up as much as you can... you are absolutely right that things are non-linear, and each of us have our own n=1. Don't beat yourself up too much on your journey, okay?

### drezy — 2018-07-19T22:52:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-junky.21664/post-246234>

There are tons of articles like this one about my former industry:
[https://www.forbes.com/sites/robert...er-in-silicon-valley-and-beyond/#5137444a188a](https://www.forbes.com/sites/robertglatter/2015/11/27/lsd-microdosing-the-new-job-enhancer-in-silicon-valley-and-beyond/#5137444a188a)

I have no idea why they do it.

Boy scout moment: I've never taken a single illegal drug in my life. Too many of my cousins that went nowhere did all that so I stayed as laser beam focused as I could.

I don't write about this much, but since it's a small thread for the last year of strictly getting AM & PM sun and chilling myself I've experienced either lucid dreams or night time OOBE/astral projections during my sleep since fully "signing up" here. I keep a notebook near my bed these days since ideas flow in the AM. No substances, other than ice, needed. I actually don't believe in whats happening. It seems like total bullshit relative to my education and training. In the past I would have pointed a finger and laughed at [@Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/). I jumped into maximizing my redox and experienced very odd side effects. Who's the first person I reached out to? [@Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/) .

In a round-about way I'm saying that I'm convinced that every modern zoo human has an immense amount of abilities that lay untapped. No exogenous chemicals are needed. Back off the chemicals and sun drench yourself. If need be we'll slap some hooks in you

### Michalis — 2018-07-20T11:43:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-junky.21664/post-246262>

[@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) I know what you mean about the boy scout. I had the same starting point but in the way i acquired a lot "flight time". I will be honest , one of the first times i jumped on the rabbit hole it was after an intense night of mind bending music. In the morning the sun came up, i was up in the mountain, everyone was sleeping or gone and i was out in the burning midday sun... I was looking at the sun and felt a great thing which could not put it in words. And i told my friend there, "This is where everything starts" and pointed at the sun... He told me that i had lost my mind already.. Which obviously 20 years after and discovering this site says the opposite. I might have taken a detour.. But i think that I haven't fried everything - there might still be hope.

Currently the redox seems what is seems to be important to me. It doesn't mean that who has it need to go hog wild doing BS but to know that you got the ability to get away with it is an asset as it unlocks many doors... That is the critical take home message.. I watched the Hadza video and these people seem to have an amazing Redox, so they can jump rabbit holes easy, or eat crappy food if they want to..

However i cant disregard the power of psychedelics. What is happening is another story - We live in a market society so people have to be able to sell themselves better,be more competitive into the workplace so they naturally want to hack it in some way or another, just to get ahead of the game..Given the dominant paradigm people (or the oligarchs that rule us with our consent) create markets and markets shape people. The counter culture of psychedelics was assimilated into the dominant culture as it is useful, the products need to reach everyone. Margaret Thatcher used to said " There is no society - Just individuals" I think i am pushing the envelope here too far..But that is just my opinion.

It seems that your redox is through the roof by the way having all these dreams and OOBE. Lucid dreaming might be fun  I could never do it.

I am not bothered about the job because i know i will get one.. The only thing that troubles me is the logistics of "changing my environment".
