Got my Cornet in the mail yesterday, as well as mine and my daughters Ra Optics glasses. Spent the rest of the day and much of today testing pretty much everything I can and have found some nasty offenders. Seeing what my phone did made me want to puke (and immediately I ordered home phone service). I've got work to do, and know some things I need to avoid.

The kid and I went to the library this morning rocking our red glasses. She likes understanding why we do what we do, so I had explained to her simply that people need to wear the red glasses around fake light to protect our eyes and our health. That was good enough for her, but she realized that meant EVERYONE needed to wear them. So in the library she kept walking up to adults minding their own business and saying "Hey you see my glasses? Everyone needs red glasses! They protect you from the fake light!" Wow did I get stares. Nearly 50-60% of the people in my town are either medical professionals of some sort, or married to one. ### countingstarsx — 2018-09-02T21:04:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diving-in.21750/post-249989>

I did an experiment yesterday. I've had my phone on airplane mode 90% of the time since I got my Cornet the other day. We went to my sister in-laws house to grill yesterday and I just left it at home.

Well, my husband and I had a good time grilling, and watching everyone else be on a device most of the time. Their 3 year old had no interest in playing with ours -- he just wanted to sit in front of his screen too. The most shocking part of the whole day though was the effect being in that house had on me. I've never considered or noticed myself to be particularly EMF sensitive. I spent 3 days avoiding EMF's at my home, and then went into this home with 10+ devices, wifi, lights, and more going on within. I had an instant headache, and after 2 hours of being there I felt a pressure in my head and I was filled with a great irritation that was bordering on becoming rage. For no reason! Its shocking to me that I would become sensitive this quick, or notice it now after all these years but I can not come up with any other explanation. It felt like hell, and I couldn't tell anyone because no one would have gotten it.

### countingstarsx — 2018-12-15T18:55:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diving-in.21750/post-258166>

I fell off the wagon, and then MN winter hit.

Im struggling to gain some health back at this point.

Exhaustion, body pain, and incredible constipation are working against me at this moment, but I am determined. I am also pre-diabetic again.

Ive been spending time barefoot in the snow in my backyard, with as much skin showing as I can muster. I cam reliably tolerate 5 minutes in 0-10 degree cloudy weather in a tee shirt and capris. The sun is out today and we should reach 40 degrees, so much more time will be spent outside.

My heat has been kept at 65 degrees, and I have my window right next to my bed open all day for the last month.

Two major things I have to change immediately are sleep/wake time and diet.

### Inger — 2018-12-16T11:33:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diving-in.21750/post-258203>

Ouch! Now is the time to explore the cold, dark and boring! Do not miss it
Try to keep window open in the night too! Sleeping in cold and dark is really awesome. Just use extra blankets or a thick duvet if you are too cold. I cant sleep if I am freezing either. But you will get more used to the cold and do not need so much cover as time goes by. Your head and face will get the cold, and the fresh cold air is so nice to breath

I eat breakfast sitting in my open bedroom window, watching the sky/sunrise. Wrapped in my duvet . That is so nice! Maybe you can do that too? That way you get the morning light in a very comfortable and nice way

And using only a candle or two after 6 PM will fix your sleep/wake time like a charm
I try to stay in bed until it gets light. So I do not use any light or even candles in the morning, aiming for at leat 10 hours darkness. It is not hard . Except if I have to wake up earlier because of work (which I try to avoid so it happens very seldom) then I use a candle and red light in the morning.

You can do it  You will start to love it!

> Ouch! Now is the time to explore the cold, dark and boring! Do not miss it
> Try to keep window open in the night too! Sleeping in cold and dark is really awesome. Just use extra blankets or a thick duvet if you are too cold. I cant sleep if I am freezing either. But you will get more used to the cold and do not need so much cover as time goes by. Your head and face will get the cold, and the fresh cold air is so nice to breath
>
> I eat breakfast sitting in my open bedroom window, watching the sky/sunrise. Wrapped in my duvet . That is so nice! Maybe you can do that too? That way you get the morning light in a very comfortable and nice way
>
> And using only a candle or two after 6 PM will fix your sleep/wake time like a charm
> I try to stay in bed until it gets light. So I do not use any light or even candles in the morning, aiming for at leat 10 hours darkness. It is not hard . Except if I have to wake up earlier because of work (which I try to avoid so it happens very seldom) then I use a candle and red light in the morning.
>
> You can do it  You will start to love it!

Thanks for your valuable input Inger! You have described what I am aspiring for.

We havent closed our bedroom window in over a month, and it hasnt bothered us at all. In fact, I have found myself too warm on several nights with a medium weight blanket when the outside temps have dropped to under 10° F. We live in one of the windiest places in the USA, and I am curious to see how we will feel about that open window once the temps drop down to -20°F with a wind chill of -50°F or lower.

The sun rises on the opposite side of my home than my bedroom, so usually I will go outside on my porch for the view. Ive been waking up within an hour of sunrise for a few years now, closer to it in winter than summer. Thats something I need to improve on.

I would LOVE to live in a cabin with no electricity relying on candle light for light at night. Its been a dream for so long. Right now I live in a small townhome with my husband and child. He loves TV and video games, and will have them on any chance he gets right in the middle of our family room.

It gets dark here around 4:30PM right now, we typically eat supper around 6PM and the little goes to bed around 7. I know optimally we should be eating before sunset, but that would entirely eliminate family dinners at this point, and leave me with a "starving" four year old for 3 hours. My husband isnt on board with candle light/red lights so from 4-7PM our house is pretty well lit. I wear blue blockers, and get the family time. After 7PM is the only time I have alone with my husband most days. He invariably will have the TV on at this time, and I try to turn off most of the lights except an incandescent or two in the living room. I could go to sleep at 8 or 9 most nights, but stay up until 10 just to have that time with him. I'm having difficulty figuring out how to make this more optimal. I know its prettt straight forward what would make it optimal, but it seems I would be sacrificing quality family time. We hope in the next year to be able to buy a home with a basement or something where he can go hide with all of his tech and I can have more control over my environment when I want it.

### countingstarsx — 2019-03-26T19:08:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diving-in.21750/post-266505>

Spring is here!

I made it through winter, mostly unscathed. Things were starting to look pretty bad the last time I posted, but I turned it around. I am so grateful that I haven't fallen back into the pit I was in a few years ago -- such low energy, incredible anxiety, spending most of my days laying on the couch. I've been a functioning human again for almost a year, and its still a blessing I count every day.

I've got solid eating habits, solid CT habits and at very least wear blue blockers whenever exposed to artificial light. I am pretty solid at going to sleep at a decent hour (except when my daughter is having a rough night -- more on that later). I have been continuing to make my way through the blogs, currently in the Brain Gut series.

I started reading Going Somewhere a few days ago, and it is such an excellent book I can't stop talking about it to my husband. Speaking of him, he still continues to amaze me every day with how much he is absorbing from me, and changing. We were talking last night about our goals and where we plan to move, etc and we are on the same page for the most part. I'm glad he is becoming more cooperative.

I spent the whole winter working full time at two office jobs and with my daughter in a daycare. I realized in February that it just wasn't working for multiple reasons, so I quit one of my jobs and took my daughter out of daycare. We have started working on a kindergarten curriculum that is nature based and spending lots of time outside everyday. I'm SO SO pleased with this change of direction. This summer is gonna be great!

Back to my daughters rough nights, we finally got a conventional answer on why she was having them. After a very very awful sleep study they said its parasomnias and restless legs. Her sleep already improved when we turned off our wi-fi router, but now I feel like we have more to work with. I increased her magnesium intake and that alone has improved her sleep to having no incidents in the last week!

I haven't posted much, but I am still here most days reading. Thank all of you so much for the continued inspiration.

\----

Between being able to figure out things that help her intuitively, and this AWESOME business idea my husband and I are playing with I can tell my brain is getting much much clearer with everything I am doing.

Unfortunately the last few days I have been dealing with some pretty bad panic attacks. I will be doing some digging to get to the bottom of that -- wow are they uncomfortable.

### countingstarsx — 2019-04-09T22:47:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diving-in.21750/post-267731>

My daughter continues to get better each day with sun, grounding and the iron/magnesium. Everyone is getting solid sleep every night for the first time in 4.5 years, and that is like a miracle.

She saw her GI doc today because she has had increased stomach pain lately and can't eat much without her tummy hurting. He basically told us to ignore it because its for attention (what BS), and then ordered a bunch of labs. All of it is pretty uninteresting, except I was looking at her BUN/Creatinine ratio, and its NUTS. I am wondering if I am doing something wrong, or if optimal is different with kids? The reference range is lower than in adults, and her ratio is 65! Does anyone know if it isn't supposed to be 10 or less in children? Getting it there her creatinine would be well out of the reference range.

The sun has been GLORIOUS lately, but after a week of 60-70's tomorrow will drop to the 40's with 6+ inches of wet snow. This is what we get for living in MN.

The absolute redlight one can receive stays the same. But why is cloudy better, in the case of the free diver with that leg injury doing free diving on cloudy days? # Thread: Glyphosate in area
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glyphosate-in-area.21754/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bionaut — 2018-08-02T03:11:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glyphosate-in-area.21754/post-247255>

I live near some orange groves and I can get an epic sunrise running through the roads that connect them. It's a low emf area. I came to the realization after seeing vats of glyphosate at the hardware store that the field is probably being sprayed.

Does anyone know if glyphosate evaporates into the air? Or is it absorbed via skin contact. I try to minimize contact with the plants and stick to the road.
My thinking is I could make sure I am getting proper amounts of glycine from broth to offset any I am absorbing. Thoughts on this?
If anyone knows any hacks to mitigate this compound it would be helpful.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-08T18:00:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glyphosate-in-area.21754/post-247911>

My information indicates that glyphosate is a water soluble toxin/poison, which makes it incredibly more dangerous to the environment (as opposed to the usual man-made fat-soluble toxins commonly in our environment-- which tend to get sequestered in body's fat cells) .

Since glyphosate is water soluble it has a free pass to go anywhere in biosphere-- evaporated to clouds and falling as precipitation everywhere. It gets in our waterways, our soils, our air, our foods. You don't have to live next to intense farming areas to get the 'benefits' of glyphosate contamination.

Yes, i've heard that glyphosate disrupts pathways that utilize glycine amino acid. So supplementation may help. As you know already, glyphosate also attacks our gut linings integrity, and among other things prevents our bodies from fully utilizing plant alkaloids that are so helpful to us for detoxing and as adaptogens.

### Katie Durham — 2019-02-07T23:25:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glyphosate-in-area.21754/post-262531>

> [bionaut said:](/goto/post?id=247255)
>
> Does anyone know if glyphosate evaporates into the air? Or is it absorbed via skin contact.

It is absorbed through the skin. Do not walk barefoot, or walk your dog, in any area where glyphosate has been applied. (Do not expose your cat to a GMO corn litter.)

# Thread: A lesson in marketing..........
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-lesson-in-marketing.21756/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-02T03:34:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-lesson-in-marketing.21756/post-247258>

A lesson in marketing: This is why I am not a JOOV fan or supporter. Two of my members gave me their panels to hack because of some uneven data points. They asked me to hack them and I took the panels apart and tore into the data I found and what their marketing said. They offered to send me panels to hack but I told them there was no need too. I already had off the rack product from my members they sold. They have asked me many times to hawk their products and when I hacked them I realized they were lying and found out how they altered Hamblin charts when I looked my the data carefully. Marketing 101. The first thing that suffers in marketing is the truth. I do not want to do business with bullshit artists. You can choose if you want to use their panels on your own.

[https://saunaspace.com/saunaspace-vs-joovv/](https://saunaspace.com/saunaspace-vs-joovv/)

Given the price of the god damned unit the flicker and power filtering seriously sucked donkey d\*ck!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-02T17:45:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-lesson-in-marketing.21756/post-247297>

I had a feeling there was going to be some BS because one of the owners who I briefly spoke to told me he was from Minn and worked for Medtronic. That is a company I used to do a lot of business with, so I made some phone calls and to get some back round data on the owner and his character after my hacks were complete. I would note I never said anything publically about it because I believe in capitalism and think people need to do their own testing and draw their own conclusions. I protect my members and they know I have not been a JOOV supporter. I never felt the need to delve into why, but when you publish false claims and disparage someone else who is trying like hell to help people I feel you do have a duty to say something so others can hack the product and come to a more educated opinion of whether they want to use a fake red light or the sun. For me and my members the answer is obvious.

If you want to learn here is a good video from Dr Wunsch to start with. You will not get everything at once but minimum you will understand that light cannot be chosen according to whim but needs careful planning .This is why noone jumps or why noone should jump to help you with a shopping list.

### JanSz — 2018-08-06T03:26:18+0300

On this board we view electric light in negative way.
The video you are quoting reminds me that as steam and electricity promoted industrialization, massive population shifts were happening.
People begun to work and live in crowded and dark buildings. Other were working in mines.
Lupus, tuberculosis and rickets were rampant. All due to lack of light.
1945, my neighbor with tuberculosis was dying, kept in the dark room.
While other neighbor, she was a nurse in nearby sanatorium that treated people with tuberculosis, basically just by forcing them to stay outside during the day.
Few years latter, at school, I was checked for tuberculosis.
Turned out that I was exposed to it but was able to develop antibodies that saved me.

...

# Thread: My patients handout
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-patients-handout.21757/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inna — 2018-08-02T03:43:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-patients-handout.21757/post-247259>

For some reason I was unable to upload the file, so just doing copy/ paste:

This is a handout I use for my patients:

Strategies for Optimal Health

Mitochondria have a crucial role in the cell in processing information and generating energy for the body to function properly. Mitochondria determine how close or far you are from optimal health. With aging and chronic diseases mitochondrial dna mutations accumulate (the percentage of mutant mitochondrial DNA in a given cell is called heteroplasmy rate).

Higher heteroplasmy rates adversely affects cell signaling - which is a basis for chronic diseases like cancer, diabetes, mental illness, dementia and many other so called Neolithic (or chronic) diseases.

If you want to be healthy stay connected to nature as much as possible

Strategies to improve health, to influence your mitochondria - power stations of your cells:

1\. When indoors block artificial (blue frequency spectrum) light. Make sure you wear blue blocker glasses - block artificial blue light, to restore your internal clock, which governs orderly functioning and signaling of your cells.

2\. [Expose your naked skin and eyes to sunlight](https://www.naturebuildshealth.com/blog/vitamin_D_ultraviolet) during the day, especially early at the morning - when the sun is rising. Do not wear sunglasses, wear least amount of clothes you can get away with. In the evening – try to be out for sunset as well.

3\. There are only so many sunrises and sunsets in a one’s lifetime, make a goal to be outside and see as many of them as possible!

4\. Expose yourself to cold (might have to do it gradually to adapt).

5\. Eat a seasonal diet. That diet means eating fewer carbs during wintertime, and eating local produce that grows in a particular area during a particular season; fasting is healthy, eat breakfast with a lot of protein in the morning. Eat plenty of sea food (DHA – omega 3 fatty acids) are essential part of cell mebranes. Last meal of the day should be at least 3-4 hours before sunset to preserve circadian rhythms

6\. Exercise outdoors. Walking outside is generally the best exercise

7\. Ground yourself, walking barefoot or swimming in a lake or ocean. Especially beneficial to combine grounding with sun exposure in the morning and in the evening (see 2 and 3)

8\. Sleep remains the foundation for your health. Sleep quality is very important. Your body restores itself during sleep; bad or poorly functioning mitochondria are repaired or replaced during sleep. (Process of autophagy and apoptosis)

Want to learn more about mitochondrial health: See Dr. Jack Kruse – videos and interviews on You Tube

Also [www.jackkruse.com](http://www.jackkruse.com/) - blog (beginners protocols) and forum.

### Jenelle — 2018-08-02T16:16:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-patients-handout.21757/post-247287>

I wore contact lenses for most of the day, from ages 11-39. These proudly boast on the box that they block UV light.

...Do you know how many people wear contact lenses?

Definitely worth a mention, the next time you revise your handout. Glasses aren't good either ~ I know ~ but you can look over the top of your glasses. You can take them off ... off and on ... off and on ... all day long. Not so with contacts. People won't do it.

And while I was doing some googling about my particular brand (which says it offers the _highest level of UV protection available in a contact lens_) ... I came across these new **Transitions lenses** which will apparently be available in 2019:

[https://www.slashgear.com/acuvue-oa...darken-automatically-to-block-light-12527161/](https://www.slashgear.com/acuvue-oasys-transitions-contact-lenses-darken-automatically-to-block-light-12527161/)

[https://www.acuvue.com/acuvue-oasys-transition-contact-lenses](https://www.acuvue.com/acuvue-oasys-transition-contact-lenses)

And they say they also filter blue light. Oh boy. I've asked Jack if there was EVER a good time to wear contacts ... and do they filter blue light at all ... and the answer was a resounding NO. Now here is a new product to confuse people who want to have their cake and eat it, too.

### Charyam — 2018-08-23T18:43:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-patients-handout.21757/post-249202>

I see the term 'neolithic disease' used a lot.
When I look for a description of this, I can't find anything which comes near to helping me.
When telling people about the changes I'm making in my life I struggle on neolithic disease.
Can anyone point me in the right direction to help me in my understanding ? A link to a web page would be best as it would serve a good descriptive reference point.
Thankyooooooo

### Inna — 2018-08-23T21:21:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-patients-handout.21757/post-249214>

Good question – actually Neolithic – definition is relating to stone age,

What is referred here is more “diseases of civilization” – contemporary chronic illnesses, like cardiac disease, neurodegenerative diseases, cancer, diabetes, metabolic syndrome, arthritis and other inflammatory conditions, autoimmune disease to name a few, those illnesses that used to be rare in the past are now very common and growing in number and afflicting younger population than in the past

Perhaps “Stone Age” could be referred to a current state of medical science and medical practice, sadly it is utterly inadequate to address those diseases of civilization…

# Thread: How do you know if your supply of melanopsin is toast?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-if-your-supply-of-melanopsin-is-toast.21762/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2018-08-02T21:03:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-if-your-supply-of-melanopsin-is-toast.21762/post-247320>

**So, we know that melanopsin is what makes pupils contract in bright light and I just read a study that said having crappy eyesight does not affect the functionality of melanopsin and then this gem below says that apoptosis is induced - so, is it fair to say that as long as you can go out in bright light without it bothering you, your melanopsin should be functional?**

**Blue light effect on retinal pigment epithelial cells by display devices.**
[Moon J](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Moon%20J%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617)1, [Yun J](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Yun%20J%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617), [Yoon YD](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Yoon%20YD%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617), [Park SI](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Park%20SI%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617), [Seo YJ](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Seo%20YJ%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617), [Park WS](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Park%20WS%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617), [Chu HY](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Chu%20HY%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617), [Park KH](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Park%20KH%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617), [Lee MY](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Lee%20MY%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617), [Lee CW](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Lee%20CW%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617), [Oh SJ](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Oh%20SJ%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617), [Kwak YS](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Kwak%20YS%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617), [Jang YP](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Jang%20YP%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617), [Kang JS](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Kang%20JS%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=28386617).
**[Author information](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28386617#)**
**Abstract**
Blue light has high photochemical energy and induces cell apoptosis in retinal pigment epithelial cells. Due to its phototoxicity, retinal hazard by blue light stimulation has been well demonstrated using high intensity light sources. However, it has not been studied whether blue light in the displays, emitting low intensity light, such as those used in today's smartphones, monitors, and TVs, also causes apoptosis in retinal pigment epithelial cells. We attempted to examine the blue light effect on human adult retinal epithelial cells using display devices with different blue light wavelength ranges, the peaks of which specifically appear at 449 nm, 458 nm, and 470 nm. When blue light was illuminated on A2E-loaded ARPE-19 cells using these displays, the display with a blue light peak at a shorter wavelength resulted in an increased production of reactive oxygen species (ROS). Moreover, the reduction of cell viability and induction of caspase-3/7 activity were more evident in A2E-loaded ARPE-19 cells after illumination by the display with a blue light peak at a shorter wavelength, especially at 449 nm. Additionally, white light was tested to examine the effect of blue light in a mixed color illumination with red and green lights. Consistent with the results obtained using only blue light, white light illuminated by display devices with a blue light peak at a shorter wavelength also triggered increased cell death and apoptosis compared to that illuminated by display devices with a blue light peak at longer wavelength. These results show that even at the low intensity utilized in the display devices, blue light can induce ROS production and apoptosis in retinal cells. Our results also suggest that the blue light hazard of display devices might be highly reduced if the display devices contain less short wavelength blue light.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-03T02:07:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-if-your-supply-of-melanopsin-is-toast.21762/post-247339>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boston-has-officially-fallen-5g-warning.21763/post-247337>

Spent some time today with an emf expert from Arizona that just got back from appointments around the country. He told me that Boston is 5G all over and it is causing serious problems. He tested people and the voltage on their skin is alarmly high at levels he had never seen. He said the people he met with have kids aged 5-7 that are waking up every 30 minutes to an hour every night. He tested shielding gear and said the 5G rips straight through everything he tested with no shielding at all.

He also told me that there are a few new frequencies that he tested across the country that are relatively constant that are new. Meaning they are coming from the sky and areas without cell service are covered in it. The closer he was to the East Coast the stronger the emf and it faded as he crossed the country but not completely. There is an area of West Virginia that is known for being a dead area (an emf free refuge for people who actually go there to live because of it) and it has ithe emf now too coming from the sky.

In Arizona, where I am, there is a constant emf that started about two weeks ago. We tested it at my house and it was surely present without any other RF or electrical interference. He suspects its something military that is being tested. He said he tested this up in Northern Arizona where no emf previously existed and that it just showed up recently. Even when he was in Wyoming, in the woods with pine trees the emf was present from above.

Interesting stuff to say the least...

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-03T22:25:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boston-has-officially-fallen-5g-warning.21763/post-247416>

Well, I may be wrong about believing 5G mm wave networks coming later on. Now it is sooner, because wireless companies are starting to offer mobile devices that will work with it.

For example, AT&T has been rolling out 5G 'Evolution' networks (using legacy LTE networks) until now. But that has changed.

According to _Digital Trends_ 7/18:

"In order for users to take advantage of 5G, AT&T also has plans on offering devices built specifically for the network. **The carrier plans on adding more 5G-capable mobile devices and smartphones in early 2019 and beyond.**

"The new network will be based on the industry standard approved by the 3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) just a few weeks ago, which basically encompasses the use of low-frequency spectrum (600Hz and 700Hz), mid-frequency spectrum (3.5GHz), and high-frequency spectrum (50GHz). When used together, these different spectrums should help deliver a much faster experience."

_Updated July 20: AT&T announces three new cities that will get 5G by the end of 2018.
_
Question to ponder: Are all these major wireless companies going their separate ways in rolling out these networks?

Each one might have a proprietary infrastructure in a major urban area, thus multiplying the number of transmitters/antennas radiating a given area (imagine your neighborhood thoroughly fried by separately owned and operated 5G antennas from AT&T, Sprint, Mobile One, etc.) That thought makes the hair stand up on the back of my neck.

# Thread: Thanks To Jack....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thanks-to-jack.21764/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2018-08-03T07:39:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thanks-to-jack.21764/post-247347>

I just had a building biologist who was personally trained and mentored by Stetzer come out to where I'm staying.and here's what he found:

#1:
The entire house was bathed in a MASSIVE magnetic field of up to 10 mG that was being carried everywhere from the water line; power from the grid was coming in through under the street and latch8ng on to the water line to produce both huge currents and magnetic fields which were then coming up through the floors.

The minute he cut the line I could immediately feel a relaxation response to the point where I starting yawning continuously for the next hour, lol.

#2: Even though I was using incandescent bulbs, faulty wiring was producing a large jump in mag fields for some rooms whenever the lights were turned on.

#3

I thought hardwiring in to the modem was enough, but it's not unless it's done through a SHIELDED cable.

#4

BIG ONE:

The metal lining on my work desk was conducting a giant electric field across the entire desk from my laptop, even when the laptop was grounded.

Anyways, just wanted to say thanks to Jack for opening my eyes to these things.

I knew you were right, but I had no idea how much of a difference fixing these things made and how little the other stuff truly matters compared to this.

I believe it all now, every fucking thing you've said about the priority of the environment.

### Butters — 2018-08-04T12:21:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-live-in-germany.21767/post-247451>

With my upcoming end of my college time I have now to choose where to live.
I dont want to migrate away from germany but I have to consider the rapid development of 5G now.
I thought there is time, it turned out not to be the case.
My prime destination turned out to will be a test place for 5G I read today. Its not there at the moment but its coming.

So I flirt with the Baltic Sea coast or North sea Coast. Both would provide me with CT and grounding, as well as sunrise and sunset possibilities and seafood. And its possible for my job. Im ready to cut all useless bondings with so called friends loose now.

Are there members who live in germany and have checked locations?

### Lahelada — 2018-08-04T16:20:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-live-in-germany.21767/post-247461>

[@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) she lives in North Germany.
You will need a place with a small population and a car so that you have your environment under control .I would also learn Spanish so that you can be two centre based if need be. After unification which place stayed underdevelopped in what was the old East? Urbach ?
[https://www.targetmap.com/viewer.aspx?reportId=14770](https://www.targetmap.com/viewer.aspx?reportId=14770) a map of population density

PS : Did you ask in the German Quantum group?

### Inger — 2018-08-05T10:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-live-in-germany.21767/post-247520>

Yes I live 30 minutes north of Husum. West coast. Here are loads of oysters! For free   And less dense population. I live in a town of 400 ppl.

Downside is lots of windmills. But you can still find places that are further away from them if you look for it. Still, almost the whole area is covered in windmills. Sad.
You should take a trip and check the area. Baltic Sea coast might be a great place too, somewhere remote! I guess less windmills. But no oysters. I like North Sea because of the tides, they are magic to watch. And water is cleaner as the North Sea.

# Thread: Tomatis method - beneficial?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatis-method-beneficial.21768/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2018-08-04T16:41:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatis-method-beneficial.21768/post-247462>

I have been asked to maybe co operate in a project to bring the Tomatis method to language learning.
It is based on three things it seems, music in 432 HZ, spoken word and bone conduction via special
headphones.

"During the listening sessions, music and voice are transmitted through headphones equipped for both air and bone conduction. The sound therefore reaches the auditory canal through the headphones placed on each ear and through a vibration from the top of the skull.
**With a specific setting applied, the sound transmits first through the bones of the skull. As a result, the auditory system naturally prepares itself to receive the sound through the air channel."**

It is the bone conduction part that sparked my unease.
Reading the client evaluation crib sheet the needed observations all go to evaluating dopamine levels,even though it is not explicitly stated.
The equipment itself operates on a battery pack. I have not seen it yet though.

Any one want to share their impression or experience even ? Thanks.

### johnsonmd — 2018-08-10T01:52:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatis-method-beneficial.21768/post-248059>

I have not heard of Tomatis before but I have heard about music helping with children's learning difficulties. Classical music seems to broaden the variety of sound that one hears. It can sooth stress and help with memory and understanding concepts. I imagine the tones would effect the cells and mitochondria. I wonder about the RF and EMF. I worry about the headphones effecting the hearing, long term.

### Lahelada — 2018-08-10T02:52:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatis-method-beneficial.21768/post-248060>

Thank you ! I have the same concerns. Bone conduction, its effect on mitochondrial oscillation and measure Emf are something I will need to focus on. More literature search.

### gscottyg — 2018-08-05T02:40:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-daughter-gets-it.21770/post-247501>

My 2 and a half year old eats fish every morning with her daddy. Her favorite part and I will quote is "skin and bone". Every morning I start her off right with a little am sun (Sure she won't look long but it's better than TV!) Her best meal of the day is always breakfast (bacon/eggs/fish) are the mainstays. Sure she is a kid and will want some carbs but you tell me what 2 year old will eat sockeye salmon out of a can with the skin/bones and drink the juice after lol. After breakfast we will go outside since it is summer and play/walk/swim etc for as much of the morning as she can tolerate. Jack she seems to get up close to when sun rises. I assume since she is so young that her circadian rhythm is much more intact vs an adult living a non-optimal life? Now only if I could get my wife onboard :/

# Thread: Hello to all...new here and eager to learn
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-new-here-and-eager-to-learn.21771/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PJP71 — 2018-08-05T05:00:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-new-here-and-eager-to-learn.21771/post-247508>

Hi everyone. I have always been a health conscious person but over the last few years I've begun to realize how much misinformation we've been fed our whole lives. I've always been active and my diet is pretty healthy compared to the norm. I am 47 but most people think I am at least 10 years younger. Even though I look younger and am fit by most peoples standards, I expect to feel better than I do despite my efforts. In other words, I don't feel optimal. At about the age of 42 I began feeling more fatigued than usual. My blood pressure would sometimes be considered in the pre-hypertension stage, but I attributed it to "white coat syndrome".

After the birth of my daughter almost 2 years ago, I began a quest to become as healthy and fit as possible. Being an older father, I realize this is important if I want to be a large part of her life and keep up with her later. I began to look at things more closely. My wife said I made funny noises when I slept but not exactly snoring. Knowing I had a deviated septum and was also a mouth breather, I suspected sleep apnea and knew that could cause hypertension. which was confirmed after a sleep study. It has always been difficult for me to fall asleep so I knew CPAP probably wouldn't work. I tried it anyway and could not tolerate it. This lead me to get sinus surgery to correct my deviated septum which has definitely helped my breathing. After a 2nd sleep study, I've been diagnosed with positional sleep apnea. So as long as I am on my side I don't have it. I also asked my doctor to do some labs a few years ago suspecting low testosterone. Quite a few things were checked but everything was "within normal range" except for DHEA S. After googling "low DHEA S" I found Dr. Kruse's blog on it, which then led me here. I've always had a voracious appetite for knowledge, especially when it come to heath and the human body. I love to learn the science behind things and know I can learn a great deal here.

I am trying to get a feel for the basics to start incorporating them in my life. From my first few sessions on the forum, it seems this list is a good way to start the basics:
1\. Sunlight - _**need more**_
2\. Un-fluoridated water - _**need to find this**_
3\. Magnetism/grounding to Earth/CT protocol - **_beyond walking barefoot, will learn more_**
4\. seafood - _**not a seafood lover but willing to try more**_
5\. Self Confidence - _**check**_
6\. Friends who connect with you in life and not just on line. - _**definitely need more, difficult with baby but need to find a way**_

If there are other basics or things I should incorporate, someone please let me know.

Since it has been a few years, I would like to get some more testing done to get a baseline. I just want to make sure I get the right tests and at the right labs.

It might be of note that I did a ketogenic diet for 6 months and had good results getting leaner and felt a slight energy boost. I came off of it as it became very limiting. Not opposed to trying it again though.
I have been doing intermittent fasting for almost a year with good results.
I have been using potassium bicarbonate to help with blood pressure with good results.

I just realized this post might be a great start to my journal here!

If anyone has some suggestions or other info, please feel free. I would greatly appreciate it. Also, where do I look to find out more about being a member? I am not rich, but I consider my health to be my most important asset and value the knowledge it takes to get it optimal.

### Jenelle — 2018-08-05T14:13:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-new-here-and-eager-to-learn.21771/post-247527>

Welcome [@PJP71](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21756/) ~ and yes, good idea to start a journal & copy this information over to it.

I just wanted to share a couple of quick thoughts this morning:

1) Unfluoridated water is pretty easy ~ look for anything that uses reverse osmosis. We've had one of those big 5-gallon water dispensers for a long time, and when we refill those jugs at the store, it's RO water. Aldi even has cheap bottled water that is RO if you have to have something like that for on-the-go.

2) [https://jackkruse.com/become-a-member/](https://jackkruse.com/become-a-member/)

3) [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...h-a-silver-gold-or-platinum-membership.19027/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/when-you-begin-with-a-silver-gold-or-platinum-membership.19027/)

"I am not rich" ... I can assure you that I fall into this category, as well. The question becomes "how hungry are you for knowledge and connecting the dots?"

There is an unbelievable amount of information in the webinars, Q&A's with other members, and of course all of the blogs ... I'd recommend Gold if you can swing it. You'll see that recommended here again & again. I'm not on facebook but there is a private group there available to paid members, as well.

### PJP71 — 2018-08-05T16:54:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-new-here-and-eager-to-learn.21771/post-247537>

Thanks for all the tips Jenelle! I'll start looking into all of these and will be starting my journal today. Need to watch some of the videos (didn't even realize there were some available) Recommendations are welcomed.

I've been known to read a great deal when I want to learn about something and "connect the dots", but I do realize the learning curve will be much quicker when becoming a member. Just need to decide which to go with.

### Jenelle — 2018-08-05T17:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-new-here-and-eager-to-learn.21771/post-247544>

I posted links to several of Dr. Kruse's (free) podcasts throughout the first pages of my journal ~ if you are interested in scrolling past the {blah blah blah} and looking for them.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/emerging-from-darkness.21283/)

His podcasts get me fired up (in a good way) ~ I think they are especially helpful in the beginning when information is new to people ... AND they are good to go back and listen to again sometime, when you've traveled a little further down the road. New connections are made. You have lightbulb moments of: "oh yeah ~ I remember hearing that before!" Etc. etc.

There is a thread floating around here with a list of his podcasts, as well. I'm not sure how up-to-date it is. I discovered it after I'd already been digging things up on my own and depositing them in my journal.

Vermont 2016 & 2017 talks are available on YouTube for free (last I knew) and 2018 talk is available at a discount through the site if you are a paid member (last I knew). Loved them. All of them.

### JanSz — 2018-08-05T18:18:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-new-here-and-eager-to-learn.21771/post-247546>

47yo
new baby 2 years ago
low DHEA
low testosterone?? (likely, post it here)
Sleep Apnea
deviated septum, surgically corrected
At about the age of 42 I began feeling more fatigued and higher blood pressure

\------------------------------------------------
Become Gold member.
Maximize outdoor time, max skin area exposed, sun or cloudy, rain or snow.
When you see detailed discussions of the best time outdoors, implementing them should newer cut the overall time spend outdoor.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) uses frequent and sophisticated testing and supplementation.
That is described in earlier parts of his Quilt. Read that.
Trust in general advice but always double check your personal status testing.

The most important hormone is cortisol.
It is important in at least two ways. Size and time of the day.
Feeling fatigued usually means low cortisol.
Do not exercise when fatigued.
When exercising do it outdoor.

Second would be a vitamin D. (It lowers or rather stabilizes blood pressure).

Proper metabolism.
Rarely mentioned but super important is
homocysteine
actually it is mentioned (very often), just when you read
metionine
you think and test
homocysteine

\------------------------------------------
Being 47 yo I can see that you should be able to adjust your life style and achieve naturally good cortisol.
I cannot say that for DHEAs and testosterone. But possibly [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) would say otherwise.
Having good cortisol and low DHEAs you still will be fatigued.
I have not heard of low testosterone coming up naturally at your age.
Low testosterone will eventually clog up your arteries.
Supplemental testosterone will make you sterile, so if you are still planning more babies, be carefull or speed up your procreation program.

\------------------------------------------
If you are interested, there is more.

As men age T is designed to drop and this scales back to the relationship between space and time. Matter deforms space-time to it tells is how to curve and then light flows based upon the curve and from that interaction, time is altered. How space-time curves describe the flow of time.

So what does this all means for aging men? As you age nature wants you a bit more chubby and have less muscle mass and more fat because that slows time down as we age. This is nature

as gravity varies your experience of time varies. So in that sense the bigger you are the more gravity you experience and the longer time appears to your circadian mechanism. This is why the data on the supercentenarians in Mt Sinai shows all these guys above 100 to have BMI's between 28-35.

It is also why heavily muscled guys tend to die sooner.
Nature is funny like that........

So the antiaging and T doctors who push men to use fake T might be altering the timing mechanism (telomeres) in your cells this could deplete your stem cells so you never reach 100.

How is that for a counterintuitive reality.

### JonathanHodges — 2022-05-10T15:07:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shampoo-replacement-soap-replacement.21774/post-312863>

The thought came to me not so long ago to give up shampoo. Something popped into my head, and I decided to try it. I read that it is harmful, that just water will cope and the like. However, the water didn't work. I tried to make the transition for about a month, but my dandruff became very aggravated. I could not comb my hair. It was a real nightmare. I hope that the transition will happen and that everything will go well. This, of course, did not happen, and I had to urgently save my hair. On the advice of a friend, I bought [GOLD LUST SHAMPOO](https://utiee.com/Damaged-Hair-Shampoos/35-goldlust.html) and washed my hair with it all week. My hair quickly returned to normal. I won't do any more experiments like this. It's horrible.

### ShellyFM — 2022-05-10T17:54:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shampoo-replacement-soap-replacement.21774/post-312868>

Water only is amazing! However, I do still occasionally need a wash if I let things go too long with water only, but that's like once every month or two. There's a great no poo group on FB that has fantastic information in their files. I will say, water only didn't work before we got a whole house filtration and (potassium based) softener system.

I've also had success with baking soda and apple cider vinegar rinses and using soap nuts. It's quite the experimental process, but well worth it!

# Thread: Is there any such thing as a good computer ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-any-such-thing-as-a-good-computer.21776/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Morthern — 2018-08-05T10:11:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-any-such-thing-as-a-good-computer.21776/post-247518>

hello
I am a writer And need a new computer to continue my craft. I wear glasses with orange filters and make sure I turn it off by 8 pm.

Can I be doing better ?

Is there a computer on the market that releases less emf’s?

What else can I do to decrease the hazards or writing on a computer ?

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-05T20:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-any-such-thing-as-a-good-computer.21776/post-247564>

I use a laptop but computers like it are apparently not the best from safety standpoint because of additional EMFs (ELF radiation) from their batteries plus the fact they can't be grounded. Don't ever put a laptop on your lap or anywhere close to your body.

A better PC choice might be a traditional desktop computer form factor with a metal chassis that can be grounded.

I don't know if computers are being marketed 'green' based on comparatively low RF and ELF emissions.

As an aware consumer I'm sure you are using a wired (ethernet cable) connection instead of wireless mode (but check your computer's network settings to make sure the wireless network signal is not active--- by default network antenna on board computer might continuously send out RF signals to ping a wifi connection unless you shut it down).

Ditch wireless mouse and other peripheral devices so enabled.

You are probably already using an app like f.lux to switch color temperature of your computer display from blue to warmer, more biologically compatible hues.

### Morthern — 2018-08-06T03:19:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-any-such-thing-as-a-good-computer.21776/post-247626>

Thank you jack and electric Universe for your feedback
Actually , my laptop died a few months ago and I’ve been using my phone as a computer
( I phone 7 .. big screen )
It’s this BETTER than a laptop perhaps ?
I’m concidering not replacing my laptop and just managing here and with my iPad

Ironically .. my main useage online is for forums and stuff on Health ... doh !

# Thread: My Journal - Light at the end of a tunnel...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-light-at-the-end-of-a-tunnel.21777/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 43
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PJP71 — 2018-08-05T17:02:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-light-at-the-end-of-a-tunnel.21777/post-247538>

I have always been a health conscious person but over the last few years I've begun to realize how much misinformation we've been fed our whole lives. I've always been active and my diet is pretty healthy compared to the norm. I am 47 but most people think I am at least 10 years younger. Even though I look younger and am fit by most peoples standards, I expect to feel better than I do despite my efforts. In other words, I don't feel optimal. At about the age of 42 I began feeling more fatigued than usual. My blood pressure would sometimes be considered in the pre-hypertension stage, but I attributed it to "white coat syndrome".

After the birth of my daughter almost 2 years ago, I began a quest to become as healthy and fit as possible. Being an older father, I realize this is important if I want to be a large part of her life and keep up with her later. I began to look at things more closely. My wife said I made funny noises when I slept but not exactly snoring. Knowing I had a deviated septum and was also a mouth breather, I suspected sleep apnea and knew that could cause hypertension. which was confirmed after a sleep study. It has always been difficult for me to fall asleep so I knew CPAP probably wouldn't work. I tried it anyway and could not tolerate it. This lead me to get sinus surgery to correct my deviated septum which has definitely helped my breathing. After a 2nd sleep study, I've been diagnosed with positional sleep apnea. So as long as I am on my side I don't have it. I also asked my doctor to do some labs a few years ago suspecting low testosterone. Quite a few things were checked but everything was "within normal range" except for DHEA S. After googling "low DHEA S" I found Dr. Kruse's blog on it, which then led me here. I've always had a voracious appetite for knowledge, especially when it come to heath and the human body. I love to learn the science behind things and know I can learn a great deal here.

I am trying to get a feel for the basics to start incorporating them in my life. From my first few sessions on the forum, it seems this list is a good way to start the basics:
1\. Sunlight - _**need more**_
2\. Un-fluoridated water - _**need to find this**_
3\. Magnetism/grounding to Earth/CT protocol - **_beyond walking barefoot, will learn more_**
4\. seafood - _**not a seafood lover but willing to try more**_
5\. Self Confidence - _**check**_
6\. Friends who connect with you in life and not just on line. - _**definitely need more, difficult with baby but need to find a way**_

If there are other basics or things I should incorporate, someone please let me know.

Since it has been a few years, I would like to get some more testing done to get a baseline. I just want to make sure I get the right tests and at the right labs.

It might be of note that I did a ketogenic diet for 6 months and had good results getting leaner and felt a slight energy boost. I came off of it as it became very limiting. Not opposed to trying it again though.
I have been doing intermittent fasting for almost a year with good results.
I have been using potassium bicarbonate to help with blood pressure with good results.

If anyone has some suggestions or other info, please feel free. I would greatly appreciate it. Also, where do I look to find out more about being a member? I am not rich, but I consider my health to be my most important asset and value the knowledge it takes to get it optimal and should be able to join soon.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-05T18:32:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-light-at-the-end-of-a-tunnel.21777/post-247553>

WELCOME

[https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/2255265984537743](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/2255265984537743)

People do not realize how light works. If you saw how LED tracks are made you realize something deep about cells. The add LED tracks to an aluminum track which acts as a heat sink to dissipate heat. If they did not do that do you know what happens? The LED changes its color. Anytime an LED diode changes its color it means its frequency has also shifted. When frequency shifts the power, energy, and information in light is also shifted. What is the heat sink in cells? Water that the mitochondria make. What does blue and nnEMF in every part of the spectrum of light do to cells? Dehydrates them. What does that mean to cells? When you live in a blue-lit or nnEMF environment your cells are prone to emitting an inaccurate light diode signal. Every protein coded for by DNA acts as a light diode. Every protein on Earth has to be in an aqueous environment to operate properly. This means that the type of water in that aqueous environment is the rate-limiting step in information transfer. That is how we work optically and no one seems to realize it but my members.

Cortisol
DHEAs

### Jenelle — 2018-08-07T20:38:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-light-at-the-end-of-a-tunnel.21777/post-247854>

(copied and pasted response I made elsewhere on the forum recently)

~ if you want a detailed discussion of what's going on with your labs, I think your best bet is going to be to look into an actual consult with Dr. Kruse. I believe you can do that by clicking on the "contact us" button.

Otherwise, it will be sifting through the giant amount of information available at your fingertips ~ in his book, the forum, and all of the resources you've now gained through Gold membership. Fitting pieces together like a puzzle.

Perhaps he will comment on your labs here ~ perhaps he will not. But in an actual consult 1:1 I imagine you'd get a ton of valuable information about what some of these lab values may mean _in_ _your particular context._

Some of the best tips I can give you while navigating the forum and all of this new information:

1) Pay attention to what you see _Dr. Kruse himself_ say. Do not look at information from other members and assume it is correct and in line with his thinking. Some of it is, some of it is not ~ and you will do well to remember that. When something sparks an idea in your mind, it sounds good to you, and you want to know more about it ~ google whatever it is along with "Jack Kruse" and see what you find.

2) I think almost everyone comes here eager for answers and wanting to make changes right away. There are changes that can be made right away, and cost you nothing. They are foundational to everything else you will learn. Get the basics down before you start diving down other deep rabbit holes. For example: really, what good is trying to learn all of the details about deuterium ~ if you are still staying up way past dark, and sleeping in past sunrise?

3) Keep in mind this is a marathon, not a sprint. It's a journey. This place is different from pretty much everything else you have come across ~ meaning, no one is going to give you a list of foods you can/can't eat, a list of supplements you must take, etc. Seldom will you see someone say "you must do this" and if you do, and it isn't Dr. Kruse ~ question it. No one is going to make promises like, "such and such happens in 30 days!" Things are very individual here ~ as we are all unique individuals. There will be no quick fix. Optimal is not a place where you arrive one day and say, "okay, I'm here!" It's a lifelong commitment to being the best human you can be, and your view of optimal will be forever changing.

### Jenelle — 2018-08-07T22:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-light-at-the-end-of-a-tunnel.21777/post-247862>

Believe it or not ~ the purified water bottles from Aldi are RO and say "BPA-free". Yes, I'm sure there are other things in the water bottles that will kill us. No, we don't drink them all day every day. But I've got 3 kids in sports & I am dealing with limited (ha) finances and real life every day here ~ so... that's what we buy for now. \*At home\* and for the majority of the time ~ we have the big 5-gallon hard plastic jugs which are also BPA-free. I was delighted to know that the water we've been refilling with for the past 15 years is RO. Prior to that, we lived in the country and had well water with a RO filter system.

### countingstarsx — 2018-08-08T18:41:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-light-at-the-end-of-a-tunnel.21777/post-247916>

> [PJP71 said:](/goto/post?id=247861)
>
> I remember you mentioned getting water form a reverse osmosis source and [@Joyfun](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21372/) mentioned Berkley Filter (her reply to this thread doesn't show up anymore for some reason?) For simplicity, I was trying to find a refrigerator filter that removes fluoride. No luck as of yet. I suppose I could get bottled water but aren't BPAs a concern?

Hi there! I use a Berkey at home too -- but its not the most optimal solution. From what I have read the only 100% way to get all fluoride out is with RO or distillation. The Berkey uses activated alumina which does remove some fluoride, but in optimal conditions (a specific pH) it only gets out something like 96%. Unfortunately, most tap water isn't in that narrow range of optimal pH level so you are left with an even more reduced percentage of fluoride removal.

When we buy a home we will probably get an RO system installed, but until then I am considering getting a few glass carboys and filling them with RO water at the store.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-light-at-the-end-of-a-tunnel.21777/post-269989>

> [PJP71 said:](/goto/post?id=247840)
>
> Additional things I thought I would mention:
>
> I practice intermittent fasting 5 days a week. Usually from 6pm to 12pm or later the following day.
> I work as a Computed Tomography Technologist
> This year I started wearing prescription glasses when reading or using computer. The lenses are anti-blue light but not sure how much they block. Purchased them at Costco.

They probably block the minimum, but they will work to some degree. I had cheap $5 ones I bought on amazon back in Dec 2017. They were "ok" but I could feel the difference between wearing them in the office and not. Then I bought the primal hacker yellow tint glasses for the office and those were wonderful. I recently got the RaOptics ones and they are good too. I like the primal hacker ones better !

Can you change the lighting in your office or workspace to limit LED/Florescents? Do you work near windows or anything? Do you sit in front of a screen all day?

Regarding your fasting, are you eating a big ass breakfast if you are fasting from 6pm to 12noon the next day? That could be a reason why your DHEA is low too. The breakfast helps reset your body and hormones. If you want to fast I believe eating early at 4 or 5pm and then waiting until morning is enough. Anyone else please chime in.

# Thread: Why do we see fires in new 5 G regions have melted cars and green trees?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-we-see-fires-in-new-5-g-regions-have-melted-cars-and-green-trees.21779/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-06T04:25:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-we-see-fires-in-new-5-g-regions-have-melted-cars-and-green-trees.21779/post-247633>

For those asking how trees don't burn with temperatures reaching thousands of degrees. Look to the frequency of induction heating via directed energy.

"The electrical frequency influences the process of induction heating. The desired frequency range is determined by the dimensions of the workpiece, type of material, the arrangement between the object and the coil, and the desired depth of penetration. As the frequency is increased, the penetration of heat is decreased. The normal range of frequency is 500 Hz to 10 kHz, known as medium frequency, and 100 kHz to 2 MHz, called high range. Frequencies in the range of 100 to 450 kHz create comparatively high energy heat that is suitable for rapid heating of small objects, or the skin of large parts. When deep penetration of heat is required, extensive cycles of heating at low frequency are essential, frequency range being 5-30 kHz. The most transfer of energy during the process of induction heating is at high frequency when the depth of current penetration is low. It has been established that the recommended frequency should be such that the depth of penetration should not be more than 1/8 times the diameter of the work piece."

[https://www.brighthubengineering.com/hvac/95097-frequency-and-induction-heating/](https://www.brighthubengineering.com/hvac/95097-frequency-and-induction-heating/)

# Thread: Evening routine to make dozing off easier

**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Forest — 2018-08-06T12:12:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-247645>

there's gotta be a to make this easier

### drezy — 2018-08-06T17:06:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-247692>

Immersion in 55F water around 2 hrs before bed
Direct sunset watching
No lights or eating after sunset
Direct Sunrise sun on skin and eyes

Rinse and repeat for 45-60 days

### Danco3636 — 2018-08-11T22:51:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-248246>

All the the things I do from sunrise until mid day in optimizing my quilt makes me feel like a sleep tank hits me shortly after sunset.

Morning am sun to the max and am Movement along with seafood and connecting the dots sets your sleepy ness at night.

### Lahelada — 2018-08-11T23:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-248247>

The morning sun routine makes the evening routine easier. No blue light at night. Leptin Rx protocol!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-11T23:13:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-248248>

sleep makes optimal easier. stop posting at night.

### Inger — 2018-08-12T10:00:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-248292>

In addition to the above/morning sun/outside time, just go with the dusk. Do not put on any lights at all, not even a candle. Watch the darkening sky and the stars coming with no glass in between. Very peaceful, very easy. You do not need to do anything. Makes sleepy

I some days have to work later so it is dark as I come home. Not good I know but what I do is immediately as work stops I put on blueblockers, drive home with them on, and enter my dark home, take a shower in the dark and go to bed.
When it is still light out when I come home I do the same, I shower, go to bed and watch the darkening sky from my wide open window and I have no issues falling to sleep at all.....
Pre bed time is for me like meditation time... I love to just.... be.

In winter as it gets dark early here in the north, I just have 1 candle on, and I read. I go to bed earlier in winter and sleep longer. I love that too. It is so healing. This winter I am planning to do some days with no candle at all, just in the dark. I need to try it. I bet it is super healing!

### Sun Disciple — 2018-08-16T20:29:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-248612>

1\. Watching Sunset
2\. Blue blockers
3\. Getting into blacked out bedroom
4\. Buteyko breathing 5 mins
5\. meditation 10 mins

### freddy bee — 2018-09-19T18:06:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-251350>

Hot/cold shower 4-5 hours before bed, eating during daylight hours, foam rolling, meditation, night time tea with a touch of honey, etc..

Mindset is important, but if lifestyle mostly overlaps it doesn't have to be a problem. For example it's more practical to find someone who likes to watch sunrises for the hell of it, then someone who watches sunrises because it has a UV/IR transition. If it's someone who likes sun and grounding, that's already more than half the match.

Let Mother Nature play Cupid and give a guiding hand (and whiff) to help those looking for romance.

while this is great advice Simon, I think there is so much more to it. I spent almost everyday at the ocean but it wasn't until I started emitting positive energy/light did my life change by leaps and bounds. Up until then I was pretty much invisible.

Personally I think we need to work on ourselves first and foremost and our optimal journey. Jack always tells us that we have to love ourselves first before we can expect someone else to love us.

We have been travelling for almost 6 weeks and every day we hear the most incredible stories of survival and love and fortitude and kindness.

Right now we are staying at a veterans campground in the middle of a pine forest. The welcome from these vets is way above anything we have experienced so far. A lot of these guys are Vietnam Vets ....and goodness knows - they were treated terribly.

This caravan park was started by a Vet who wanted to create a place of peace and tranquility and a safe haven for all those who have served ...and the rest of us too.

# Thread: Cleaning 101

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-247709>

like a bag of it

Looking for it in the Durham nc area. Hopefully like a wholesaler thats already got them shipped so you don't have to
How long will it be before they go bad
Is this even a good idea

Buying them for like 1.20 each for blue point gets pretty expensive. Been buying them individually for like 6 months now and got this wild idea. Also which type is best.

### drezy — 2018-08-10T22:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248153>

You'll have to do your own big girl homework on google by searching the terms "durham nc bulk oysters". Mind you that oyster harvesting may be out of season currently and will come back in the fall.

> [Forest said:](/goto/post?id=247709)
>
> How long will it be before they go bad

I've bought 50 at a time and ate them over 10 days and had zero issues. YMMV.

### mitch.moore1 — 2018-08-10T23:02:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248156>

Oysters come with a harvest date tag which will tell you how long the oysters are good for.

### Phosphene — 2018-08-10T23:41:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248158>

I get pints (16 ounces) of fresh, pre-shucked New England oysters for $15 at the local farmer’s market, dated to last 2 weeks but I eat 2 containers per week now. There are about 24 in each plus some juice that I slurp at the end. I could order 100 count live bags for between $60-$90 but then I’d have to store and shuck them myself. This is so easy—just open container, spoon a few out, squirt with lemon and slurp them down with a big glass of water. I do wonder if there’s any DHA quality difference between shucked and unshucked, but not enough to worry about it.

### drezy — 2018-08-10T23:52:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248159>

Well done! Keep rocking it.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-11T02:53:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248167>

### Forest — 2018-08-11T07:02:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248207>

Yeah gotta change this night owl stuff. Many years of bad habits will take at least 90-120 days. Or maybe just 30. Had a no caffeine pre workout beverage (are those quantum?) it had good ingredients

Seem to have the most creativity and least 'writers block' on night. What about the notion that some h&g (about a quarter) were supposed to be night owls and work the old shift doing the special things like guarding against big cats. Is that just wrong?

If it's from fl, won't the shipping costs for a 90 lb bag be huge (some say it would be like 900). At the same time- how do stores and restaurants get it - it's already been shipped. How did u get them in bulk bags when in Nashville?

### Phosphene — 2018-08-11T17:49:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248232>

The seafood purveyor at my farmer’s market is also the local restaurant distributor. The fact that he will gladly sell you the same oysters for 1/4 the price charged at restaurants is not advertised. You have to ask. Nicely.

Check out the farmer’s markets and ask around. Asian markets usually have good fresh seafood too. None of those near me unfortunately, but I’d certainly make the hour drive if I didn’t find this convenient option.

I get you on the night owl thing, but it’s amazing how quickly the habit left once I really cut back on evening blue light. Some other poor schmuck will have to guard against the big cats.

### OptimalSkr — 2018-08-08T16:56:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-247898>

Hey all so I am finally taking Jacks advice and packing up for Mexico this winter! I plan to go for November to possibly February. I just wanted to start a dedicated thread for the PDC – I know there have been a lot of posts about it in other threads but this will create a library for us folks looking to go there. I think it would be good to catalogue a few things and get advice from others who have been there to make the transition easier on us newbies. Here are a few of the most pertinent topics:

· Best hotels to stay

· Best long term stay locations (monthly rentals) – Areas/Districts of the city that are optimal

· Lowest EMF zones – which types of cell towers they have - where are the good nature areas

· Cenotes List – (have already seen a few of these on the forum)

· Water issues (safe to drink tap water? and wash fruits/veggies etc)

· Where to get high quality groceries (grass fed proteins and organic veggies/fruits etc) and bottled water

· Websites people have used to book deals and find things.

Thanks

### Hunger4More — 2018-09-11T23:53:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-250766>

Just came back from PDC.

Cell towers are everywhere if you stay in the city. Normally, I have a huge issue being close to a cell tower. Once, while on vacation in Dominican Republic, my digestive issues and eczema went crazy as there was a cell tower next to our resort. Even though we were a similar distance to cell towers in PDC, due to the magentic anomaly, I had zero issues.

Cenote jardin del eden was my favourite.

Anything restaurant that's not on 5th ave will be reasonably priced and have local, seasonal food available. Seafood is plentiful. Some of my fav cheap eats were: Los Aguachiles, La Cueva Del Chango, and La Perla Pixan.

### mbhemming — 2018-10-03T21:55:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-252272>

Cell towers are everywhere.

It's so easy to make friends there. I wasn't expecting to have a full blown social life within 4 days of being down there. I still skype with two friends I met there. One is from PDC and one from Veracruz.

I stayed about a 5 minute walk from the beach in an AirBnB. Prices of everything are cheaper there. The prices are really cheap if you stay on the other side of the highway bu that may be too far to the beach, unless you have a bike. The place we stayed at cost 80$USD per night split between two people. Everyone told us that we got a good price for Dec 1st to Dec 23rd. I know the members here stay at The Royal. I didn't intend to but I ended up staying close to it and using the beach entrance right beside it. I also played beach volleyball at the resort everyday.

If you stay off the beach then take a taxi home if it's late at night. The police will rob you. They planted drugs on me and I had to give them my money to not go to jail. The actually set up ambush points on side roads and wait for tourists to come. You can walk home if your with a group that has mexicans in it. In the end they only took like 20$ from me lol. It felt like a real trip to mexico after being a victim of extortion!

Even walking 5 minutes away from the beach the food and drinks will be significantly cheaper.

Groceries shouldn't be a problem no matter where you are in the city. But check google maps before booking. I don’t know if the meat was grass fed or anything. Also there is one brand of butter that is real butter. I forget what it was called now.

Taxis will try to rip you off. When you meet mexican friends get them to tell you what prices should generally be.

Don't drink the tap water or order drinks that have ice cubes.

The beach is is nicer in Tutum but it’s nice enough. There is one entrance that smells like shit. Don’t worry, it’s just rotten seaweed. And it’s only at one small area.

If you like to party, then I can tell you about a hostel to go to on wednesday nights. They fill the roof with rooftop parties. It’s open to anyone, you don’t have to be staying in the hostel. Girls drink free and the beers are cheap for guys. I would say it’s 33% local mexicans and 66% tourists. It is the friendliest vibe I have ever seen at a bar.

The main street is made of bricks. You can just walk barefoot and be grounded.

I really loved it there. In fact just typing this message really stimulated feelings of how I mis it. I’m moving to Latin America in December. I plan on being nomadic at first. I hope to spend a significant portion of the year in PDC. I think it would be too hot for me to live there year round. It would probably be harder to get work done too. I plan on mainly living in the altiplano at around 4000-5000ft elevation. It’s like having spring all year round.

### GringoPerdido — 2018-10-04T01:27:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-252283>

Bro! Awesome info! Thanks!

I would like to check out that hostel party.

### mbhemming — 2018-10-04T04:37:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-252311>

[@RobInDC](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21869/) The hostel is at the corner of calle 34 and 20th ave. It's called Lobo De Mar.

This is me on the roof with a couple friends I met there.
