The last few months my BF has come around to the crazy. My happiest wins are (1) he is going to buy a reverse osmosis system for under the sink (2) he's been eating canned oysters on the regular (3) he's wearing his blue blockers!

### Phosphene — 2019-10-29T19:24:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-281481>

This is another thread where for some reason I don’t get alerts even though it’s watched. I unwatched and rewatched—let’s see if that helps. Glad you’re doing well Christine!

### Christine_L — 2019-11-06T16:20:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-281892>

I also dont get alerts! I thought it was me.

[@Cuffy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23820/) your strategy for the leather mocs is exactly what I did. I bought 2 pairs of the tough leather bottoms. I use those to ground in the winter and earthrunners otherwise. I found alot of barefoot toe box shoe brands on Instagram. Soft star shoes will be my next purchase. I dont think they do leather bottoms on everything though. Xero shoes are my go-to now. I love the way my feet look and feel! I actually wore my Kate spade heeled booties yesterday in an effort to boost my energy for going to work (long story but I've been an emotional drain on myself the past few weeks). My feet felt crooked and yucky after. No more heels for me. Letting go of the past is dirty business.

Check out crown prince naturals for the canned oysters. Where are you based? I'm in NJ. I get mine at trader Joe's and fairway.

### Christine_L — 2019-11-06T16:22:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-281893>

I agree with mountain valley and gerolsteiner bottles are annoying to keep buying and lugging around, but I love mineral water. It fills me up! RO water is recommended by Jack as well on one of the water special blogs.

### caroline — 2019-11-07T01:22:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-281949>

not sure Berkley is much better .....check it out first.....

### Christine_L — 2019-11-27T00:14:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-282821>

11/26/19
Been away and busy with life - which is good! Focusing on my seasonal eating, light, cold, CT, meeting friends, and keeping consistent for my health. Its hard but worth it.

I don't think we talk about that enough. Not the struggle itself but the fact we are trying, doing, accomplishing. All the small things add up to something greater. I do not always give myself enough credit - a pat on the back so to speak - not in a prideful way but an accomplished way.

There's something to be said about progress, positivity, your good thoughts, and dreams. Having given myself stress beyond belief this past month, reflecting on what the past/present/future holds, praying for both the living and the dead's souls, reorganizing priorities, and then giving myself a break again - I have to say that the changes you make matter. Even if you fall- you MUST ASK for forgiveness from God and yourself. Without this, you are just holding yourself back.

I look at my own progress and from those around me. I look at all of you, my friends who I do not always talk to, or who I neglect to check back on. This is for you. I raise my glass of mineral water to you as well!

For those who did not want anything to do with blue blockers but now want a custom pair!
For those who only wanted carbs to eat but now are buying oysters and salmon for dinner!
For those who who hated the cold but now go outside in 30 degrees in a bathing suit!
For those who bundled up and did not expose an ankle to sunlight but now are basically naked all the time!
For those who only drank tap water but now only drink spring fed live water!
For those who thought they couldn't... but then they did!

Love you all...

### Christine_L — 2019-12-05T22:47:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-283155>

12/5/19
Reading the relationship redox #10.

This quote has struck an old chord.

_"I suffered deeply from relationship ambivalence—this is the agonizing process of feeling uncertain about a romantic partner even while going through the daily motions of a relationship."_

Back in March I felt so strongly in my heart about what Jack was going through. It echoed my own sentiments. It gave me fire. I made a decision to break out and seize my life back. Then the attempt failed. After that disappointment I rationalized myself back to my safe zone, thinking I could fix my problems from within that safety until my situation changed. I thought that plan would be my saving grace. Of course plans are only that. Plans dont make you happier, only actions can free you and thus give you space to grow.

Feelings are beautiful and emotionally charging. They can give us wind in our sails to do things we never thought we could do. Yet when crisis of health strikes, the fleeting feelings cant give us the energy back. The fleeting feelings cant give us the time back. Thats when you need a rock. A fellow human or circle of friends who understand deeper than the happy thoughts and vibes of temporary moments.

Discernment counseling. I think everyone needs it. Maybe not just for couples relationships but for life, friendships, or career.

### caroline — 2019-12-09T00:11:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-283314>

What Jack has been going thru has really affected me too......it is so hard to see people in so much pain who you really care about.

All of this has so many lessons intertwined.

When you take charge of your life there can be so much pain .....but then ....the sun comes out!

When we take responsibility for our own happiness and wellness ......doors close and then wonder of wonders - new doors open.

Why are we so often afraid to take charge? what are we afraid of exactly?

We are addicted to our comfort zone .....no matter how bad, unhealthy and miserable we are.

After my DH passed away I was seeing a therapist to try to make sense of all of it.

This young woman said one thing to me that I will never, ever forget ........

WE ARE ADULTS AND WE MUST ASK FOR WHAT WE NEED. If a partner doesn't get what we need and what is important to us .....we must ask!

### Christine_L — 2019-12-10T20:03:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-283404>

Thats a valuable lesson [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/)!

Under the rocks and stones, there is water underground

Letting the days go by, water flowing underground
[Into the blue again after the money's gone
Once in a lifetime, water flowing underground](https://genius.com/Talking-heads-once-in-a-lifetime-lyrics#note-1174945)

### Christine_L — 2020-01-14T23:41:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-journal.21803/post-284586>

Update 2020

Happy New Year! A few thoughts on my mind lately, in no particular order:

1\. Everything you have or don't have in your life is a choice. Whether you actively or passively let it happen. Everything else is an excuse. You chose it!
2\. Time is a limited resource. Spend it doing things that enrich your being. The things you wanted to do, do them now. The past is over. Give tomorrow's "You" something to be proud of.
3\. Define what taking care of yourself means. Eating right, exercise, getting outside, seeing your friends, skin care, dressing with confidence, getting a haircut, getting a massage, having tea. Don't whither away due to the old "you".
4\. God is important. He is faithful, be faithful to Him. If you want to love God, then do it.
5\. Learn. Take it bit by bit to begin.
6\. Outlets for creativity help feed your soul.
7\. Are you proud of what you are today? If not, do something about it. Don't forget this. Don't let all of the " get through todays" make you comfortable.

# Thread: Why does blue light cause insulin resistance?  Ans:  Melanopsin.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-blue-light-cause-insulin-resistance-ans-melanopsin.21808/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-09T16:36:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-blue-light-cause-insulin-resistance-ans-melanopsin.21808/post-248016>

The realization of just how far behind nature I was in 2003 described in a simple math analogy of my life. Consider the plight of any clinician my age now trying to change within this paradigm and you'll see how hard it truly is. I graduated from college in 1986. In the 31 years since then the amount of biological information has increased 16 fold; during these 4 decades, my capacity to absorb new information has declined at an accelerating rate according to neuroscientists (if you believe them) and now is at least 50% less than when I was a medical student. If one defines ignorance as the ratio of what is available to be known to what is known, there seems no alternative to the conclusion that my ignorance is at least 25 times as extensive as it was when I got my bachelor’s degree. Although I am sure that my unfortunate condition comes as no surprise to my students and younger colleagues, I personally find it somewhat depressing looking at the math. My depression is tempered, however, by the fact that all biologists, young or old, developing or senescing, face the same melancholy situation because of an interlocking set of circumstances. But does this situation stop me from being optimistic now that I am inside my sixth decade? No.

The result of any project is not an endpoint to your life unless you are actually going to die at the end of the project.

The outcome of a project or life event opens up a new door, path, or change.

And whether the project succeeds or fails by your standards doesn’t actually matter to the black swan. It still leads you to the next event in your life.

And while it is true that each outcome leads somewhere different, you don’t actually know whether the success or failure door is the better one to go through.

Each result in your life is just a fork in a path that is endlessly forking. Nature is a pattern of forced decision making pathways for living creatures. This is why life remains dynamic and evolution really exists. And it is impossible to predict where it is leading until you understand the steering currents present in the environment at that present moment.

We need to begin to treat bad fortune with the same equanimity as you treat good fortune. Bad or good information is just that........information. Use it properly and you will still win when you understand how the steering currents of nature works on us.

And go do something big. Focus. Work hard. Aim for an outcome, if you want. But know that it’s just a tool to use to motivate yourself, not something that will make or break your life. There will be plenty more projects and outcomes afterward for the black swan mitochondriac. this is why hard things become OUR way. Discomfort and bad outcomes are the wind in our sails that allow us to tack a different course in our journey to gain Optimal.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-01-15T01:22:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-blue-light-cause-insulin-resistance-ans-melanopsin.21808/post-260339>

Bart Wolbers posted an interesting blog: [https://www.naturebuildshealth.com/blog/iron](https://www.naturebuildshealth.com/blog/iron)

My answer:

Iron's toxicity is mediated by sunlight and few people seem aware of it. the first steps of heme synthesis begin in the mitochondria in humans. Simcox JA, Mitchell TC, Gao Y, et al. Dietary iron controls circadian hepatic glucose metabolism through heme synthesis. In a series of elegant studies, they show that higher iron intake, and consequently higher hepatic iron, was associated with the change in the circadian rhythm of glucose and gluconeogenesis mediated by the repression of key gluconeogenic enzymes, PEPCK, and glucose-6-phosphatase (G6Pase).

The effects of iron were mediated by increased oxidative stress, resulting in increased peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor γ coactivator 1α (PGC-1α) transcripts and protein levels; induction of aminolevulinic acid synthase 1 (ALAS1), the first enzyme for heme synthesis; and an increase in total heme and heme B in the liver. The mechanism of the repressive effect of iron was shown to be the requirement of heme for nuclear receptor subfamily 1 group d member 1 (Rev-Erbα) to bind nuclear receptor corepressor 1 (NCOR) to form a repressor complex without any change in its protein levels, instead of increasing hepatic heme by administering aminolevulinic acid, thus bypassing ALAS1 or by feeding heme repressed gluconeogenesis. Here is more food for thought: an endogenous ligand of Rev-erb is heme (the iron-binding element in red blood cells). RBC's have a serious diurnal cycle. Heme is degraded into bilirubin. Elevated levels of bilirubin cause jaundice in kids born from parents who germlines are blue light toxic. The older treatment for jaundice was exposure to UV light but today's treatment of neonatal jaundice is now exposure to blue light in NICU's. This has been associated with pigmented skin and higher risks of skin and ocular melanomas as the kids get older because of the stimulatory effect of blue light on melanocytes. Blue light is a major regulator of circadian rhythms and Rev-erb is an executive-level player in this game. When heme is degraded carbon monoxide (CO) is made. CO prevents Clock/Bmal1 from binding to DNA. This binding Inhibits this process and throws off numerous other circadian rhythms in the liver. This is why blue light exposure is causative in most cases of diabetes. When you add in the melanopsin issue it is no surprise why diabetes is now explosive globally. In humans, there is reciprocal regulation of carbon monoxide and the circadian clock mechanism. (Klemz et al., 2016)

Bart seems very worried that eating more iron means more diabetes. That science has also been done and it is not true. The study by Simcox et al. underscored an important feature of chronobiology. A change in dietary iron did not perturb the periodicity of the chronome and it only impacted the amplitude of expression. The periodicity of expression of transferrin receptor, ferritin, PGC-1α, ALAS1, PEPCK, or G6Pase did not change and peaked at zeitgeber time (ZT) 10 and is likely related to activity and food intake. The key is the LIGHT that food was eaten under. If you eat that TOMAHAWK steak at 10 PM under LED light then being a carnivore could be an issue, not because of the steak but because of the effect of light. Most human-carnivore prior to 1879 never had to worry about this because we have no artificial light. I'm glad Bart post this because I hope he slays this dragon of bullshit because it once again shows my thesis light always trumps food nutrients in chronobiology always exists. Food is not the primary driver in anything. It just appears this way when you are missing the key data as this post points out.

The sequence of peak expression of ALAS1 at ZT10, followed by heme concentration at ZT12 and of heme oxygenase 1 (HO1) at ∼ZT18 ensures the tight regulation of the cellular concentration of heme at the mitochondrial level. People keep forgetting that heme is a mitochondrial process and blue light causes melanopsin dysfunction and destroys iron-based photoreceptors. High dietary iron was associated with higher peak expression of PGC-1α, ALAS1, HO1, and the total heme in the liver, but it never causes a problem unless the toxic light is present. The antioxidants (SOD1 and catalase) peaked at ZT6. These data point to the exquisite regulation of diurnal rhythm by central oscillators tied to LIGHT, not the diet.

What has Uncle Jack always said in his Leptin Rx?

SUNLIGHT and food in the morning are most wise and let endogenously produced CO from heme breakdown rhythmically tune the clock in the evening. This is why ALAN has to be absent!!!!! ALAN = artificial light at night.

# Thread: Light carries information; does this answer the mystery of telepathic communication?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-carries-information-does-this-answer-the-mystery-of-telepathic-communication.21809/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Denise0918 — 2018-08-09T17:24:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-carries-information-does-this-answer-the-mystery-of-telepathic-communication.21809/post-248031>

My dad was a radio operator on a ship in WWII that was torpedoed by the Germans off the coast of Murmansk Russia in cold waters. The ship went down. He and the captain stayed on the ship until everyone got off. He was sure these were his last moments on earth so he called out to his mother to say I love you and goodbye before he jumped into he icy waters. He survived and 6 months later he came home to see his mother in NYC. They were having tea and catching up. She listened to his war stories and ran out of the rooom and grabbed her journal. She opened to the page where she wrote down, I heard Billy call to me saying he loved me and good bye as if he was here with us. It was the exact moment my dad called out to her in the icy waters on his sinking ship off the coat of Russia. Now mind you my dad went on to become an electrical engineer and was not into any weird stuff but he told me this story as a child when I asked him about ESP when it was on the cover of time magazine. We agreed it was a mystery. Did photons of invisible light carry this message between them? WiFi is invisible light correct? Are we humans just building things externally that mimic what we can innately do but forgot how to do? Is all this wireless communication just mimicking our abilities ? But a poor copy?

### Michalis — 2018-08-10T14:14:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-carries-information-does-this-answer-the-mystery-of-telepathic-communication.21809/post-248085>

That seems to be the pertinent question! Would be interested to read what people think..

### Sue-UK — 2018-08-10T17:58:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-carries-information-does-this-answer-the-mystery-of-telepathic-communication.21809/post-248115>

> [Denise0918 said:](/goto/post?id=248031)
>
> My dad was a radio operator on a ship in WWII that was torpedoed by the Germans off the coast of Murmansk Russia in cold waters. The ship went down. He and the captain stayed on the ship until everyone got off. He was sure these were his last moments on earth so he called out to his mother to say I love you and goodbye before he jumped into he icy waters. He survived and 6 months later he came home to see his mother in NYC. They were having tea and catching up. She listened to his war stories and ran out of the rooom and grabbed her journal. She opened to the page where she wrote down, I heard Billy call to me saying he loved me and good bye as if he was here with us. It was the exact moment my dad called out to her in the icy waters on his sinking ship off the coat of Russia. Now mind you my dad went on to become an electrical engineer and was not into any weird stuff but he told me this story as a child when I asked him about ESP when it was on the cover of time magazine. We agreed it was a mystery. Did photons of invisible light carry this message between them? WiFi is invisible light correct? Are we humans just building things externally that mimic what we can innately do but forgot how to do? Is all this wireless communication just mimicking our abilities ? But a poor copy?

I think it could be that all the technology is interfering with our ability.

When I was a child I remember asking my mother if there was such a thing as ghosts. Her reply was that she didn't know …. but that in the same way as light reaching our eyes from a distant star could have taken thousands of years to get here, light from a person living a long time ago could be crossing time and space and reaching into the now …

Your dad was a transmitter, his mother was a receiver. If his _intention_ was to communicate with her, and she held, however unconsciously, the intention of wanting to hear news of him, I can see how that phenomena might be possible. Its a bit like when you think of someone you haven't seen for ages, and the phone goes and its them, or you meet them unexpectedly. Its almost as if an intention, or a thought, sends out a pilot wave, or information, from the transmitter to the receiver, that is not constrained by photons or the speed of light, or the 'standard' rules of time and space.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-10T20:28:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-carries-information-does-this-answer-the-mystery-of-telepathic-communication.21809/post-248129>

That's a wonderful, personal story about your Dad and his telepathic distress call to his mother in wartime.

I have read other accounts of this phenomena between people that are connected in some way. Even though our materialistic society discounts ESP abilities (or any other phenomena for which their current science and instruments can not account for) it is very real.

Telepathy is nonlocal in that it is instantaneous-- forget light and speed of light limitations. Nonlocality is part of quantum mechanics. You have probably heard of quantum entanglement (Einstein called it 'spooky action at a distance'). Quantum entanglement is when, for example, two particles which are paired or entangled, know and emulate instantly what each other is doing (for example, spinning in a certain direction). Distance is no object-- they could be at opposite ends of the known universe and it wouldn't matter.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-11T03:08:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-carries-information-does-this-answer-the-mystery-of-telepathic-communication.21809/post-248179>

I think it is part of the story. I think the bigger part is the biophoton release and the field to detect. I think the tensegrity of the melanopsin system and the skin and eyes optical windows are more critical

### Denise0918 — 2018-08-11T04:45:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-carries-information-does-this-answer-the-mystery-of-telepathic-communication.21809/post-248192>

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=248129)
>
> That's a wonderful, personal story about your Dad and his telepathic distress call to his mother in wartime.
>
> I have read other accounts of this phenomena between people that are connected in some way. Even though our materialistic society discounts ESP abilities (or any other phenomena for which their current science and instruments can not account for) it is very real.
>
> Telepathy is nonlocal in that it is instantaneous-- forget light and speed of light limitations. Nonlocality is part of quantum mechanics. You have probably heard of quantum entanglement (Einstein called it 'spooky action at a distance'). Quantum entanglement is when, for example, two particles which are paired or entangled, know and emulate instantly what each other is doing (for example, spinning in a certain direction). Distance is no object-- they could be at opposite ends of the known universe and it wouldn't matter.

Was it Einstein that said “ we are non local beings having a local experience “ ? I have read about research that Russians have done with particles and quantum entanglement showing one divided particle in two different places. They disturbed one of the particles and the other particle showed the intention it was going to be disturbed before the actual happening. Spooky science indeed! I like the possibilities it reveals for us.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-11T05:46:15+0300

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-11T18:25:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-carries-information-does-this-answer-the-mystery-of-telepathic-communication.21809/post-248237>

Dr Kruse is on to something with his bread crumb of an idea about nonlocality and time. Time does indeed seem to be a construct that humans can interact with but it is a latent power that we rarely witness.

Another wartime _telepathic_ incident highlights this mystery.

The German battleship _Tirpitz_ was sunk in a Norwegian fjord by British bombers in WWII.

About 1000 sailors died, but there were survivors. One survivor gave his private account of his desperate and perilous escape from capsized _Tirpitz_ by climbing up through the bowels of ship to the bottom of hull (now above water since ship had turned turtle).

He was ready to give up this life and death struggle when he saw a vision of his daughter waiting for him. This gave him a strong resolve to continue, and eventually he and other sailors were rescued when rescuers cut through outer hull of ship so they could get out.

_Now here is the punchline of story_\-- his daughter was as yet **unborn** when he had his vision trapped inside ship. And she was later born and grew up as in his vision.

Somehow he broke the time barrier and was able to see his future.

She opened to the page where she wrote down, I heard Billy call to me saying he loved me and good bye as if he was here with us. It was the exact moment my dad called out to her in the icy waters on his sinking ship off the coat of Russia. ### Jack Kruse — 2023-01-24T19:02:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-carries-information-does-this-answer-the-mystery-of-telepathic-communication.21809/post-319181>

Time is an emergent effect of the flow of entropy in a system. We are dissipative systems that create a negative entropy inside of us while we sense time outside of us.

What have my recent Patreon blogs said about periodicity and the flow of entropy? Periodicity makes clocks more accurate.

**Biological clocks are built as flow meters for entropy inside cells.

Biological clocks are built as flow meters for entropy inside cells. Potassium orders water molecules by hydrogen bonding network cooperation. Entropy is a measure of the disorder or chaos in a system, and the more ions present in a solution the more disorder there will be. In matter, solids have the most order and least entropy. They are held in a crystal lattice or network. Cells become liquid crystalline when K+ is added to a cell. The water molecule is quite small but the hydrogen bonds inside water are even smaller and most important to the creation of accurate biological clocks = they create an ideal periodicity = better timepieces. Damage to PER 2 genes by hypoxia ruins clock periodicity to drop NAD+ recycling in the matrix = less water created**

**What is the stoichiometry of K+ to water in mitochondria in your SCN that is working fine in a healthy state of redox power between -300mV to -400Mv in your mitochondria?**

**Experiments by Ling et al. have shown in healthy cells that each molecule of ATP in a cell controls 8,800 water molecule binding sites and 20 potassium ions to allow water to become structured inside every cell of your body. As redox varies in your matrix so does the K+ concentration and # of water molecules created by mitochondria. Melatonin, NAD+, and CO2 all vary in the same way as potassium does to redox power inside a cell. Environments change redox power by varying the charge density of the hydrogen bonds in water that mitochondria create. What changes charge density most in nature? PHOTONS.
**
Sunlight controls the major metabolic pathways in all living substrates. Light controls metabolism, not food.

Modern science now reports that the entire activity of the enzymatic machinery of the TCA cycle is known to be regulated by Sirtuin3 (SIRT3)-mediated de-acetylation ([Yu et al., 2012](https://www.cell.com/cell-reports/fulltext/S2211-1247\(19\)30910-6?_returnURL=https%3A%2F%2Flinkinghub.elsevier.com%2Fretrieve%2Fpii%2FS2211124719309106%3Fshowall%3Dtrue#bib73)), What centralized healthcare misses is that system is under circadian control as reported by [Peek et al., 2013](https://www.cell.com/cell-reports/fulltext/S2211-1247\(19\)30910-6?_returnURL=https%3A%2F%2Flinkinghub.elsevier.com%2Fretrieve%2Fpii%2FS2211124719309106%3Fshowall%3Dtrue#bib50). This means that when we study metabolism light-mediated controls have to be included in the methodology. If it is not controlled for your study is WORTHLESS.

Poor sunlight, darkness, and geoengineering will have a major effect on cellular hypoxia. Cell hypoxia is controlled by the hypoxia-inducible factor (HIF-1). Do you know the link of HIF 1 to the sun? Light- and oxygen-sensing pathways are linked on a cellular level in all mammals ([Gu et al., 2000](https://www.cell.com/cell-reports/fulltext/S2211-1247\(19\)30910-6?_returnURL=https%3A%2F%2Flinkinghub.elsevier.com%2Fretrieve%2Fpii%2FS2211124719309106%3Fshowall%3Dtrue#), [Hogenesch et al.,1998](https://www.cell.com/cell-reports/fulltext/S2211-1247\(19\)30910-6?_returnURL=https%3A%2F%2Flinkinghub.elsevier.com%2Fretrieve%2Fpii%2FS2211124719309106%3Fshowall%3Dtrue#), [McIntosh et al., 2010](https://www.cell.com/cell-reports/fulltext/S2211-1247\(19\)30910-6?_returnURL=https%3A%2F%2Flinkinghub.elsevier.com%2Fretrieve%2Fpii%2FS2211124719309106%3Fshowall%3Dtrue#)). Hypoxia-inducible factor 1⍺ (HIF1A), is an evolutionarily conserved transcription factor enabling cellular adaptation to low oxygen availability ([Semenza, 2011](https://www.cell.com/cell-reports/fulltext/S2211-1247\(19\)30910-6?_returnURL=https%3A%2F%2Flinkinghub.elsevier.com%2Fretrieve%2Fpii%2FS2211124719309106%3Fshowall%3Dtrue#bib58)). Here is the kicker: It belongs to the same protein family as the light-inducible circadian core protein Period 2 (PER2)
([Liu et al., 2012](https://www.cell.com/cell-reports/fulltext/S2211-1247\(19\)30910-6?_returnURL=https%3A%2F%2Flinkinghub.elsevier.com%2Fretrieve%2Fpii%2FS2211124719309106%3Fshowall%3Dtrue#bib38)).

So any damaged to PER 2 = lowered clock periodicity = low NAD+ = hypoxia = occurs when sunlight is missing = Time is relative according to Einstein = I just showed you how time is changed in this post.

Time manifests when photons and water meet in a cell to build a self-organizing dissipative structure.

[https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/cells-amo-physics-experiment-nature-jack-kruse/](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/cells-amo-physics-experiment-nature-jack-kruse/?fbclid=IwAR3GWtqGPY_Eu8objcZ7JN5GYT2dwhdeZlxBMgBM1ifuq__kXlQ0iVZjkew)

# Thread: Every doc needs to build their own forum to communicate.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/every-doc-needs-to-build-their-own-forum-to-communicate.21812/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-10T04:02:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/every-doc-needs-to-build-their-own-forum-to-communicate.21812/post-248068>

You can use your forum to qualify people and see who really has skin in the game and really wants to help themselves. Then when they do qualify themselves use Patreon to really build your brand in helping those who want to help themselves.

I am so thankful for content creators who are moving all of their best \*\*conversations\*\* behind paywalls such as Patreon. I hope they would KEEP them there and ti changes the business on social media.

Removing people unwilling to pay for access is one of the best parts of life -- people who don't value a topic aren't people who will add value to the conversation. When people do this and waste your time you can just thin the herd and focus on the people who really are on a mission. It is the anti-burnout mode of future medicine.

I almost wish Facebook/Linkedin/Twitter would just go away and be replaced with an entire pay-for social network. Want to read an intriguing post with wisdom? Pay for it. Want feedback or comments? Pay more for it. Want to share it? Pay for it, but you get to charge for access to it from others. That is how you create value.

The idea that Facebook is "free" for billions means it's not valuable at the core.

SteemIt is a poor social network that doesn't do it correctly. It still attempts to be "free". The paywall is the ultimate resource for restricting access to low-value participants.

I would pay $50-$100 a month for a fully paywalled social network, but I wonder what it would look like at first. Noiseless and productive to sharing your best ideas.

It would be awesome to share book chapters this way.

### caroline — 2018-08-10T07:36:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/every-doc-needs-to-build-their-own-forum-to-communicate.21812/post-248071>

I need one place to go for info and to communicate ......please, please,please. It used to be here , the forum and the blogs and the podcasts. etc.

I simply can not manage twitter and FB and all the other platforms to keep up.

I want to keep up ....but I also intend to live my life in the best possible way and that means I need to seriously limit my devices.

To be happy and well I need to spend as much time as possible outside and doing things I love with my partner.

Also - being on a device a lot sets up an exclusion zone ...... I am giving priority to these stupid gadgets over the people I love.

We need to concentrate on our relationships/life .........and I also need to listen/read as much of Jack's information as I can manage.

And - he is so freaking prolific!

### Antonis — 2018-08-10T16:31:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/every-doc-needs-to-build-their-own-forum-to-communicate.21812/post-248099>

I haven't been here for a long time. I was since july last year and I learned many facts of life and to sense the reality. Am still learning and try to connect the dots of each aspect of life coming from the golded lessons of Jack Kruse. That's where we all focus to road of successful optimal life. I believe YES this forum should be the only place to communicate. No Facebook, Instagram, Linkedin and Twitter. Mr Jack knows why he is using them and I believe, once more people be members then he may decide to keep it only here.

### George Papamarkos — 2018-08-10T11:07:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-counter-measures-list.21814/post-248079>

I'd like to collect all the possible counter-measures that can be taken to minimize the effects the bluelight.
I have collected and applying the following :

1.  Bluelight blocking glasses. Yellow during the day, red after sunset
2.  Wear long shirts to protect the skin
3.  Wear redish and purple clothes most of the time
4.  Have a UV light (aka black light) + red light in front of you when working on screen - only during the day.
5.  Drink water with a bit of salt to force hydration
6.  See the sun @ the morning and during the day

Please contribute more

Increase DHA

DDW

Wear a scarf around the neck to protect the thyroid

Download Iris software

Have candles near screens and devices for red/infrared

Quantlet

Download twilight on android smart phone

On iPhone turn on night mode for all day and all night. Can also turn on a red colour filter for the iPhone

No artificial lights after sunset

Reduce screen time and time on technology

Best time to use technology and screens would be at solar noon

Take regular breaks outside every hour

Spend as much time as possible outside in natural daylight

Best option of all, get the hell out of the power grid and eliminate all use of technology/Artificial lights

### Jack Kruse — 2019-07-30T22:22:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-counter-measures-list.21814/post-275205>

Ok, everyone, I'm serious. We need to talk...........about the light you allow around you as you live.

It's time for you to see the light.

Actually, its time for you to not see the light at night.

In this study of elderly adults, artificial light at night of just over 5 LUX, increased their risk of [#diabetes](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/diabetes?source=feed_text&epa=HASHTAG&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARBXwqAJNeL3AKv0P83UCTO8XIZ3QctDs00JcgfFg87JMz0ZnEh-KFho5IkP6TM3kCGorfITUb-UH6qsqrfo3O4HS0qZrJolte_Ua9jQWvwZrYThZiyws6kcRRPIUu66f4OVD2CGM5rF7F0RluEmtEFrLGNFjQtNWxDrafHaPPQWPcJ4eUayhpZBePchq_6kz1xRmMRvCEkqVgHvu_ss9HgXGRz7LbU28ASOZYHV1NY_VNK03NsGuhX6mubGsoGOttGlhxbMvf7-kmb0EPnPrCWkDoH5Bn6F7DBVc8Or4A8owuklk7TRxhgk97f293gyB60hEHwGNuHCywvvENUrK6f_j1QpXT6UovbAvLi5bbiT8pifJS6ge-Hrugc&__tn__=%2ANK-R) by three times.

Even at 3 lux, the results were significant. An on/off blinker light on one of your electronic devices can generate three lux of light.

Continually exposing yourself to light at night is kind of like eating cupcakes 24/7.

Put the light cupcake down.

[#MakeLUXLowAgain](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/makeluxlowagain?source=feed_text&epa=HASHTAG&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARBXwqAJNeL3AKv0P83UCTO8XIZ3QctDs00JcgfFg87JMz0ZnEh-KFho5IkP6TM3kCGorfITUb-UH6qsqrfo3O4HS0qZrJolte_Ua9jQWvwZrYThZiyws6kcRRPIUu66f4OVD2CGM5rF7F0RluEmtEFrLGNFjQtNWxDrafHaPPQWPcJ4eUayhpZBePchq_6kz1xRmMRvCEkqVgHvu_ss9HgXGRz7LbU28ASOZYHV1NY_VNK03NsGuhX6mubGsoGOttGlhxbMvf7-kmb0EPnPrCWkDoH5Bn6F7DBVc8Or4A8owuklk7TRxhgk97f293gyB60hEHwGNuHCywvvENUrK6f_j1QpXT6UovbAvLi5bbiT8pifJS6ge-Hrugc&__tn__=%2ANK-R) (at night)

### Jack Kruse — 2019-10-16T19:40:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-counter-measures-list.21814/post-280582>

Why is the pupillary response the most critical part of the cranial nerve exam diagnosing TBI
The ipRGCs are known to connect to many brain regions that regulate very different tasks. The cells tell one part of the brain how bright it is outside so that our pupil can rapidly close—in less than a second. When this does not happen with a TBI patient it alerts us there is a problem with the catecholamines like dopamine or with melanopsin damage in the retina due to the liberation of Vitamin A from this opsin. The same ipRGCs also connect to the master clock in the brain that regulates our sleep-wake cycle. However, it takes several minutes of bright light (blue) to make us fully awake. How the same ipRGCs do these very different tasks with different time scales was not clear until now based upon this study. Some clinicians have realized this for a long time and been using the pupillary light test with blue light and red light to see the damage in the eye clock in TBI cases for years.
The investigators found that the difference has to do with the way that light detected by the retina reaches the brain. By delivering the mini-SOG to the eyes of the mice, they were able to trace the signal to the part of the brain that constricts the pupil in response to light to identify the pathways.
It turned out these connections were much stronger—similar to water pouring out of a garden hose. Whereas the connection between the ipRGCs and the master clocks in the SCN in the retina were much weaker—more like drip irrigation. This is because the ipRGCs deliver the light signal to the circadian center in the eye clock phenomena through this slower drip system, it takes longer for any meaningful information to reach and reset the brain clock. It also implies since this system works slower in the retina in TBI’s the use of light has to be altered to reduce the symptoms of photophobia and post-concussive effects.
CITES:
[https://www.cell.com/cell-reports/fulltext/S2211-1247(19)31183-0](https://www.cell.com/cell-reports/fulltext/S2211-1247\(19\)31183-0)

### Jack Kruse — 2019-10-24T18:50:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-counter-measures-list.21814/post-281176>

Here's a new one for Dr. Jack Kruse "I told you so" archives.
Blue Light at night (bALAN) raises blood glucose, alters insulin response and also increases hunger in DIURNAL rodents.
"Diurnal" means these animals in the study are naturally active during the daytime like humans and not active at nighttime (nocturnal) like the vast majority of rodents used in studies. So this is an important study because it relates more closely to you (diurnal humans).
Start taking artificial light at night seriously folks. You may think those weird colored glasses from RaOptics that some of us are wearing at night are funny but it is certainly no joke to your long term health.
[https://physoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.14814/phy2.14257](https://physoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.14814/phy2.14257)

# Thread: Social circle (5 closest friends) hack
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/social-circle-5-closest-friends-hack.21827/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Forest — 2018-08-11T07:35:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/social-circle-5-closest-friends-hack.21827/post-248209>

Reference group or "social circle"
the Harvard study found after many years you become the average of the 5 people you hang around most. People mirror each other.

When I first was told about this years ago I thought it could be overcome by discipline. Now after reflecting maybe there was wisdom to this.

How do you find mentors and peers who are good quality. Especially if you live far away from them. How have people done this in the past and distant past and the way way past? Is there quantum insight to this? Can this be overcome by self discipline? What criteria should you seek? Is this that important? How have y'all done this very successfully in the past, moderately successfully and not at all successfully? Are virtual friends, vids and books at least 80 percent as good, or not even close? Should you hang out with people who are"easy" to hang out with if there aren't that many options. What if really cool people are busy? How much time do you have to spend with them weekly for it to be effective?

Sorry if these questions are a lot. Been thinking about this for years.

### Sue-UK — 2018-08-11T14:31:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/social-circle-5-closest-friends-hack.21827/post-248220>

One of the hardest things I've had to do is assess my relationships. It has meant letting go of people who are energy vampires, even if they are family. It doesn't mean I wouldn't speak to them if we meet, but they are kept at an energetic distance. My criteria is that if every time we interact, the interaction leaves me feeling drained, its time to rethink the relationship. A one off if they've got something happening and they are unloading is different, but you know the type I mean.... Persistent negativity ...And yes, in hindsight I see how in the past I've been locked into periods of negativity myself, and I was that person.

I have a small group of very good friends, and when we arrange to meet up, the _anticipation_ of meeting up with them raises my dopamine, the meet up is a joy, and that feeling is there afterwards, and every time I think of them. They are the people that even if I don't see them or speak to them on the phone for weeks, when we do speak its like we pick up where we left off. Its as if I have a pattern stored of their energy, and the pattern is reinforced by our next interaction. Leave contact for too long, and the pattern seems to start dissolving... but often that's what is meant to be.

Virtual friendships are different, for example on this forum I get an idea of someone's energy from their words, and from the avatar picture they post, but nothing beats meeting someone face to face, even once. Eye to eye contact would give a lot more information about them to me, and me to them. I think it goes back to light - both parties emit light from their eyes, and those photons interact and exchange information. When I was practicing as a therapist, I would do phone consults, (it was in the days when face time or skype video etc wasn't the norm) but I always preferred to meet them at least once.

I saw a programme recently about how technology affects our interaction with people, and apparently when you talk to someone face to face, the brain lights up in a certain way, talk to the same person on a computer screen and, although you can see them, the brain doesn't light up in the same way. It was quite interesting to me as it kind of answered more scientifically my intuition on why I haven't wanted to move abroad to live permanently in the sun - my brainwave patterns from my interactions with my children and grandchildren would not be the same using visual technology. If I had no other choice, it would be better than not 'seeing' them at all, but if all my important relationships were based on that, in the long term it wouldn't be a good thing. Another thing I saw was a study on centenarians, and maintaining 'real' social contact was number one important, well before smoking, diet etc.

I don't think its about finding 'cool people' to hang out with - it's my responsibility to try to be someone that cool people want to hang out with. Which takes me back to Jack's post in the beginners forum:

_'Don't survive, thrive. Do life soaring. Our mitochondria in our cells read and react to our environment; it shapes us and we are shaped by its "perspectives".
The six best healers in the world that I know:
1\. Sunlight
2\. Un-fluoridated water
3\. Magnetism/grounding to Earth/CT protocol
4\. seafood
5\. Self Confidence
**6\. Friends who connect with you in life and not just on line.**
That is all...........Carry On.'_

### Lahelada — 2018-08-11T15:47:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/social-circle-5-closest-friends-hack.21827/post-248224>

Beautifully put Sue. I would add that it is step 5 after 1-4 have worked properly. My new friends literally came out of the wood work when and who I least expected. I was not looking. Your energy signature is what attracts a friend/a mate. We have a narrative of "we are into the same things in the knitting class" but it is not really about the knit one,pearl one,drop one aspect of the activity. The activity is an excuse to meet likeminded people or people you want to be like.

# Thread: Genious way to Eliminate ALL Blue Light from your TV!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/genious-way-to-eliminate-all-blue-light-from-your-tv.21829/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PayitForwardinLife — 2018-08-12T23:54:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/genious-way-to-eliminate-all-blue-light-from-your-tv.21829/post-248329>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

This is genius! This company, Saffron, came up with this device that’s called DriftTV and it plugs into your tv and elimates 100% of blue light! Check it out here- [http://seesaffron.com/drift-tv-box](http://seesaffron.com/drift-tv-box)

As we all learned in the Vermont 2018 talk that our skin is HUGELY sensitive to blue light and receives far more then then the eye does simple by the amount of surface area exposed to ANY and ALL artificial light sources. So wearing your blue blocking glasses is NOT enough! Period! Not even close! Lets face it people ANYTIME we are using these electronic devices, tv’s or under man made artificial light we are “dancing with the devil”. If you dance with the devil long enough...your gonna get burned! Just always keep that in mind. Don’t ever lie to yourself or accept it as a half-truth. The Vermont 2018 talk really should be EVERYONE’s wake-up call!

So how does this device work? You unplug the HDMI cable that goes into your tv and plug it into this device and then take the HDMI cable that comes from the DriftTV device and plug it into your tv. It basically acts like anin-line blue light filter/eliminator. You can actually adjust the percentage of blue light the tv receives from 0 to 100%. Pretty cool.An on screen menu allows you to adjust the percentage of bue light that’s displayed on the tv from 0 to 100% and even what time it does this from and to. All for 120 bucks! Pretty cool in my opinion. No tv is the best option in my opinion though. Nature and great friends is the best entertainment. Period.

If you have a smart tv and what you watch is NOT coming thru a HDMI source then the DriftTV device will NOT filter squat! So if you are using any of the smart apps such as NetFlix, Amazon Prime, etc that come with your tv your screwed.

However, I have a hack! Simple too. Get a Apple TV, Roku, etc. or DVD Player that has built-in smart apps (NetFlix, etc.) and then plug the HDMI cable that comes from any of those devices into the DriftTV and then plug the HDMI cable coming from the DriftTV into your smart tv and BOOM! Hack = zero blue light! \*Note- just always make sure to use the Apple Tv, Roku, DVD Player to watch your shows thru!

Hope this helps! Reviews online ALL sound great! The company is small and so they are currently just making small batches. So pre-order is the option for now..

After watching the Vermont 2018 talk we just ripped two of our three flat screen tv’s that we have in our house off the wall and going to give them away! We are DRAMATICALLY reducing this toxic shit in our lives, and our kids. Period. Next we are cutting a big hole in the wall where the tv’s where and putting in a big ass window to see nature. That will be our “entertainment”! The only tv in the house left is gonna be used mostly for watching Jack and used as a tool for learning!

We bought a high powered dark field microscope with a high powered camera which connects to our big screen tv so we can look at our blood and do better hacks and learn the effects on real-rime on what it’s doing to our bodies. Super great way to “awaken your consciousness” to how you and your environment are treating your body. Super cool! Our kids are stoked!

with gratitude and light, Jason & Jamie

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-15T20:50:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-docs-are-beyond-repair-when-they-are-ignorant-of-dha-effects-on-thinking.21831/post-248539>

Speaking of ignorance, I'd like to hear more discussion about causes of an ominous drop in IQ levels in general populations.

This is a reversal of the famous 'Flynn Effect' which observed an increase in IQs in the last century.

Research has shown steady decline of IQ scores for those born since 1975. One estimate is IQ scores dropped about 25 points in the last 20 years (with boys suffering slightly more decrease than girls).

It is without doubt due to environmental factors, but researchers are baffled as to causes.

My information indicates a key culprit is exposure of fetuses to microwave RF radiation, which of course has increased tremendously since that time.

Unless society puts a leash on our global microwave RF experiment, it is conceivable that each succeeding generation will be dumber (and probably more sterile), which puts humanity closer to some form of extinction.

If the overall current level of behavior in public, media, government, TV etc. is any indication, I would say society is indeed fraying at the edges of cognitive competence.

And if you know movie "**Idiocracy**", it would be an ironic form of life imitating art.

“The thing about these games is it gets their dopamine systems involved releases addictive chemicals, very similar to a drug addiction. It literally causes cravings they can’t live without it they just get obsessed with it," McCarthy said......

“I was setting my kids up for failure. I was trying to get them to follow the rules and them to self-police," she said.

McCarthy says if your child is playing more than an hour a day, it’s time for you as the parent to pull the plug – cold turkey. And it won’t be easy.

It is a very dynamic process driven by hydrated protein antennae that act as resonant light levers for action sending and receiving information constantly to attune the system and build a fidelity to the signals it senses. The screen built by nature is the liquid crystalline array of topologic insulators designed to capture sunlight and use it in a myriad of ways allowed by quantum mechanics. IT ALREADY HAS........If you travel a lot you better pay attention.** The start of the disease is not important, but when they decided to put WiFI inside a metal tube with 250 people inside is a big deal and when they got to 3 G and 4G this is when illness showed up because heteroplasmy rates in mitochondria ramped up in flight attendants. I actually spoke to a Delta union about this and warned them it was coming in the next few years.......and now American is feeling the effect of non-linear aspects of light by seeing medical costs rise. See CARIE FISHER as our canary in the coal mine from 2017 for what is coming soon 24/7 for us all.

Her lifestyle and job as a shift worker under blue light and nnEMF for decades gave her a low heteroplasmy rate. Heteroplasmy rates rise as our ability to generate heat in the mitochondria DECLINE.

The hotter an engine can run the more efficient it is. This is why a jet engine is more efficient than the one in your car.........but nothing man has ever built matches the efficiency of the mitochondrial matrix heat engine.

What atom is in the matrix? H+ = hydrogen in the protium isotopic form. It heats up faster than its other isotopes and it makes perfect thermodynamic sense why nature chose it over deuterium to run our hydrogen heat engine.

**_Carnot_** showed us a few hundred years ago how a hydrogen heat engine like mitochondria really operates. **He showed that the best way to increase the efficiency of a heat engine was to increase the difference in the temperature from the heat source (matrix) inside the engine and the exterior surroundings.**
Fisher's life as a shift working actor decreased the colony of mitochondria in her to make heat. As it got worse in her retinohypothalamic tract she could not make the 6 chemicals that come from POMC. One of them is _**beta-endorphin**_. When you do not make enough of this because your hydrogen heat engine runs cooler.........you begin to have to use exogenous drugs to offset the inability to make this chemical from the lack of heat your colony can create. As she lived, her heteroplasmy rate lowered further with her drug use. Another chemical made from POMC is **_alpha MSH_**. This controls appetite and your skin color. It controls how much melanin is in your skin to absorb sunlight well or poorly. Carrie was white as ghost almost her entire life. As her hydrogen heat engines failed alpha MSH crashed and she became leptin resistant and got obese. There is a deep lesson here for those not ASLEEP because of what they learned in their flawed pasts.

As heat generation capability drops, less energy is made to do the physiologic work of making chemicals our cells rely on to operate properly.

Frequent flyers will soon be wise to buy term insurance once the transition to 5G is met. In December 2018 we are at this point. Next weekend I will be bringing this story to Los Angeles to many of Ms. Fisher friends and co-workers. I expect sudden deaths, heart attacks, psychotic breaks, and blood clots to become commonplace on frequent flyers, stewardess, and pilots. Any shift working business traveler better know the real reason why Ms. Fisher had the life she did. It was all caused by how the AC power grid, blue light, and nnEMF conspired to decrease the amount of heat her colony of mitochondria made and it leads to chronic leptin resistance.

The AC power grid, with blue light nnEMF exposure (2G-5G) day or night, degrades your heat engines = HETEROPLASMY RATE. How does the cascade appear to the clinician? More sun = more melatonin = less chance of leptin resistance. More time spent within the AC power grid, = more tech use = more screen time = more blue light = 2g-5G EXPOSURE = your colony of hydrogen heat engines cannot generate heat = sickness comes faster and death comes sooner.

# Thread: Is CT beneficial to asian and african?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-ct-beneficial-to-asian-and-african.21836/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tsoang — 2018-08-14T06:26:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-ct-beneficial-to-asian-and-african.21836/post-248426>

Jack mentioned that northern european can uncouple their mitochondria so CT is beneficial to them. But some people with dark skin with L0 haplotype does not uncouple their mitochondria. I heard Jack once said CT is not good for people who cannot uncouple their mitochondria.

I'm south east asian and I want to find out if doing CT is beneficial for me. I have 23andMe report but I can't find if I have L0 haplotype. How do I find that out on the 23andMe report?

Thanks guys.

### tsoang — 2018-08-14T07:06:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-ct-beneficial-to-asian-and-african.21836/post-248427>

I found out my haplotype is N.

Should I do CT? Is it beneficial or bad for me to do CT?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-14T15:01:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-ct-beneficial-to-asian-and-african.21836/post-248449>

The hypothesis of Asia as the place of origin of haplogroup N is supported by the following: Haplotype group N is descendant from African L3 after these people left to populate northern parts of the planet in Eurasia. This haplotype also went south to Australia too. The migration was likely due to a supervolcanic eruption in the tropics in Sumatra that may have affected photosynthesis and the growth of food due to blockade of the sun. This lowered the temperature inside the tropics for highly coupled humans and was the environmental driver to leave to look for land where food could grow with sunlight. It might have been further north and south outside the tradewinds where temps were colder but fodo grew. Hence this drove the L3 group to a more uncoupled N group who could handle colder temperatures. Haplogroup L3's exact place of origin is uncertain. According to the [Recent African origin of modern humans](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Recent_African_origin_of_modern_humans) ([Out-of-Africa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Out-of-Africa)) theory, the clade is believed to have arisen and dispersed from [East Africa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Africa) Rift zone I wrote about in the brain-gut series of blogs between 84,000 and 104,000 years ago.

1.  Haplogroup N is found in all parts of the world but has low frequencies in Sub-Saharan Africa. According to a number of studies, the presence of Haplogroup N in Africa is most likely the result of back migration from Eurasia.
2.  The oldest clades of macrohaplogroup N are found in Asia and Australia.
3.  It would be paradoxical that haplogroup N had traveled all the distance to Australia or New World yet failed to affect other populations within Africa besides North Africans and Horn Africans at the southernmost tip. This suggests an ocean migration.
4.  N1 is the only sub-clade of haplogroup N that has been observed in Africa.
5.  CT can be done with an N group but your SNP's and SAP's will tell you how effective it might be.

### Joseph Thia — 2018-08-14T17:27:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-ct-beneficial-to-asian-and-african.21836/post-248462>

> [tsoang said:](/goto/post?id=248427)
>
> I found out my haplotype is N.
>
> Should I do CT? Is it beneficial or bad for me to do CT?

Did 23andme give you more details for your maternal haplogroup? Mine is M8a2, and there was a paragraph in the summary report for mine that said, "Overall, members of M8a remain relatively close to the region of origin today, and are found mostly in southern Siberia, Korea and the northern Chinese provinces of Liaoning, Xinjiang, and Qinghai."

### tsoang — 2018-08-15T04:55:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-ct-beneficial-to-asian-and-african.21836/post-248495>

> [Joseph Thia said:](/goto/post?id=248462)
>
> Did 23andme give you more details for your maternal haplogroup? Mine is M8a2, and there was a paragraph in the summary report for mine that said, "Overall, members of M8a remain relatively close to the region of origin today, and are found mostly in southern Siberia, Korea and the northern Chinese provinces of Liaoning, Xinjiang, and Qinghai."

Yes, it says Haplogroup N is through out Eurasia. Why are you asking?

### James Lech — 2018-12-05T10:25:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-ct-beneficial-to-asian-and-african.21836/post-257288>

In my interview with Jack Kruse he discusses coupled and uncoupled haplotypes. This interview specifically referencing Africa and South Africa, where there is around a 50/50 of coupled and uncoupled haplotypes.
[http://jameslech.co.za/2018/07/23/e...rgeon-understanding-how-nnemf-is-child-abuse/](http://jameslech.co.za/2018/07/23/ep-7-superdads-dr-jack-kruse-neurosurgeon-understanding-how-nnemf-is-child-abuse/)

In the interview, it is discussed how CT isn't a very good thing for coupled haplotypes and that a light protocol is better. However, it doesn't mean the coupled can't still engage in cold-adaptive training. The article is an excellent example of this. The Khoi San, referenced in Dough Wallace's research as an L-0 coupled haplotype. The Khoi San typically live and migrate in a hot and arid environment. However, this group engage in the Frigid cold waters of the False Bay. They dive into the Kelp (seaweed) forests, rich and abundant in amazing sea life. You will really love the visuals. Including swimming with Great White Sharks

[https://magazine.africageographic.com/weekly/issue-44/wild-beneath-the-waves-sea-change/](https://magazine.africageographic.com/weekly/issue-44/wild-beneath-the-waves-sea-change/)

Hey guys, Newbie here - I went to the fountain for a drink, and now I'm drowning in the ocean!

I understand the "eat your latitude" concept and it makes sense. But when does your latitude become your latitude when you move? Immediately, after 5 years, how long? In other words, I moved from Philadelphia (lat 40 degrees) to Tampa area (latitude 27 degrees) about 20 years ago. So I image that is enough time to consider "my latitude" is 27 degrees and it correlates with my light cycles.
But what about the epigenetics? My parents, gparents, and even ggparents all came from Philadelphia/Italy all around the 40th parallel. Does that mean that I'll be mismatched if I eat local or mismatched if I continue Philly-style? And what are the ramifications from the epigenetics when families move like this? Jack, you did this same thing leaving NY on your way ultimately to NO.

I've felt worsening pain after first week of cold face dunking and showers alternating hot/ cold and also bath with 76 degrees water (coldest possible until I start adding ice later). Thanks!

### kris90 — 2019-04-25T20:46:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-contraindications-for-ct-therapy.21852/post-268913>

Based on everything I've learnt over the years, CT is always beneficial for any condition if we have a loosely coupled haplotype, however, there's a lot we must get right in order to EASE the adaptation process. I'm actually working on a Circadian Reset Program that is a step-by-step guide to doing everything you must do in the proper order to improve your health (what we learned from Jack). I'll share the order with you:

1\. Mitigate nnEMF and Blue light
2\. Restore the circadian rhythm (i.e. AM sunlight, Leptin Rx, PM darkness)
3\. Begin water loading (i.e. source good water, increase consumption and consider DDW if applicable)
4\. Learn optimal breathing to increase oxygen assimilation
5\. Begin CT Protocol (i.e. facedunks first, shower, then tub. Start temps high and gradually steepen gradient)
6\. Begin Intermittent Fasting Protocol (i.e. 16/8, 18/6, 20/4 or 1-2x 24 h fasts per week)
7\. Begin Exercise (i.e. basic movements)
8\. Seasonal Eating (i.e. add carbs back into diet in summer months)

Hope that helps!

I was in mexico for much of winter and I found I could hang out in 80 degree ocean water for even an hour with no problem...Jack said that THAT was a good level of CT for me for now. # Thread: Hi from New Zealand

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thrive1992 — 2018-08-17T11:28:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-new-zealand.21857/post-248670>

Hi team
I have been following Jack Cruz for about 1.5 years at this stage. I have been blocking blue light with Uvex Skyper since 2014 but back then I thought they only needed to be worn for 30 to 45 minutes before sleep. Now I wear them as soon as the sun is down and minimise all artificial light.
I have actually searched through this forum quite a lot as a nonmember. I know all the basic information and some of the more advanced information as well. I was living in Sydney for almost a year working in the tech industry (I wanted to move somewhere with more sun and so I could do the pursue a hobby) but I decided to quit and now I am training to be a beekeeper and studying organic farming at university back in New Zealand. I think this will allow me to have an income without having to sacrifice my health in the future.

Are there any other New Zealanders here??

Here is a brief overview of my health problems:
ADHD, supposedly had "depression" during 2014 but it was probably just blue light toxicity, cold hands and feet (not sure if I have raynauds or if it's just poor blood circulation or some other blood related issue), possibly some gut health issues (BM are not as consistent as I would like), I have an odd small bone growth on the side of 1 of my fingers which could be a ganglion, since increasing sun exposure and red light therapy (JOOVV and redlightman) I have noticed an increase in freckles on parts of my body (I have no proof that this is because of the increase sun exposure but I have definitely noticed it over the last year ) maybe there is something toxic in my blood at the moment, I have some clicky joints/possibly low-level arthritis, gadolinium toxicity due to the fact that they used the gadolinium dye in my left hip for an MRI 7 years ago, pectus excavatum, seems like I have a weak connective tissue as well so possibly Marfan syndrome,

here is a brief overview of the things I do to improve my health:
pitch black bedroom, Wi-Fi router off, no gadgets in bedroom, we live at the end of a no exit Street with farmland and forest bordering 3 sides of the property so very low wi-fry, at this stage there is no 5G in Christchurch, reverse osmosis drinking water, shower water is still regular tap water, 1st thing in the morning I spend 5 minutes outside in natural light grounding, DHA consumption – seafood, organic eggs and meat, currently eating minimal grains and no dairy – I might try ghee soon, sweet potatoes often kumera, apples and pears often, fruit and vegetable is organic when possible, I do fasting but it's not same amount every day, in 2 days I will be having a consultation with a biological dentist to get information on removing my amalgam fillings which I have approximately 4 of, SOTA blood cleaner and water ozonator, I'm also going to start creating my own magnesium bicarbonate soon, I'm also working on my mouth and tongue posture after learning about Dr Mike Mew and Sarah Hornsby last year

I am thinking of getting the magnetico 10 Gauss but I've read so many stories of people having a difficult time with that and you also see a lot of people selling secondhand on Facebook so I'm a little bit unsure of whether it's a good thing to spend money on or not.
I'm thinking of getting the lessEMF.com gloves and baseball cap – the baseball cap would be for when I am in a lecture or library with a lot of non-native EMF. The gloves are for at home when I'm forced to use my laptop for 30 minutes or so to protect my fingers from my laptop.

Would love to hear from anyone, I plan to become an active contributor to this Forum

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-05T14:43:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-new-zealand.21857/post-257293>

Researchers fine tune how Gadolinium causes its problems as it passes through the brain's water networks to cause the symptoms associated with this transition metal.
In a study published online on 25 November by Investigative Radiology, Dr. Katerina Deike-Hofmann and colleagues from the German Cancer Research Center (DKFZ) at Heidelberg University Hospital and Essen University Hospital explain how gadolinium enters the cerebral spinal fluid (CSF). They show that GBCAs are excreted through specific entry points, such as the choroid plexus and the anterior chamber of the eye, into the CSF and subsequently enter the glymphatic system that, for instance, surrounds the perineural space. [https://www.auntminnieeurope.com/index.aspx](https://www.auntminnieeurope.com/index.aspx)

# Thread: Aaron's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-journal.21858/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aaron Freeman — 2018-08-18T00:30:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-journal.21858/post-248706>

Debating how to write this Journal.... Context is important, but this could get to be very long.

Grand Mother died in 80's of spinal cancer
Mother is in 70's and is obese and has many health issues, many years ago had ovaries removed due to cancer.

I used to be very fit... Martial Arts, Weightlifting, Cycling

Got injured in 1998 and everything went to hell.
Now 6' 4" obese 418, 47 years old. Now even with my injury, I was what I would call a highly functional obese person, would work circle around everyone, until 3 months ago.

I was home alone and just out of the blue I thought I was going to die. Passout, could not focus, felt like someone was going to flip my light switch off and that was it for me.

Over about 4 weeks I was in the ER 5 times with one overnight stay. At this time I was on no medications.. When I would first go to ER my BP would be 190/110, but after being there for 30 minutes un medicated my BP would be normal and they would send me home, no medication. Even the overnight stay nurse took BP ever 4 hours and by morning time they were going to give me BP meds, and I asked what is my BP and they stated normal, so I asked why should I take it, the doctor came and yelled at me for being a bad patient. So I caved

Went to my Primary doctor, Cardiologist, Neurologist.
Blood work basically normal
EKG normal
CT normal
Wore a heart Holter 2 weeks, Normal
MRI normal

The primary doctor wanted to put me on antidepressants for anxiety. I really felt like it was symptoms first followed by anxiety, so I picked up the prescription, but after I read the side effects I just did not take or refill. The primary doctor now not returning my calls.

Symptoms 3 months ago:
Numbness in the head(both sides of my face, and throughout the day a circle of numbness around my head.
Feel like I am going to pass out
Numbness in arms
Chest and back pain right side
vision issues

Symptoms now:
Crowning numbness few times a day
Face feels heavy instead of numb
Chest and back pain right side once per day
vision issues(sometimes I feel like I do not need my glasses and sometimes I really do)
Just feel off and not myself
I used to walk 3 miles a day, and now I am lucky if I can do .5 miles

About 2 months ago I went to see a functional doctor and feel a much better report.
He did much more complete blood work.

Low testosterone
High estrogen
I can add labs if needed.

SO I am now on many supplements, and Testosterone pellets, and Thyroid and estrogen blockers.

Test left to do cardiologist wants to do a Treadmill Stress test, and tilt table test.
I do also have occasional hand tremors so trying to schedule an appointment with the neurologist.
Awaiting heavy metal test results, and a fecal test.

So when this all started I was 460, and as stated above 2 weeks ago I was 418, only weigh myself at doctors. The strange thing is for the last 7 years I tried everything to lose weight, but nothing worked, and I lost 30 pounds prior to meds from the functional doctor, so I do not think I can say it is from his scripts.

What has changed in my diet?
No dairy (used to drink a few glasses of milk a day)
added fiber

That is all, so did the fear of dying to make me lose weight??? Or was dairy and lack of fiber key??? Whish I would have known this 7 years ago.

So why did I join this group?

Still having the symptoms, and feel like I am a quarter functional then I was 3 months ago. Seems essentially all other doctors other than functional say you are fine except anxiety. I know there is something missing and needs to be addressed I just do not know what.

Lastly, the BP meds I have been taking 40mg, I cut back to half a pill by meditating 3 times a day, and working from home and not driving into work(stressful due to vision issues) on half a pill my BP is going down to 95/74 and I am most likely going to stop taking soon.

For all that have made it this far thank you and I appreciate your time.

# Thread: Living in Ethiopia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-ethiopia.21860/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Valerie — 2018-08-18T03:03:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-ethiopia.21860/post-248713>

I flew back on the plane next to a high school girl from Ethiopia who spoke excellent English and who had previously spent summers in the US. We talked about life in Ethiopia compared with USA. She commented on how energizied she felt in Ethiopia, how religious people are and how frequently they would fast as part of their religion. She commented on how old people in Ethiopia were so healthy compared with US and that chronic disease hardly exists. I shared with her what I had learned from Jack's teaching about sunlight and how going forward she would need to think about that since she was moving to San Diego to go to school. Our discussion opened up her eyes and she said this made perfect since to her. This just reinforced for me how we need to get back to nature.

### JanSz — 2018-08-19T00:05:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-ethiopia.21860/post-248757>

Overnighted people are likely a rare occurrence there.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-ethiopia.21860/post-261777>

# Thread: How melatonin operates........
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-melatonin-operates.21870/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-19T00:38:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-melatonin-operates.21870/post-248763>

Someone asked me on the Vermont 2017 video: "Why are sunsets so important to a mitochondriac?"
Answer:
Because the color temperature of PM light rises and this is the blue crescendo photic stimulus to the melanopsin/retinol system and signals the origin of activation of melatonin during darkness after its aromatic rings were initially excited by AM IR-A and UV-A light. This is also a big leptin and amylin trigger too for the same reasons.

### Jack Kruse — 2021-04-25T06:55:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-melatonin-operates.21870/post-297735>

Neurodegeneration is always associated with a sleep defect because of mtDNA damage that destroys melatonin autocrine production. This sets up the circadian cycle loss that destroys dopaminergic neuron destruction.

Sleeping less than six hours a night when you're in your 50s, 60s, and 70s increases your risk of dementia by 30%, a long-term study has found.

[https://edition.cnn.com/2021/04/20/health/sleep-dementia-risk-study-wellness/index.html](https://edition.cnn.com/2021/04/20/health/sleep-dementia-risk-study-wellness/index.html)

### caroline — 2021-04-25T08:48:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-melatonin-operates.21870/post-297738>

We are almost always in bed by 8;30pm but quite often wake up at 3:00am. we usually fall back asleep in about 30 -60min and wake about 5:30am.

This seems weird - I don't understand why we always wake around 3:00am?
But it does seem like we get enough sleep.

Is this okay for continued good health?

### Jack Kruse — 2023-09-20T18:49:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-melatonin-operates.21870/post-326630>

What happens when you knock out melatonin receptors via melanopsin dysfunction from the liberation of Vitamin A by blue light???

You get insulin resistance and set the stage for many mitochondrial diseases like muscle atrophy, diabetes and cancer.

In muscles this is how their function is destroyed in a gym because it changes muscle fiber type and lead to popped biceps, achilles tendons, and triceps so popular now in the NFL.

It is why all the MLB pitchers keep needing elbow and shoulder surgery but most of the centralized orthopods do not know a dam thing about how light really changes the colony of mitochondria in muscles in gyms, with cell phones, and iPads (their playbooks)

Do you know what I'm talking about? Light shapes life. How is it shaping yours? The link below should open your mind a bit even if you are a centralized shill for Pharma or a professional sports team.

[https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1038/oby.2010.24](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1038/oby.2010.24)
