# Thread: I love this group!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-love-this-group.21873/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dr. Jon Motley — 2018-08-19T07:03:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-love-this-group.21873/post-248780>

Hello, it is really great to Dr. Kruse and this group!

I became interested in light and energy when I started Chiropractic college (1995), I read The Body Electric, and gave a presentation to my class in my first year on Holograms, Homeopathy, and Acupuncture on how they worked energetically. I thought that the Acupuncture Meridians were step down transformers for cosmic energy (light), turning it into a dc current in the body. The presentation went over everyone's head, including the teacher, lol.

Later I learned of **Dr. Christopher Hills** who wrote several books on the nature of light color and consciousness, which I am still trying to process, because his views on color and light are so different from what we are taught. I was wondering if Jack has heard of his work, **Nuclear Evolution: The discovery of the Rainbow Body**, I think it is something he would understand. Out of Dr. Hills work came the Heartmath group, which I use in practice.

In 1996 I discovered Applied Kinesiology and Dr. George J. Goodheart Jr., where I learned about muscle testing. Since then, I have been practicing in Manilla, Philippines.

From Applied Kinesiology my perspective on the diet was to correct abnormal function of the digestive system through proper diet, once that was working I had patients follow a Keto diet. After listening to Jack I realize that a lot of what I have been doing is a temporary thing, which is why many people tend to get better for a while, then they regress into their old patters and problems. I am in the process of education my patients on the Leptin Rx and CT now.

A bit about me: I am 51, born in Denver, Co., lived all over the midwest to California. Father was Italian and Cherokee Indian, Mother was a big mix of Western European (traced her family to coming off the mayflower). Do not know my Haplogroup.

Was always skinny, even when I moved to the Philippines, never wore shoes, swam everyday, and always ate higher fats and proteins, although I snacked a lot. Then I married and after the first year I blew up like a balloon. Since then I have gone up and down with the weight and currently sit at 210, at a height of 5'9".

I was an avid video game player for years, with many hours glued to the computer screen, which has dropped off dramatically with the finding of this group.

I have always sweat profusely with mild exertion (same as my father), which gets worse if i drink coffee, was foggy in my thinking and often had to grasp for words, even though I knew what I was trying to say. Had a low energy level, which I would try to bring up with coffee in the morning, I smoke golden virginia tobacco, use hemp papers, no loss of sex drive with the wife which has always been high, I do not exercise, but my work on patients keeps me fairly fit (I have a website with some videos of patient treatment [www.chiropracticphilippines.net](http://www.chiropracticphilippines.net)).

I have always been a night owl, often staying up to 2am in the morning, when I would go to bed my mind would often stay active and not allow sleep. sleep has never been restful, and always woke up early, 7-9am, sometimes crashing at around 3-5pm and napping 3-4 hours. I have always practiced mindfulness and introspection, or have tried too.

After discovering Jack and this site I have been studying like mad to catch up on what i have been missing. Printing out tons of information because I just hate reading off the pc.

I get out into morning sun at 8am for 15-30 min while grounding.
Eat breakfast after that.
Been eating seafood, Tuna, Salmon, shrimp for lunch and dinner, fresh eggs from our chickens in the morning.
Drinking ice cold water after meal.
always take cold showers as it is usually hot here, swim almost daily in cold pools, beginning ice baths in the next few days.
Bought BlueBlockers glasses and wear them religiously when indoors around lights, tv's, pc's, cellphones.
When not posting or treating patients, I am outside reading and getting the sun.

Any thoughts on how I am doing would be much appreciated, wife thinks I am crazy... again, lol.

Again, thank you Jack for this wonderful site!

### caroline — 2018-08-19T08:26:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-love-this-group.21873/post-248781>

Hi Jon and welcome. It sounds like you are doing pretty good.

Are you outside for sunsets too? swimming daily in cold pools is so awesome .....naked with your wife would even be better!!

### thrive1992 — 2018-08-19T14:23:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-a-bio-dentist-what-are-the-best-materials-to-replace-amalgam-fillings-with.21874/post-248792>

Hi team,

I'm Seeing a Bio-Dentist, what are the best materials to replace amalgam fillings with??

[http://www.dentistryonmerivale.co.nz/team/](http://www.dentistryonmerivale.co.nz/team/)

I have been told the main 2 types are "composite" and "crowns" ($$$$)

There are a few different types of "composite" options. The Bio Dentist can also probably get access to other options if i ask for that.

I have heard quartz mentioned in other biohacking groups. I think the composite does include some quartz but not completely quartz.

What is the best replacement material considering we are going into nnEMF world?

I want to do this right first time.

What sort of research can I do for this?

Would love to hear any opinions or thoughts on this.

Also if this is the wrong sub forum to post this in please let me know

Update 20th August 2018::

Today I saw the bio-dentist.
Turns out i only have 2 amalgam fillings (both on the right side of my mouth - side note: many of my health problems are on the right side of my body but not sure if this is coincidence)

I also have 1 composite filling on the left side - although this is not toxic like amalgam it may be from a less good brand of composite fillings and the bio-dentist is also able to remove this one.

I told the biodentist about Dr Jack Kruse and Dr Dean Bonlie (magnochelation). He had not heard of either but has said i can email him links as he is interested in other natural health doctors.
I explained how Dr Kruse has mentioned that blue light getting deep into gum/dentin during the amalgam removal operation is not good. The operating room has a huge window so maybe i can time it for a time of day that has the sun shining through? Not sure if that would make up for the blue light though...

My options for replacement are:

$1200 per ceramic filling (porcelain)

$300 per composite filling
[https://www.kulzer.com/en/int/dentist/products\_from\_a\_to\_z/venus\_2/venus\_diamond.aspx#wr-tab-3](https://www.kulzer.com/en/int/dentist/products_from_a_to_z/venus_2/venus_diamond.aspx#wr-tab-3)

Prices in New Zealand Dollars
Both prices include the dentists' cost for actually doing the procedure.

Ceramic advantage is:
I know exactly what is in the product. It is stronger than composite (= less risk of something happening later in life and it breaking). It is more "natural" than composite. With the composite product, i can't actually find out all the ingredients as apparently it is proprietary information.

Composite advantage: Price is lower. The Venus Diamond composite is better than most other composites as it is BPA free. The company also has a history of producing dental products that are more on the "natural" end of the spectrum according to the biodentist.

The Bio Dentists' wife is a holistic doctor/naturopath type and she can help me do a heavy metal lab test using my a hair sample (is this the best way to be doing it?).

# Thread: Meg's Optimal Journal - Hormones and Fat Loss
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/megs-optimal-journal-hormones-and-fat-loss.21876/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Meg V — 2018-08-19T22:12:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/megs-optimal-journal-hormones-and-fat-loss.21876/post-248812>

BIO: Manhattan KS 39 parallel. 37 year old female with history of PCOS (metformin), low thyroid (Armour) which lead to difficulty getting pregnant. It took us 3 years. Our son is now 2 YO. I’m 150# and about 22% body fat. I have a history of endurance training/racing (Ironman triathlon) which I believe contributed to some of my hormonal issues; I stopped racing in 2012. I have followed some of Dr. Kruse’s work for the past couple year but have really went down the rabbit hole the past 2-3 months. I was getting more sun as soon as it was warmer out and now realize how important the sun is (2 months ago I started tracking with dminder), and low and behold, I got my period back for the first time since before being pregnant. Clearly the morning sun got my hormones back on track. When I became very interested in this information, my husband found out he has some heart issues (low ejection fraction, enlarged heart) and has followed me down the rabbit hole. It’s a lot easier when we are both on board with making some changes especially to our house and spending as much time as possible outside including the morning sunrise.

MOTHER: over weight, high blood pressure, history of Graves disease, most likely on the verge of diabetes. Lives in northern MN and has all her life.

GRANDMOTHER: died at 94. Stroke at 70 but returned to baseline. She smoked and drank beer all her life - but stopped smoking after the stroke. The last couple years were spent in the nursing home and quality of life was poor. She kept her mental capacity up until close to the end. No diabetes. She was lean and a hard worker. Lived near lake Superior in MN all her life.

DNA: I have not done 23 and me but I have done ancestory. Scandinavia 22%, Ireland/Scotland/Wales 21%, Europe South 17%, Great Britain 11%, Finland/Northwest Russia 8%, Europe East 7%, Native American 4%

PROTOCOLS: I’m currently doing the leptin reset. It’s not perfect but I’m making the changes. Prior I ate a lot of food and was hungry every couple hours - but maintained my weight. I now eat close to 30 min upon waking, which is all fat and protein (this is what I was eating prior to doing the LR, it seems to help regulate my blood sugar for the day) and black coffee. I’m getting about 50g of protein. Then I eat a large lunch about 5 hours later that contains carbs (green apple, almond yogurt, berries). About 5 hours after that I eat dinner. I’m not to the point where my dinner is the smallest meal. I have made breakfast my smallest meal for years so this is a big change. I’ve ben able to go without snacking but I’m definitely hungry come meal time. I do crossfit but changed the time to evening class at 420pm. They keep the lights off right now and the garage doors open so naturally lit. I’m getting about 10-15 min of grounding and AM sun (due to my work schedule). I work a couple hours but in a hospital so horrible lighting. I wear my blue blockers as much as I can, but my arms are exposed. I can somewhat control some of the lighting in patient rooms but that’s it. I’m only there about 2 hours (but about 7-9am so I miss the most important sun of the day!). I try to be outside in the sun as much as possible the rest of the day with my son. We have no WIFI in our house but have many neighbors around us with it.

IMPROVEMENTS: better mood, more calm. Got my menstrual cycle back. Morning fasted blood sugar is lower (avg just below 90 now).

QUESTIONS:

Any tweaks or what am I missing with the LR? Basically I’m eating early and exercising in the evening. I’m also eating a lot more seafood. I was eating basic paleo prior so not many changes to what I am eating.

After a little over 3 weeks, which I realize isn’t far in, I have not lost any weight in fact I am 3 pounds heavier. I’ve read women tend to have body comp changes before weight loss but curious about others experiences. My meals are pretty large, but I can hardly make it 5 hours between meals. And I realize this is more than just weight loss, but I am frustrated with that part. It's so much easier for me to skip breakfast.

When should I add cold?

What do you guys do for lighting in the winter? For example, right now I put on a little make up with a salt lamp as the sun is starting to rise but I think this will be more difficult as the fall and winter approaches. Do you use any red lights in the winter?

We also live in KS near a military base and other members have made comment to how bad this is for our environment. Can someone explain what is bad about Fort Riley and how far would I need to need to be away? We are getting some meters to do some testing so maybe that will help us there.

GOALS: My hope is to come off all mediations and heal my PCOS. We want to get pregnant again. I also want to be an example for my son. I also want to loss a little fat while maintaining muscle.

LABS: I don’t have exact numbers in front of me, but my T3 runs just above the “normal” range, my TSH has always been very, very low despite being hypothyroid. I will have labs run again in October so will have more data to add then.

Thanks so much for any thoughts/suggestions and insight you have. I am thrilled to be on this journey with you all!

### Sue-UK — 2018-08-20T15:47:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-and-green-blockers.21877/post-248832>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2018-8-20_12-49-0-png.8667/) (Top line is rural).

I want to see a spectral analysis of ratios in the "light recipe" commercial blue blockers allow through, not just what they exclude. For example there's the question of green in relation to intraocular pressure. [https://www.healingtheeye.com/Articles/AJO\_IOP\_ light.pdf](https://www.healingtheeye.com/Articles/AJO_IOP_%20light.pdf). Sunset at 0 degrees solar elevation contains green, and a -6 degree solar elevation doesn't exclude green. Blue may be balanced by red, but the green may be balancing the red … Yellow also drops after sunset compared to a +6 elevation, but it isn't zero.

There's also the question of what other frequencies are being excluded that may be important so I also want to know if signalling other than melatonin and melanopsin is affected by going from a "same" (Martian? ) landscape to darkness …. Better than doing nothing, but with an eye problem I can't take the risk of too much red. I'd like to be able to buy some more stylish 0 degree, -6 and -12 degree solar elevation glasses, rather than have some cobbled together ones made with clear safety glasses and gel filter paper. I live in hope.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-20T17:28:13+0300

### Lahelada — 2018-08-20T19:26:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-and-green-blockers.21877/post-248891>

My five cents. You cannot expect yellow blueblockers to block blue and green, not even orange ones will do that satisfactorily. The tint needed borders on red and is very dark. Lens material is another. I have both the orange glasses and the red ones. I make them here for Argentines and they pass the test.

### Trev Douglas — 2018-09-06T10:09:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-and-green-blockers.21877/post-250336>

I think clarity around the terminology "blocks' vs "filters" is important to communicate clearly.
If one claims to "block" a particular "colour", imo it ought to allow 0% transmittance of that entire range of the spectrum.
If any of that colour transmits through, then "filters" would be more appropriate, and disclosing by what % at what nm even better.

I think something like this chart below at least makes it clear what they do, and what they don't do...

I would be interested to see the curve on a lens that claims to "block" green...if it really blocks 100% of green at the farthest end of the green spectrum and doesn't let 0.01% in until Yellow, then WOW!

# Thread: Jon's Journal

### Dr. Jon Motley — 2018-08-20T06:21:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.21879/post-248823>

I have been doing the Leptin Rx protocols/CT for 7 days, been using BlueBlockers and just bought a new pair from Ra Optics. Here is what i have noticed:

1.  Sleeping easier going to bed at 9-10pm instead of 1-2am, mind does not race as much, don't toss and turn as much.
2.  Energy levels have increased, more awake in the morning, no brain fog, thinking clearly.
3.  I used to be very sensitive to noise, angered easily, now none of that! Totally amazing!!!
4.  Feel relaxed, always been a happy person, but just feel better in general, happier.
5.  Body feels tight, like it is shrinking, or like I have worked out even though I haven't
6.  I do not crave the snacks I used to, not hungry after my main meal.
7.  Getting up much earlier to catch the sunrise, when I used to sleep in to 9-10am.
8.  Staff and family notice my belly is getting smaller.

### Dr. Jon Motley — 2018-08-22T07:09:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.21879/post-249096>

So where does one start on this site? There is no reference, and the search bar has not been helpful.

Coffee, fresh brewed (Lavazza), Is it good or bad?

If good, when can you have it? If my routine is Light+grounding, Breakfast, CT.
I like to read while getting the light, and usually have coffee with mapel syrup and butter. cut out the maple syrup and replaced with VCO because not good for LR.

Same thing with Chocolate, 80% dark?

I am currently eating 4 fresh eggs with butter and Fish eggs on top. Ice cold water after (Perrier). Not hungry at all till 5pm, then have sauteed shrimp or muscles.

I read outside and get the sun as it sets, but 30 feet away there is a post with 3 big ass transformers on it... that can't be good. The bright halogen light come on just before the sun goes down, so I move to the back yard.

Try to make it as dark as possible, but family still uses the kitchen light which I changed to amber incandescent, and their cell phones and PC's, complete with 4G wifi. wear my Blueblockers inside, and the bedroom is always dark, so I have been trying to get to bed at 9pm or before. Body is much more relaxed but still do not sleep right away, so I am mindful. waking earlier every day to watch sunrise.

Doing what I can to get myself better, then family. Until then, they are going to use their electronics.

### Dr. Jon Motley — 2018-09-08T07:24:49+0300

Update... 3 weeks into Leptin reset, all good. Then I got an inflatable tub that I could fill with ice, got it to 62 degrees, used it once at around 5pm, then in the morning at 5:45am, stayed in about 5 min, ate breakfast, drank water, got in it again for 8 min. (was unaware that should do the CT after eating/drinking.)

For the first 3 weeks, cold showers and pools (empty stomach), eggs/bacon/butter breakfast, shrimp or sashimi for dinner, coffee with butter/coconut oil/maple syrup with breakfast. No problems whatsoever. lost 8 pounds.

# Thread: Janek's road
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janeks-road.21880/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Janek — 2018-08-20T11:31:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janeks-road.21880/post-248826>

Hello

It's always easy to visualize yourself writing all your stories and background, and seeing all these helpful posts, but when it comes to reality it's way harder to start then it seems to be. So it's for me now.

My name is Janek, I'm 25 years old and I'm living in Poland in really poor quantum yield. When did my story start? Like everyone's story, it started 9 months before my birth, but I will go through it in later posts because I'd like to start from now and go to my past.

Since I'm not getting a pension for being a student anymore I can call myself a professional poker player, because it's my source of income. Later I will write more about it, but now all you need to know it's not a gambling, it's like chess, but with a variance, so weaker player in short-term may win against me, but skill-wise and in long run I'm the winner.

In 2013 I worked as a builder's assistant for one month in July. I worked 10-hour a day in sunlight doing physical work. Work was pretty hard and my life was like working, taking a nap, hanging out for an hour or two and go sleep because I had to wake up at 5 am. Even so I felt really good, my mind was clean and I felt happy. A month later I flew to work in Denmark in a plastic factory. I worked on 2 shifts, morning (6am to 2pm) and afternoon (2pm to 10pm). Then my problems appeared. I had trouble to fall asleep, I was feeling really bad, my depression started and I started to google, and google, and google... Because I'm very critical to what I read It started the journey to where I'm now. I worked there for 3 months and go back to Poland for lovely winter. My dopamine was low, I was doing nothing, but playing video games and hanging out with energy vampires (actually my energy was so low I don't know could they drain anything). I was a little better because I didn't work in toxic environment anymore.

In 2015 I moved out from my parents and another dark chapter began. I'm living in block of flats on 9th floor with power line 50m from it. I started to see all my neighbors are in poor health. Many are abusing alcohol, some are kind of mental, so I know this is the worst possible place to live. Especially my town is high in population density. I think one of a problem in this flat was bright artificial light from outside that made my sleeping room bright enough to see everything. I hacked it, but I must change my place because hacking doesn't help much. I made my sleeping room as dark as possible, I'm taking care of my circadian rhythm and pay attention to blue light after dark. I'm doing better because I had a trouble to fall asleep again and sometimes could do it even till 4 am. Right now I'm in better condition but It's far away from being optimal. Every year is same story. In summer I can do well and I have some motivation, but in winter I'm depressed and feel really badly. This winter I was literally playing 12h a day video games. I was trying CT and only if I did it everyday I had a little motivation boost and once I didn't go CT-ing it all collapsed.

This summer I'm doing well and made some money, but I fell quantum yield is lowering (saw falling leaves recently that made me sure about it) and I start to feel worse. That's why I need this journal to measure a change and to keep me on track to change my environment. I want to move probably to Bali at the begining of November otherwise I will see a dark side of life this winter again. And I'm really feared by it.

I will write soon more about me, post my health history and my blood test to do a picture of me, because I'd like to become a member and get help from Dr Kruse. Please let me know what should I post here else.

Greetings
Janek

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-amazing-news-article.21885/post-248884>

Dr. Michael Holick’s enthusiasm for vitamin D can be fairly described as extreme. The Boston University endocrinologist, who perhaps more than anyone else is responsible for creating a billion-dollar vitamin D sales and testing juggernaut, elevates his own levels of the stuff with supplements and fortified milk. When he bikes outdoors, he won’t put sunscreen on his limbs. He has written book-length odes to vitamin D, and has warned in multiple scholarly articles about a “vitamin D deficiency pandemic” that explains disease and suboptimal health across the world.

### Nick004662 — 2018-08-21T04:14:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-brisbane-australia-daily-habits.21888/post-248961>

Hello fellow Mitochondriac's!! A bit about me, SInce my teenage years I've really struggled with ADHD and was medicated with Dexamphetamine for a number of years. It somewhat helped, but it made me feel like a robot. I lost the ability to connect with people, i never joked around anymore!!. Once that medication stopped working, I swapped it for cannibus which worked great as long as I was always stoned. If not then I'd be depressed. I also had my health food kick around now which certainly helped. Still, I partied a lot and once I had to get a real job, I sobered up and severe despair and depression took hold and I couldn't function. I'd never felt such hopelessness before.

Around 3 Months ago I actually committed to daily morning meditation. Each day I felt a little better. Until 2 months ago after listening to abraham hicks on youtube, the next video started playing and it was Dr Jack Kruse. I resonated with what he was saying immediately! Since that day I have been reading/listening to this magnificent light, water and magnetism story as much as I can around work. I've been implementing what I learned and I feel AMAZING, I'm more social, confident, clear headed, energised and most importantly HAPPY!

Seeing as there's so much to learn about I'd like some advice on what I should be doing

This is my routine so far

Cold shower then Morning sun 8-9 (grounded ofc)
Water is from italy because our water wont hydrate us because of the levels of dueterium??
Seafood daily
Ice bath twice a week (5min)
Iris blueblocking software and blue blockers for when I'm working at the hospital
A have a red light, but it's not LED, does that matter?

Also
I read somewhere that Aussies really need a magnetico? why is that?

SO GRATEFUL FOR THIS COMMUNITY

Nick

# Thread: Kids CT / Leptin
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-ct-leptin.21889/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Erin Gaiser — 2018-08-21T15:20:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-ct-leptin.21889/post-248983>

How are people adapting the protocols for kids?
In terms of CT: just being outside and going in cold rivers/lakes/ pool?
What about in terms of Leptin? I can't imagine that my kids will eat for example 2 meals a day. Is three ok without snacks?

Any help appreciated!

### countingstarsx — 2018-08-21T19:28:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-ct-leptin.21889/post-248998>

For my daughter I cut all grains out of her diet, and am not letting her go crazy with carbs anymore. I am also structuring her meals, breakfast earlier, only one snack, and supper earlier. No more eating after dark. Once fall/winter is here I will probably cut her carbs back a little more.

As for CT, I have been giving her cooler baths lately, and we occasionally go to the pool. The real deal will start when winter comes and I let her play outside somewhat minimally clothed. She is 3 so I am going to let her guide the process.

# Thread: Playa del Carmen, Mexico is not the only place to go - Africa's cradle of life has some gems
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-mexico-is-not-the-only-place-to-go-africas-cradle-of-life-has-some-gems.21891/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### James Lech — 2018-08-21T19:02:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-mexico-is-not-the-only-place-to-go-africas-cradle-of-life-has-some-gems.21891/post-248996>

Jack was kind enough to be a guest on my show \[ [http://jameslech.co.za/2018/07/23/e...rgeon-understanding-how-nnemf-is-child-abuse/](http://jameslech.co.za/2018/07/23/ep-7-superdads-dr-jack-kruse-neurosurgeon-understanding-how-nnemf-is-child-abuse/) \]

Admittedly, I was extremely nervous in the interview but the discussion points about Africa and the brief discussion after the show really created some disturbing curiosity inside me.

Some of my results to share are as follows:
Note: I confirm there are many roads to Rome, but this is what I found based on the interview.
Following the three-legged stool of Light, Water and Magnetism. Jack talks about Playa del Carmen, Mexico having magical mitochondrial properties: I managed to find observations and spatial mapping to find similar effects within Africa. Note: yes when looking at the earth's magnetic anomalies properties map (link further below), Playa del Carmen has an insanely high magnetic field compared to surrounding areas to it. The three-legged stool is an equation. Thereby, like Playa del Carmen is an easier flight for those in North and South America. I believe the proposed region I discuss below is ideal for those in Africa and Europe.

When you think of Africa referenced in movies and the Lion King, they also film and reference this part of Africa. It is also the area of the great Wildebeest migration Jack talks about.

Magnetism:
East Africa's Great Rift Valley, spanning Ethiopia, Kenya, and Tanzania. [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great\_Rift\_Valley](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Rift_Valley)
The area has high volcanic activity with old and new forming volcanoes, thereby, close to the magma.
A better view of the magnetic fields map can be found at: [http://www.wdmam.org/download.php#downloadmodel](http://www.wdmam.org/download.php#downloadmodel)

Additionally, from Gerrald Pollacks work, there is very high wind gusts as a result of the high negative ion content in these regions. This helps with greater EZ generation. Note, this also links to the highest concentrations of negative ions is in the afternoon in African and Europe, but in the evening in the US.

Light:
Not only is the region on the Equator but you have a number of very tall mountains resulting in even greater levels of UV.

Water:
From the region there is a mix of DDW and high D concentrations of water sources:
[https://agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1029/2009JD012166](https://agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1029/2009JD012166)
Naturally, the rain and rivers have the highest concentrations of D. Typically, the sources from underground have the better DDW levels, however, not all areas are equal. If you look at the graph carefully, you will see a few well points with very very depleted DDW levels. This would link to the areas with greater magnetic fields linked to volcanic activity.

Diet:
Seafood: The ocean is not too far, whereby if you are inland, it is not the furthermost distance to get your seafood. However, it isn't always needed because the high sunlight and DDW water is available.

Wild Game: the wild game meat further depletes the deuterium from the food and water.

Jeff Leach's Hasda and Masaai studies: looking at Jeff's papers, I believe that some of this data could be explored more to cross-correlate to the above environmental and geographical factors as well. Note: I am not advocating there must be D depletion completly, as the D in nature with the sun is designed for a reason, but with respect to treating people with poor Mito, it may be a more viable option for persons living in Africa and Europe instead of travelling to Mexico.

Population density: Those areas have a history of political instability and recent cival wars. Thereby, you won't find 5G there or 4G. Unless Musk plugs some satellites into the area. But for nnEMF damaged people, it is a worth while trip.

Observations to Wim Hof. Wim Hof the Ice Man, one of his favorite past time visiting places is to climb Mnt. Kilimanjaro. Compared to other mountain peaks of that height, it is the one place on earth that doesn't need prior training to climb it. In reviewing Wim's previous expeditions, it is only with Mnt. Kilimanjaro, I have read him being able to take people that are really really old, and or really ill, and they are able to climb the mountain in shorts and have greatly improved upon performance. From my observational research, this is greatly made possible by the combination of the factors above.
Not forgetting Wim Hof's Tumo breathing technique on oxygen tensions helps with the spin rates of the TCA, UREA and ATPhase.

Steps forward:
I have generated preliminary data and spatial GIS maps. However, now I need to make contact with the tourism boards, minister and travel agents to get assistance in gaining greater access to confirmation data and funds to travel and conduct from ground work research to confirm locations for visits.

Who knows, perhaps Jack's next new year's event may be in Africa?

Africa misses many of the effects the Yucatan does not.

**YUCATAN MITOCHONDRIAL BENEFITS THAT ARE UNSEEN:** An environment with a higher magnetic field strength offers human tissues a massive upgrade because nnEMF and blue light are not as effective as destroying the morphology of a cell because of the higher flux in the Yucatan (3.4 vs 0.4 milligauss) due to the crust being closer to the magnetic dynamo at the surface of the Earth in this area. Then there is the benefit of the KArst effect on the water trapped in the crust for the last 65 million years to create DDW in the cenote system where it normally would not be located based upon the latitude of the crater.

The science of grounding: the sun is a cathode ray who’s light hit earth which acts as the anode. Since it is the third anode from the sun this sets up its harmonic that determines basic morphogenetic process via photoacoustic cymatics. When a cathode ray hits an anode free electrons are liberated from the anode. This is why humans have sweat glands on their hands and feet. The human breast is also a modified human sweat gland for electron transfer between mother and infants mitochondria. Those free electrons are liberated according to the sunlight barcode Fraunhofer lines which vary. This is how light is used photoelectrically and photoacoustically to power life give it information to organize cells and to drive body plan to build out via cymatics by changing the light to sound wave which can be controlled magnetically by the Earth magnetic field. You must hire experts who know the basics of how life organizes to maintain health.

That magnetic flux in the Yucatan stabilizes stress cellular architecture and it also allows the ATPase to spin fast than it does in areas with a lowered gauss reading.

Magnetic fields control acoustic phonons. That research was done at Ohio State. Not only does it control morphogenesis it can also increase the heat a matrix liberate as the ATPase spins faster and it can actually control how the cell uses that heat properly so that heat is not wasted and thermalized. This is why cells can remain far from equilibrium in Mexico where the same people will struggle to do this in other locations they live in.
Magnetic flux control of heat and acoustic phonons: [https://news.osu.edu/landmark-study-proves-that-magnets-can-control-heat-and-sound/](https://news.osu.edu/landmark-study-proves-that-magnets-can-control-heat-and-sound/)

Cymatics show you how light waves are turned to sound to make things of specific size and shape to impart organization just by changing from light waves to sound. Magnetism controls the resultant sizes and shaped in cells. This is how light water and magnetism interacts in your cells.

### WarrenT — 2018-08-21T22:59:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-and-befuddled.21899/post-249029>

Thank you Dr. Kruse for your service to us all!

I’m so grateful to have stumbled on one of your Vermont talks on YT, thanks to the engineer who built the algorithm that plopped that vid in my queue. You Sir have begun to change my family’s health trajectory for the better!

I’m a noob to this site but super excited and motivated to plug in and learn. After just one of Dr Kruse’s interviews, my head is spinning and my biology degree felt like a waste of time. You’ve pulled together so many threads and uncovered the core truths which are often elegantly simple but with massive implications.

Just kinda sensing things nnEMFs aren’t quite right I’ve been searching and experimenting for years. EMFs have been on my radar ever since I had the “fortunate” experience of an acute reaction while sitting in a conference call with an Eero dual-band router a few feet behind me. Within 5 min, I felt hot, my hands tingled, and my head was foggy- I felt drugged! That experience validated my own efforts to minimize EMF exposure to me and my family although they still roll their eyes when I tell them no phones in their rooms at night and each must be on Airplane mode.

I work in IT and am surrounded by blue light, nnEMFs, etc. I’ve taken some steps to mitigate fields like grounding e-fields and turning off overhead fluorescent lights and have seen improvements to my energy levels. After reading books by dr Milham, Karl Riley, and Steven Magee, I hired a building biologist to sweep through our home and clean up wiring code violations, shield wifi router, etc. I’m experimenting with shutting off bedroom breakers at night and found I sleep much deeper and have vivid dreams.

A few months ago, I converted an old far-IR (EMF hell hole!) sauna into a clean near-IR lamp sauna and use in 30 min/day. Incredible! I lost 8 lbs (probably mostly water), raised energy, fall asleep quickly.

My family’s health is my main concern and from my own research and experimentation I’ve been trying to help them understand the risks of our nnEMFs. Being a dad with 4 teens I’m used to dissent and push-back but in these cases I usually just pull the “my way or the highway” card. With my wife, it’s a little different. She works as an OB/GYN and has to complete hours of charting a night, on her laptop. Drs are usually quite skeptical and demand data. Plus, breaking conventional wisdom is difficult for most and especially challenging for health care providers in practice for 19 years. I’ve provided her with loads of data/studies on EMF dangers but I know she hasn’t bought in 100% bc she still doesn’t use the laptop shield (now I know the really bad dude is the blue light). She wants to feel better but often deflects that she’s too busy etc. Without going into detail, let’s say she’s not as healthy as she’d like to be. I love her and want to grow old with her. Her health is my concern so I’m going to continue to fight for her by trying different avenues. She watched about 10 min of dr Kruse’s Vermont talk and she was entranced, and said she only understood about 25% of it. It’s a start.

I have my 23 and Me results and family health history but not sure what steps to take next. Any help would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you!

More melanopsin wisdom related to PD: The eye is where PD begins because this is where the dopamine deficit begins. The acute model of light-induced retinal degeneration uses short exposure to bright white loaded with 435-465nm blue light to study photoreceptor cell death leading to loss of vision and alteration of dopamine in the RPE. The mixture of high photon flux, altered oxygen tension and the high levels of liberated and oxidized polyunsaturated fatty acids present in rod outer segment membranes make rod photoreceptor cells especially vulnerable to photochemical damage. Although light affects rod photoreceptors primarily, cones seem to be more resilient surviving for a prolonged period of time after light exposure. The reason for this is the amount of retinol in rods is far greater in cones. Retinol cycling leads to toxic degeneration of Vitamin A derivatives that thin the retina in the area of the melanopsin photoreceptors. Cones eventually do die, but their death is secondary to the loss of rod cells and the effect of the toxic retinol damage. This occurs because of the weak covalent bond between melanopsin and retinol in diurnal mammals like humans. Endotoxins of the retinol cycle are released by degenerating rods, and with the lack of trophic and mechanical support after the apoptotic loss of rod cells or sudden chronic exposure of blue light to increased oxygen levels in the absence of rods have been implicated in the secondary cone cell death in the human retina. #melanopsinwisdom on display here folks. .[https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2018/08/180816114433.htm](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2018/08/180816114433.htm)

Transient electromagnetic fields (dirty electricity), in the kilohertz range on electrical wiring, may be contributing to elevated blood sugar levels among diabetics and pre-diabetics. By closely following plasma glucose levels in four Type 1 and Type 2 diabetics, we find that they responded directly to the amount of dirty electricity in their environment. In an electromagnetically clean environment, Type 1 diabetics require less insulin and Type 2 diabetics have lower levels of plasma glucose. Dirty electricity, generated by electronic equipment and wireless devices, is ubiquitous in the environment. Exercise on a treadmill, which produces dirty electricity, increases plasma glucose. These findings may explain why brittle diabetics have difficulty regulating blood sugar. Based on estimates of people who suffer from symptoms of electrical hypersensitivity (3-35%), as many as 5-60 million diabetics worldwide may be affected. Exposure to electromagnetic pollution in its various forms may account for higher plasma glucose levels and may contribute to the misdiagnosis of diabetes. Reducing exposure to electromagnetic pollution by avoidance or with specially designed GS filters may enable some diabetics to better regulate their blood sugar with less medication and borderline or pre-diabetics to remain non-diabetic longer." ### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-22T02:26:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-and-befuddled.21899/post-249063>

Another link of bone and muscle physiology to the melanopsin retinol mechanism. Blue light causes the circadian arrhythmia in the peripheral clock genes and bone and muscle. Researchers at Leiden University Medical Center in the Netherlands tracked the health of rats exposed to six months of continuous light compared to a control group of rats living under normal light-dark conditions — 12 hours of light, followed by 12 hours of dark.
During the study, the rats exposed to continuous light had less muscle strength and developed signs of early-stage osteoporosis. They also got fatter and had higher blood glucose levels. Several markers of immune system health also worsened, according to the report published in the medical journal Current Biology. [https://www.cell.com/current-biology/fulltext/S0960-9822(16)30497-3](https://www.cell.com/current-biology/fulltext/S0960-9822\(16\)30497-3)

Our brain can’t help but look at a series of facts — facts that often have incredibly complex cause and effect relationships — without forcing a simple, logical link between them. As a result, we mistakenly assign meaning where there is none. There is a clear danger in doing this. The more our stories diverge away from the reality at hand, the more likely we are to land ourselves in situations that are going to cause some sort of health harm or a behavioral conflict. Evolution, ultimately, doesn’t care for our thoughts and feelings, and it’s not afraid to punish us with health consequences if we refuse to get out of nature's way. On social media, you can see those people who look for "the sermon" in another reported suicide, for the social or moral lesson in the murder of a group in Chicago. We interpret what we see because of what we believe, as a result, we select the most workable situation for our cognitive bias of the multiple choices. We live entirely, especially if we are writers, by the imposition of a narrative line upon disparate images, by the “ideas” with which we have learned to freeze the shifting phantasmagoria which is our actual experience. This is made easier in a low dopamine state. This is why bullshit pervades modern life. We all live with an identity, fueled by a stream of memories, that is weaved together to create its own imaginary character, living in its own movie.

# Thread: Sea Girl Journal: zip code makes a difference
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sea-girl-journal-zip-code-makes-a-difference.21901/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sea Girl — 2018-08-22T07:52:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sea-girl-journal-zip-code-makes-a-difference.21901/post-249098>

Hi,

It’s my first blog here (my apologies if I make any mistakes as English is not my native language).

To begin with, I feel grateful for all the life challenges and health issues that I’ve had that eventually have brought me to JK blog and this forum. It’s great that there is this community of mito-minded people helping each other. I hope to find some answers here for myself.

I am 37, originally from Northern Europe, immigrated to Australia 10 years ago. We came here with my husband looking for a better work-life balance, safety and better environment to have kids. For the first 2 years we lived in Melbourne and had our first daughter there. Then we moved to the island which is located 150km south from Melbourne (38th longitude) and is connected to the mainland with the bridge. It seemed like a perfect place for us surrounded by the bay with sandy beaches on one side, ocean with waves on the other, only 10000 permanent residents (though lots of tourists, during the week-ends and summer months).

We built our dream house here – designed it ourselves and chose the most eco-friendly materials available. We wanted to have more kids, but unfortunately in 2012 we lost our second daughter in the car accident when a crazy drunk idiot went to the opposite side of the road (I was 7th months pregnant with her and her brain was severely injured in this head-on collision). Luckily 3 of us survived.

Since moving to the island I was always trying to make the healthiest choices: buying seasonal organic veggies and grass-fed meat, growing my own greens and herbs, eating fresh local fish (thanks to my husband’s hobby), spending hours on the beach, getting sun, swimming in the cold Southern ocean (mostly in the wetsuits though), doing yoga, meditation etc.

In 2015 I felt like we could try for another baby, but that winter I started to feel very tired. My ferritin level was super low, so all the doctors (allopathic and functional) recommended taking increasing amounts of iron supplements (which I diligently did for 3 years). At that time for about a month we were exposed to low-level petrol fumes which leaked from the garage into the house and after that I started having constant tension headaches. Even though we ventilated the house, removed all petrol sources from the garage and even changed the roof insulation, my headaches persisted. I thought I was reacting to something in the house as I felt worse inside, especially in hot weather and felt better when sleeping on the veranda or in any other place we went to (including Melbourne, Sydney, Perth and any rural areas).

Eventually, I persuaded my husband that we need to move to the other place. 4 months ago we rented the house 300 meters away from ours. That’s where the hell began – my head tension didn’t go away – in a few days I started having severe headaches plus heart palpitations. After falling asleep I would wake up as from an electric shock, my muscles burning and heart pounding. I realised what the real depression feels like - I was quite stressed before moving, but there I felt horrible both physically and mentally. There was no way I could possibly relax in that place (meditation / breathing did not work to a slightest degree). My husband and daughter we not doing well either (mostly emotionally).

This rented house was a double storey located on the hill, it had a nice view over the town, including 4 mobile towers. Looking outside with all my symptoms going I finally put 2 and 2 together. Our own house had a direct view to one of the towers 500 meters away. This rented one was a bit further from it (about 700 meters), but sitting on the hill it was directly radiated by the round dish on that high tower and by another 3 towers located about 2km away.

In 2 weeks I moved back to our old house from that nightmare (I still had had some headaches and felt tired, but at least I could sleep there and do my everyday chores). I bought Cornet ED88T meter, started reading all I could find about EMF biological effects, listened to all the podcasts from EMF Warriors and finally found JK site. The EMF series explained a lot to me, however I still have unanswered questions. **At this stage all I have - my health, my marriage, my daughter’s future and the possibility to have more kids - all depend on the finding the right answers.**

I started doing a lot of extra things to improve my Redox potential like getting direct sun light into the eyes first thing in the morning, taking sunbreaks during the day (unfortunately there’s not much sun in winter on the island), avoiding artificial blue light, minimizing my technology use (wasn’t really addicted), eating mostly ketogenic diet with extra seafood and seaweeds, drinking DD water, taking more magnesium, grounding as much as possible. Despite all these efforts I still feel crappy most of the time when I am at home and I feel much better after spending just half an hour near the ocean or a few hours away from the island in most other areas (that includes much more densely populated areas).

I stopped taking iron supplements after that rental home experience (as I felt really sharp liver pain when I took my usual 50mg of iron there). After a few months I have less headaches, but feel more and more tired. My ferritin dropped back to 6 (although with constant iron supplementation it never got more than 20), haemoglobin is borderline low. **Could it be that my body is not storing iron to make it less magnetic in the bad EMF environment? Should I still take any in supplemental form or just focus on iron rich foods (which I always did)?**

Back to the nnEMFs - our own house does not have high RF readings inside: around 0.05 mW/m2 in the front room (closest to the mobile tower) and only 0.005 in the bedroom. Wi-fi, mobile phones are off most of the time, nothing is on at night. The smart meter is on the garage wall, about 30 meters away from the bedroom. Cornet didn’t detect anything coming from the neighbours. LF inside the house vary from 0.02 to 0.06mG. We cut all the power for a few nights but it didn’t make any difference. The main suspect is that high mobile tower with the dish, however our brick house with steel roof seems to screen us quite well from the MHz frequencies coming from it. **Could the tower be emitting any other frequencies I am sensitive to that Cornet cannot pick up? What meter should I get to measure them? Could the nnEMFs on the island be more powerful in blocking the Schumann resonance and sunlight frequencies than those on the mainland?** My family does not want to move from the island, but after 3 years of constant pain and fatigue I don't have much hope to get back my heath here while still doing my best. **How can we find a better place to live where I won't be affected by the same nnEMFs as on the island?**

Sorry for the long story and thank you for reading it. Would be very grateful for any feedback and advice.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-22T16:35:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sea-girl-journal-zip-code-makes-a-difference.21901/post-249118>

Your environment sounds like it is loaded with nnEMF where you built.

I'd hire a building biologist with a 5G meter and them make a decision.

### Sea Girl — 2018-08-23T10:51:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sea-girl-journal-zip-code-makes-a-difference.21901/post-249177>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=249118)
>
> Your environment sounds like it is loaded with nnEMF where you built.
>
> I'd hire a building biologist with a 5G meter and them make a decision.

Thank you for your welcome and reply, Jack. I will try to find a building biologist with a 5G meter. I have talked to a few of them over the phone, but I had doubts about their qualification and equipment that they use. Maybe someone from Oz members could recommend a good electrical engineer in Victoria?

Since Cornet does not pick up anything major, I assume that the frequencies affecting my health are not within its measurement range: either less than 50Hz (ELFs?), between 10kHz and 100MHz or higher than 8GHz (5G).

The island is 40 sq. mi and has at least 20 large mobile towers – it must have a high level of nnEMFs. It seems like I get my headaches pretty much in every spot on the island being inside the houses or in the car (not so much outside). However, I feel fine in most other places, including on the 10th floor in Melbourne CBD and 22nd floor in metro Sydney (that should have plenty of nnEMFs). So there must be a specific range of frequencies I am reacting to since at least 2015.

# Thread: ARNP living is SW Fla

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2

### Natalie R. — 2018-08-22T21:07:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arnp-living-is-sw-fla.21905/post-249143>

Hi all. I am a huge fan of Dr. K. I have been listening to him for a few years.

I currently live in SW Florida and am fortunate enough to live on a canal with a dock. I get perfect sunrise/sunset views which I can sit on my dock and watch. I do not go into the water as it is brackish and has gators.

I work in allopathic medicine and find myself giving more holistic advice, which surprises many of my patients.

I have looked around for some answers to a few questions I have. I'm hoping someone can answer these.

1\. Because I live where I do, the houses all have lanai screens so we don't get eaten alive by bugs. Can I sit on my lanai and still get the full spectrum of sun?

2\. If I'm in the shade, gardening for instance, am I getting enough benefit?

3\. I drink mostly spring water. Knowing that our mitochondria make water, is there any certain minimum amount we need to drink if our redox is satisfactory?

Thanks everyone!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-23T04:01:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arnp-living-is-sw-fla.21905/post-249168>

1\. yes
2\. benefit yes, enough unknown without context.
3\. depends upon heteroplasmy rate and BUN/creat ration and blue light hazard.

Welcome

# Thread: Warren's journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warrens-journal.21907/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### WarrenT — 2018-08-23T02:17:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warrens-journal.21907/post-249165>

Hello!

I feel grateful to have found Dr. Kruse and this community!

I’m slogging through all of this stunning information as fast as I possibly can while trying to remember to feed my kids.

Me: 50 y.o., 6’2”, 215lbs. Born in So. Cal, raised in No. Cal (Sac area).

Work: I work in an IT role; networks and application administrator.

Health: Generally good. Pre-diabetic. A bit doughy. I'm a whitey European mutt

Childhood: Grew up in the lucky generation where playing outside all day was the norm. Two brothers, loving intact family. Dad was a teacher, mom a nurse.

Played a variety of sports…incl water polo, swimming, and rowing in college. In the sun a lot while lifeguarding, swim instructor.

Major Injuries/illnesses: Dx with mild ulcerative colitis and lasted about 4 yrs, 1982-86. Torn medial meniscus on both knees 10 years apart while running and snowboarding, 1986/96. In 2005, severe lumbar muscle strain and L3/4 L4/5 disk herniation after get this, bending over to lift a tiny little reagent bottle at work. Cause: Over 3 years of a daily 90 min commute to the Bay area shredded my back. Fortunately, I was able to avoid fusion and instead focused on PT and am almost totally recovered except no more running or basketball and very uncomfortable to sit for periods.

Family health: Mom passed in 2004 at 61 due to idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. My mom’s sister passed at 65 with same underlying cause. Neither smoked, one lived in LA and one in Sacramento. Both were otherwise generally healthy. Within past year, my older brother, 53, started having persistent non-productive cough and shortness of breath. Tests show early dx is interstitial lung disease. He’s in Colorado Springs. This has my younger brother and I concerned since I’ve read of possible genetic link with IPF across multiple generations. I currently don’t have any symptoms. My grandmother was a smoker and lived in Mexico City during the last decade of her life. Primary cause of death due to emphysema.

My first wife passed at age 38 due to sub-arachnoid hemorrhage 4 weeks after my daughter was born and my son 5. Neurosurgeons said there most likely was no connection to the labor of birth and she might have had a weakened arterial wall for years. She was in outstanding health and only complained of a headache a few days prior.

Interesting twist highlighting that there are no coincidences in life. Someone upstairs was watching over us because, a year later, after very strange circumstances and timing, I met my current wife on-line. It turns out this wonderful woman was my first wife’s OB/GYN. So now we have a big blended family (circus) with 4 kids. Very blessed! These experiences have certainly knocked a few screws loose but also made me hyper-aware of the frailty of life and contributed to my drive to try to make the most of the time here by trying to maximize the quality of life for me and my family. And this has led me here!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-23T04:13:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warrens-journal.21907/post-249171>

welcome. I would disagree with the neurosurgeons about the labor and the rupture.

### JanSz — 2018-08-23T04:46:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warrens-journal.21907/post-249172>

Get your fasting insulin to low single numbers.

### WarrenT — 2018-08-24T21:16:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warrens-journal.21907/post-249310>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=249171)
>
> welcome. I would disagree with the neurosurgeons about the labor and the rupture.

Yes Sir! I would be very interested in your professional take on possible causes of her aneurysm. I second-guessed everything, racked my brain for a couple of years. I know we can't get MRIs after a couple days of headaches. We were told there was absolutely nothing we could've done differently. In 2005/6 she did work in office environment on computer, at home we had a dect phone and flip phones. No wifi yet. I wonder if she was the canary in the coal mine...
Thank you JanSz! My wife and I are getting our labs done soon.

### WarrenT — 2018-08-24T21:25:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warrens-journal.21907/post-249312>

Update:
Colorado Springs- My brother's CT scan confirmed he has IPF damage in lower lobes. More tests to determine which type of IPF and treatment options. Resting O2 is 85%, starting supplemental O2.
I'm trying to learn what his typical day looks like and work and home...levels of blue light exposure, emfs, diet, etc.

### caroline — 2018-08-25T12:16:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warrens-journal.21907/post-249353>

Hi Warren and welcome.

If you can manage it ..... become a Gold member and you will have access to all the monthly webinars and a follow up live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything. The last one went for over 3 hours. The June Q&A went for over 4 hours!

I love your story BTW.

### Inna — 2018-08-26T04:20:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warrens-journal.21907/post-249423>

Warren, welcome!
Sorry to hear about your brother, ipf is a nasty disease and usually progresses over several years. Existing treatments are usually just marginally effective. From what Jack said about Colorado, it is a place to avoid from health perspective...
It sound like ipf might be familial in your family and you and your younger brother might be at risk.
Short telomeres are sometimes associated with ipf:

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0027510711002892](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0027510711002892)

[https://www.atsjournals.org/doi/abs/10.1164/ajrccm-conference.2016.193.1\_MeetingAbstracts.A5106](https://www.atsjournals.org/doi/abs/10.1164/ajrccm-conference.2016.193.1_MeetingAbstracts.A5106)

You might consider genetic studies (23 and me) and perhaps telomere length to assess your risk. Though I am not a big fan of testing, in your situation it might be indicated.

That your grandmother lived to old age in Mexico might be a clue that high quantum yield environment might help to mitigate the risk.

Best wishes to you and your lovely family!

Thank you Caroline! I will definitely consider bumping up to Gold.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/double-lenses-from-cataract-surgery-lost-cause-please-help.21909/post-249175>

Hello!
I've been type 1 diabetic for 28 years now and have torn both retinas and had my lenses replaced after the cataracts formed following attachment surgery.
From what I'm learning about the eyes and how the UV spectrum is needed in all these processes , am I lost cause situation?

I found a tanning salon that offers red light beds, I switched my water to spring water, I'm switching my diet to a deuterium depleted one and lots of DHA , I ordered blue blocking glasses and phone filter, I ordered different light bulbs and shutting my wifi off at night and making sure I get outside a lot more.
Will all this be enough or do I need to do more?
I've only been researching this for 3 days so forgive my lack of knowledge but I really want to learn and get healthier. I feel like if I don't get the best known knowledge on this, my life will just keep deteriorating.

I live in Torrance, CA/ Los Angeles . I am O+ blood type .
My Mother is A+. I need to order my 23 and me test.
My mother has no thyroid and had deep vein thrombosis .

I look forward to your replies!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/double-lenses-from-cataract-surgery-lost-cause-please-help.21909/post-249239>

just use Qlarivia as a HALF of your daily water intake, and you should be fine (or at least much better off).
..

Preventa--->have medical pedigree, (can be used in published research)
Qlarivia---->does not
..
I think it is obvious
that you would do your best dealing with
Circadian Rhythm (very first priority, regardless of your location).
\==================================
Half Qlarivia plus the other half of tap water makes **87.5ppm
**
According to dr Boros
we want to have in body fluid (90-120)ppm

I think that using 87.5ppm DDW you should be able to get under 120ppm in body fluids (I got close to it).
But with your diabetes (1) you may need to to go lower.

Anyhow,
using some DDW is better than no DDW.

Fats are low in deuterium, and do not raise need for insulin.
Eat lots of fats.
Lots of fats in diet (I think) starts with 50% calories from fats in daily food and go up.
Then you have a carbs that do not raise insulin, cabbage, broccoli, cauliflower
also

pick from top 40 or so (and black pepper.

Sep 28, 1984 (Age: 33)
type 1 diabetic for 28 years

I know what you feel
I am alone too. Fiance left me a year ago with another woman because he could not accept how different I live. Even if we still loved each other. I did forbid him putting on lights! And having his smartphone by the bedside...
The new woman is mainstream, and lives like the most. But he is not happy. Still wants to talk to me.. and weirdly, misses what we had. He tells me he feels so empty, and tired.

Now I just learn to live in the present.. at least I am free to do whatever I want

Being present.
Somehow I learn a ton through that.. going through the pain and loneliness and all
Makes me really grateful. I guess there is a diamond hidden in every pain, if we just face it, and do not run away. But man, sometimes it do hurts like hell
And more often, life is just.. bliss

I work from home so it's easy for me to get outside. I even open up the window that let's in the sun and has a big enough space for me on the floor to sunbathe nude and I also got a cat stroller to take my cat out for walks and exercise .
Getting enough sun is not difficult for me, it's staying away from wifi and blue light outside of my house that is a problem. I feel like I need to be in an aluminum bubble suit lol.
I had to go to the hospital top see my diabetes doctor and asked if he could shut the light off and just use the hallway light to see and he asked me why and I just told him it made feel sick because he couldn't handle the truth. He looked at me like I was a weirdo.
I already got kicked out of the diabetes clinic for not cooperating with my last doctor . They wanted me on blood pressure pills for my kidneys and cholesterol pills and vaccines and I refused them all.
I can't get kicked out of my primary care doctor that I have now because I need my land and insulin . They can't possibly accept that they went to years of school and you come along telling them they're wrong . They see you and just another patient that doesn't know anything. They surely don't have many or any other patients like me that question them

I also live next to a marsh preserve and there's coyotes so it's best he's inside the stroller because I can't fight off things while trying to hang onto my cat and get all scratched up..

Cats are resourceful and resilient. Hope yours actually has a chance to get paws on earth. I once had a rescue that was front declawed by previous person, and I think suffered some serious crazy because of it. Is the place in Costa Rica off the beaten path a bit? Think it will be good for you no matter what.

# Thread: Does natural sunlight outweight hf-radiation exposure?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-natural-sunlight-outweight-hf-radiation-exposure.21913/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2018-08-24T12:45:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-natural-sunlight-outweight-hf-radiation-exposure.21913/post-249263>

So since Im measuring more often, I made the experience that you often have some kind of hf-radiation where you have a place to get sunlight.

The question is:

Is the exposure to natural sunlight outweighing the effects of nnEMF (HF) at this place?
Or is it nonsense because you get dehydrated?
Is is dependent on the peak intensity?

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-24T18:45:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-natural-sunlight-outweight-hf-radiation-exposure.21913/post-249297>

I'm sure this is a definitive _Dr Kruse question_, but I will weigh in.

Sure, sunlight will help you immensely in adverse toxic environments, but it is not a magic bullet that will on balance give you superhero invulnerability to nnEMF exposure effects . You would only be deluding yourself to believe that.

5G reality is destined to destroy a lot of peoples' illusions that they can still coexist safely with a pulsed microwave RF-saturated environment if only they do everything right with sunlight, diet, exercise, breathing exercises, and what have you.

There are still some places where you can be at peace with nature, safely grounded and linked to the sun, without too much nnEMF beating you up at the same time.

But time may be running out for those safe havens. Who knows?

To paraphrase a quote attributed to Einstein--

_"Two things are infinite: the universe and human stupidity."_

### Antonis — 2018-08-24T19:05:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-natural-sunlight-outweight-hf-radiation-exposure.21913/post-249301>

### Inger — 2018-08-26T10:58:23+0300

I think of it as non polarized light, and how we need a ton of that! Because it carries information that makes order in our bodies. So I try to be in touch with that light whenever I can and as much skin as I can.

With many of toxins/stressors we face today we often have a choice on whether to be willingly exposed-- for example smoking, denatured and glyphosate laden foods, chlorine/fluoride in water, light pollution, and so on.

Great balls of fire, what a mess we have created.

# Thread: Light was the missing ingredient
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-was-the-missing-ingredient.21920/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mary B — 2018-08-24T19:09:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-was-the-missing-ingredient.21920/post-249303>

So in 2016 I found Wahl's protocol. Dropped the snacking and turned to meat, vegetables and a little fruit. Then I discovered grounding with amazing results to my plantar fasciitis. Then I headed back to my carb heavy snacking ways. In January of this year I found Dr. Fung and with intermittent fasting on a 1 meal 3 meal weekly routine I dropped off 35 lbs by June. Then I discovered what was missing from Dr. Kruse. I have dropped about 8 more lbs eating 2 meals a day and am at the right weight. My carb cravings are gone. I cant believe how fantastic it is to be out in the sun and blocking the indoors blue light. I am so drawn to be out in the sun now. Worried about winter in Ohio. Must make a plan to get to the sun. I am so grateful for all the time Dr. Kruse has spent making this blog. I so want my children and grand children to understand all of this but it isn't easy. I tell people about it and they cant quite believe it. Love the books Epi-Paleo RX, Body Electric and the online talks (wearing my Uvex glasses) about mitocondria and 4 phases of water. Thank you!

Yes Im listening to one for the 2nd time while I clean this afternoon. Ive gotten brave enough to sit out in my kiniki suit in the AM. Neighbors probablyk think Im nuts cause I'm out there at the start of day cold or hot, rain or shine. Cant talk my friends into listening to any of this. Working on my water deuterium issue now. Just cant believe the change morning sun made!

### Mary B — 2018-08-26T01:54:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-was-the-missing-ingredient.21920/post-249417>

I’ve really changed my views on sunlight. I blamed it for my blue eyed husbands death from ocular melanoma. He loved being in the sun with work and pleasure. Reading your blogs I see that his contact lenses were a problem. He had keratoconus and was told he needed hard contacts. Our MD Anderson researcher said it is blue and green eyed people who get it. Now I wonder if they ever checked to see if contact lenses were a common denominator among his patients. And my poor man had a carb addict wife. Yipes. So many reasons people need to wake up to all that you have compiled for us.

# Thread: From Near Death to Here
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-near-death-to-here.21924/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jason Gill — 2018-08-25T07:53:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-near-death-to-here.21924/post-249343>

A quick backstory. I started off as a welder who never shielded my skin then ran CNC machines in a factory for around 10 years. Lots of injuries in those years mostly torn ligaments/tendons in my knees and elbows. Developed low-T and sleep apnea in my late twenties. Graduated Nursing school in 2013 and took a position in the ER. Stressed and fatigued all the time. I would usually just come home from work, sit down in front of the t.v. on the sofa, lay my laptop in my lap and spend hours wasting my life away. Had horrible leg cramps which I mostly contributed to working in the ER and years of factory work. On January 30, 2017 I went in to work. I had been unusually tired for the last couple of days but this particular day was really bad. I had been on my shift around 15 min before I filled a toilet full of blood and had to check myself in. An hour later I collapsed from blood loss. My normal H&H is around 18&50 but this day I hit 4&12. Had a blood transfusion and every radiology/nuc med test that we offered, spent a couple days in ICU before getting to the floor for a week. developed phlebitis while I was inpatient that turned into a full blown PE about a week after discharge. Another week in the hospital. Spent 6 months on a blood thinner last year after the GI bleed. No answers from testing, lab work or surgeons. Im skipping alot about being an uptight asshole during this time. Last fall I started keto and lost around 25 lbs from Nov to Jan. I felt a lot better but knew this wasn't the answer by itself. I was still having horrible muscle cramps and right flank pain from the PE even 9 months later. Continued searching on my own as I the advice I was getting from Dr's at work just didn't make any sense to me. Found Dr Kruse around the end of April. I admit that I have been struggling to learn the science but I am staying dedicated. In may I started limiting tv, Im about an hour a week now. Go to bed and get up with the sun, except for tonight because I have to do this. Have got at least an hour of morning sun, two hours mid-day sun and one to two hours of evening sun 4 days a week. On the days I work the sun doesn't rise until I am already at work but I have still been getting a good 30 min to an hour of sunset. Haven't been grounding at home because we have underground utilities here and I don't know enough about it to know what is a safe distance. I have went through the CT protocols. I face dunked for 2 weeks followed by torso down submersion for a week. Was experiencing increased leg cramping after CT so I switched to hot water in tub with ice bags on my torso for an hour. I have next to no fat from waist down. Been using 50% blue blockers at work and uvex at home. Lots of Lake time this summer as well. The last few months muscle cramping has completely subsided, H&H back within normal range. A year of iron supplementation did nothing for me. This last month on my off days we have had lots of clouds/thunderstorms. Still out when its not raining but I can tell a difference. Hitting the tanning bed on rainy days. I started the Family Nurse Practitioner program this week, so I am going to be increasing my PC use by a huge factor. I have a few of questions. With the increased PC usage I have considered getting a lead apron. It may sound like over-kill but as of now I am convinced that it contributed to my gi bleed. Would an apron be viable or are there better alternatives I haven't thought of. And last my maternal haplogroup is U6a1a, I'm currently living in NE Arkansas drinking my Mountain Valley Spring Water everyday, is the 36 Lat doable for me. Thanks for replies in advance
