# Thread: Insane paper on the relationship between fire, brain volume, and food intake
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insane-paper-on-the-relationship-between-fire-brain-volume-and-food-intake.21925/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-08-25T13:23:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insane-paper-on-the-relationship-between-fire-brain-volume-and-food-intake.21925/post-249354>

Contrary to many previous posts in this forum, I'm going to make some cites from the full text of this paper (emphases are mine).

First of all, the authors inquire about what could have caused an increase in hominin brain size - is that cooking food ?

"Nowadays, human brain expansion during evolution has been acknowledged to explain our empowered cognitive capabilities. **The drivers for such accelerated expansion remain, however, largely unknown. In this sense, studies have suggested that the cooking of food could be a pre-requisite for the expansion of brain size in early hominins.** However, this appealing hypothesis is only supported by a mathematical model suggesting that the increasing number of neurons in the brain would constrain body size among primates due to a limited amount of calories obtained from diets. Here, we show, by using a similar mathematical model, that a tradeoff between body mass and the number of brain neurons imposed by dietary constraints during hominin evolution is unlikely."​

Further down the road of this paper, the authors will question the presupposition that cooking was necessary to increase hominin brain size. Nevertheless, _our brain size_ _does need to be explained:
_

"Based on such “direct” and “indirect” evidence, many different theories have been proposed to explain the disproportionate growth of the brain in the human lineage, considering the high energetic cost of larger brains."
​

Why do brain sized need to be explained? Large brains consume lots of energy. To be more precise:

"Recent work suggests that the energetic cost of the brain is proportional to its number of neurons. Hence, increasing the number of neurons during primate evolution would impose a metabolic constraint to the body and brain sizes"​

The main thesis of the authors is that fire creation in hominins only became _widespread_ about 800,000 - 400,000 years ago, while brain-size expansion started more than a million years earlier:

"In this work, we revisit the correlations between brain size and cooking by means of archeological and neuroanatomical data, as well as new metabolic data on the energetic content of raw and cooked meat. **We show that large primate encephalization was reached millions of years before the widespread control of fire, a pre-requisite for cooking, and provide evidence indicating that early hominins were likely to obtain enough calories from raw meat to afford for the size of their brains**."
​

In the picture below, you can see the size of hominin brains plotted against the strength of the evidence for _widespread_ fire-use:
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Locations, where fire was used, are supplied in part C of that picture.

Furthermore, you can view the relationship between the number of brain neurons in relation to our ancestor hominins species such as the _homo Erectus_ below:

(Observe that the _homo Erectus_ already has a massively increased brain size compared to a gorilla (the scale is logarithmic), even though it lived 1.8 million to 200,000 years ago.)

Now, there's a second argument that enters the picture: does cooking increase calorie availability in the first place? The authors claim that:

"We observed a similar weight variation among animals fed with either cooked or raw meat (Figure [(Figure4A).4A](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/figure/F4/)). Interestingly, animals fed with cooked meat ingested, on the average, more meat than animals fed with raw meat (Figure [(Figure4B).4B](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/figure/F4/)). In both groups, ingestion of meat was lower on the first day, likely due to the adaptation to a new diet. As a consequence, animals from both groups lost around 2 g on this first day of the diet, but afterward their weight stabilized (Figure [(Figure4C).4C](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/figure/F4/)). **Finally, we correlated the weight variation and meat consumption for each animal (Figure [(Figure4D)4D](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/figure/F4/)) and observed that, for a given value of weight variation, animals fed with cooked meat required a higher ingestion of food than animals fed with raw meat, probably reflecting the loss of fat caused by thermal processing (Sheard et al., [1998](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/#B59)). Thus, our data indicate that energetic gain in a diet based exclusively on raw meat is similar to, or even higher than, a diet of cooked meat**."​

Cooking meat may thus _reduce_ nutrient availability.

Next, there's the relationship between hours of foraging (or food-seeking) and efficiency therein:

As you can see, once you can get food that has a higher calorie density quicker, the time you need to spend on finding food each day goes down dramatically. Foraging itself is energy intensive, so a doubling of your efficiency will lead to more than double the net calorie reward.

So, what's the take-home message?

The authors conclude with the following:

"Therefore, in this study, we favor the most classical view suggesting that early hominins increased their foraging efficiency by including new sources of food in their diet, especially seeds and meat (Milton, [1999](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/#B48); Richards, [2002](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/#B54)). Also, the use of tools is well documented (Keeley and Toth, [1981](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/#B34); Domínguez-Rodrigo et al., [2005](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/#B19)) and strongly correlates with the increase in brain size during human evolution (Klein, [1977](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/#B37); Holloway, [1997](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/#B30)). Indeed, recent evidence indicates that the use of Lower Paleolithic technologies to process meat and tubers reduced the number of chewing cycles and the total masticatory force (Zink and Lieberman, [2016](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/#B73)), explaining the increased foraging efficiency of early hominins at the onset of brain expansion. Therefore, the rise in the use of tools, rather than cooking, is more likely to explain how early hominins increased their daily energetic intake. "​

(Disclaimer: the argument of the authors I've displayed above in no way reflects my personal position. I've posted the work mainly as a provocation...)

Link:

[https://www.assumptionnih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/](https://www.assumptionnih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4842772/)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-25T14:03:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insane-paper-on-the-relationship-between-fire-brain-volume-and-food-intake.21925/post-249355>

This paper does not do anything to the ideas on this site at all. That EMF2 blog post contains the data you do not understand well.

Anything that increases in size means that organ loses energy or mass. Recall from E=mc2 that mass and energy are the same currency. This is why it is called MASS EQUIVALENCE.

So cooking lowers the biophoton content of food.......this means it contains **LESS** biophoton energy, not more. So fire augments autophagy via a fasting effect since it lowers food quality. The quantum biologist knows that this is another way of saying less biophoton energy means less mass. Fritz Popp showed in all his experiments with photomultipliers that cooked food lost its biophoton amount.

Neanderthals had 125 grams **MORE** brain mass than humans and this can be fact checked in Micheal Crawford and Stephan Cunnane book on the Human Brain Evolution. This is well documented. So the human brain **SHRANK** from earlier forms of hominids. The size of the brain was not the ADVANTAGE in evolution considering the Neaderthals elevated CMRO2. The higher the CMRO2 the higher the metabolic rate and the more this tissues costed its owner because they had to support its existence. As a result, this not only means that brain cost the Neanderthal heart more work, but it stressed the female pelvis and the size of the birth canal of hominids. Having a smaller brain that operates better is actually is a better advantage

It was the physiologic functioning of the smaller brain that was. That is the story of how more DHA in the diet with less food under the power of the sun makes you really understand the power of DHA the Bazan effect in humans.

What does physics say about size Bart? Anything that gets larger has more energy/redox, not more. Anything that gets larger loses energy. Do not believe it? What happens to the hear when it fails? It gets LARGER. What happens to your ankle when you sprain it? It gets larger. What happens to a star as it dies? It gets larger. These are scaling laws in nature that are UNIVERSAL Bart. Neanderthals brains were larger than ours.

When things shrink the gain energy according to E=mc^2.

Lesson over.

This story is not contrary to things here. It is contrary to your understanding of things here because you have an incomplete understanding of what I have said. You are not a member and you have not read all the blogs or heard any of the webinars or Q & A's. So when someone with an incomplete set of data makes the provacative comment you did, you get shown how the Dunning Kruger effect persists in humans.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-25T14:15:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insane-paper-on-the-relationship-between-fire-brain-volume-and-food-intake.21925/post-249356>

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-08-25T16:09:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insane-paper-on-the-relationship-between-fire-brain-volume-and-food-intake.21925/post-249369>

I know the argument I posted is not contrary to your position Jack. I used the word "insane" in my title description because the outcome is interesting in relation to what you've written in the past.

I fact, I posted the argument - among others - because it _supports_ your position. One example is the thesis on raw meat supports your Brain Gut series on swallowing food raw. That principle is rarely discussed (and criticized) because I hear everyone talk about how you need to chew food properly. Another way in which the post supports your position is because of the movement towards meats (and DHA of course) instead of foods collected in woods (such as Gorillas and Chimpanzees do) would be a transition towards lower-Deuterium foods.

A transition towards low Deuterium foods - such as meat - might provide an explanation about the brain development of hominins - when fire can no longer explain that fact.

On the link between brain size: I do think that the brain size to body weight ratio is lower in Neanderthals than in humans. In absolute terms, yes, Neanderthalers have bigger brains -- but Neanderthalers also _heavier_ than human beings.

There's another interesting link here:

"The late archaic humans (Neanderthals) sourced protein predominantly from the red meat of wolves, large felids and hyenas. There was no evidence of fresh water aquatic species or marine sources of protein in the bone collagen of the Neanderthal samples. In striking contrast, seafood consumption was a “staple” of the diet of the early modern humans (Mid-Upper Paleolithic period). Depending upon geographical region, freshwater or marine sources of protein constituted between 10-50% of the diet for the early modern humans. Freshwater sources occurred along rivers and included fish and/or water fowl and marine sources were coastal and included fish, shellfish and small slow-moving animals such as turtles/tortoises. When the radio isotope values were aggregated, the between-group difference in the values was statistically significant (_p_ = 0.005)."​

Source: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257695/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257695/)

And yet, hominins did consume DHA 2 million years ago already:

"Recent information on excavations at the 1.95 million-year-old Oldowan site of FwJJ20 in the East Turkana Basin of northern Kenya, published in PNAS, helps fill the void, and research shows that at least some early hominins, enjoyed a varied diet, including aquatic species that were typical of the well-watered surroundings of the site ([7](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2890732/#r7)). This work highlights the opportunistic nature of early hominin foraging and the importance of sampling as many paleo-habitats as possible as well as the need for thorough analyses of all excavated animal remains."​

Source: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2890732/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2890732/)

So homonins consumed DHA, while Neanderthalers stopped doing so in time.

On DHA in general: contrary to Ray Peat, I'm very much convinced that DHA is important. Already have a blog post planned on that topic, which will include Crawford's work.

Additionally, I've included a disclaimer saying that the argument laid out above is not necessarily my own, because I'm still forming that argument. I posted what I posted above because I thought it would be interesting to have a discussion around, and hence, the word "provocative".

On membership: I've actually been a ($5) Patreon member of yours for a few weeks. I've been reading up on deuterium, and have been researching whether an increased reliance on fatty acids (combined with remaining in a high quantum-yield environment) may have driven these brain changes in hominins. On hearing all the webinars plus Q&A: yes, agree, I've not heard all that yet.

# Thread: All things pregnancy

### LieselK — 2018-08-25T15:05:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-pregnancy.21926/post-249362>

Newly pregnant with my 4th and I am getting all kinds of questions: Can you do CT? What about eating carbs (don't you need them)? Shouldn't you take a prenatal?

Here's what I know... I feel amazing. You wouldn't even know I was pregnant except for that little belly bulge (previous pregnancies I've been nauseous.) I've only been doing light CT all summer- cold hose water in the trough in the heat of the day to cool off. Summer is the time for carbs so I guess I eat more of them right now, but winter is coming and why would I need so many carbs just because I'm growing a baby? I still take Vitamin D and a thyroid supplement plus I added Life Extension folate. (Consequently, I naturally front-loaded folate pre-pregnancy by following Epi-Paleo anyway.)

Anyone been pregnant and Epi-Paleo or know of anyone who is? As much as growing a human being IS a big deal, why is it such a big deal to everyone else in your life?! It's like I'm surrounded mb skeptics because I don't crave Chipotle and ice cream.

Does the optimal epi-paleo woman need additional supplementation? I say, no. In a less optimal environment such as my wintertime, what do I do to further optimize it for pregnancy and breastfeeding (since Vit D in breastmilk is dependent on exposure in pregnancy for instance?)

# Thread: Can one get EHS before the advent of the light bulb?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-get-ehs-before-the-advent-of-the-light-bulb.21927/post-249414>

The light bulb became reality in 1874. The power grid was born in 1893. But when did the history of electromagnetic hypersensitivity show up in humans? It showed up as soon as humans invented the telegraph. Back in the mid to late 1800's no one walked around naked making enough Vitamin D (70ng/dl for EHS from sun). In fact, they were heavily clothed which lowered their redox and Vitamin D levels. When you added in the Telegraph we began to see SICKNESS manifest and no one seems to know it. nnEMF was a telegraph issue as well. How do we know this? French Physician, Dr. Ernest Onimus, treated Telegraph Operators in Paris during the 1870s, who were continuously exposed to electric and alien magnetic fields, as suffering from heart palpitations, dizziness, insomnia, poor eyesight, headaches, exhaustion, depression, memory loss & mental illness. Black Swans know this story. If you don't you might want to consider becoming one. Most people think nothing could possibly happen prior to the advent of the light bulb. They are not wise or deep thinkers. The Carrington event happened in 1859 and killed several telegraph operators via jump conduction. That is what a CME can do to via a conductor/insulator. Just wait til 5G. That is what it can do as well. None of you know what is coming......Black Swans do.

### thrive1992 — 2018-08-26T06:32:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-to-optimal-health.21930/post-249437>

Hi team
I have been following Jack Kruse for about 1.5 years at this stage. I have been blocking blue light with Uvex Skyper since 2014 but back then I thought they only needed to be worn for 30 to 45 minutes before sleep. Now I wear them as soon as the sun is down and minimise all artificial light.
I have actually searched through this forum quite a lot as a nonmember. I know all the basic information and some of the more advanced information as well. I was living in Sydney for almost a year working in the tech industry (I wanted to move somewhere with more sun and so I could do the pursue a hobby) but I decided to quit and now I am training to be a beekeeper and studying organic farming at university back in New Zealand. I think this will allow me to have an income without having to sacrifice my health in the future.

Are there any other New Zealanders here??

Here is a brief overview of my health problems:
ADHD, supposedly had "depression" during 2014 but it was probably just blue light toxicity, cold hands and feet (not sure if I have raynauds or if it's just poor blood circulation or some other blood related issue), possibly some gut health issues (BM are not as consistent as I would like), I have an odd small bone growth on the side of 1 of my fingers which could be a ganglion, since increasing sun exposure and red light therapy (JOOVV and redlightman) I have noticed an increase in freckles on parts of my body (I have no proof that this is because of the increase sun exposure but I have definitely noticed it over the last year ) maybe there is something toxic in my blood at the moment, I have some clicky joints/possibly low-level arthritis, gadolinium toxicity due to the fact that they used the gadolinium dye in my left hip for an MRI 7 years ago, pectus excavatum, seems like I have a weak connective tissue as well so possibly Marfan syndrome,

here is a brief overview of the things I do to improve my health:
pitch black bedroom, Wi-Fi router off, no gadgets in bedroom, we live at the end of a no exit Street with farmland and forest bordering 3 sides of the property so very low wi-fry, at this stage there is no 5G in Christchurch, reverse osmosis drinking water, shower water is still regular tap water, 1st thing in the morning I spend 5 minutes outside in natural light grounding, DHA consumption – seafood, organic eggs and meat, currently eating minimal grains and no dairy – I might try ghee soon, sweet potatoes often kumera, apples and pears often, fruit and vegetable is organic when possible, I do fasting but it's not same amount every day, in 2 days I will be having a consultation with a biological dentist to get information on removing my amalgam fillings which I have approximately 4 of, SOTA blood cleaner and water ozonator, I'm also going to start creating my own magnesium bicarbonate soon, I'm also working on my mouth and tongue posture after learning about Dr Mike Mew and Sarah Hornsby last year.

I am thinking of getting the magnetico 10 Gauss but I've read so many stories of people having a difficult time with that and you also see a lot of people selling secondhand on Facebook so I'm a little bit unsure of whether it's a good thing to spend money on or not.
Also considering getting a low level air filter for my bedroom.

Considering getting some testing done for what toxins i have in my system.

Also about to start using boron/borax foot soaks for my joint pain.

Would love to hear from anyone, I plan to become an active contributor to this Forum

Researchers fine tune how Gadolinium causes its problems as it passes through the brain's water networks to cause the symptoms associated with this transition metal.
In a study published online on 25 November by Investigative Radiology, Dr. Katerina Deike-Hofmann and colleagues from the German Cancer Research Center (DKFZ) at Heidelberg University Hospital and Essen University Hospital explain how gadolinium enters the cerebral spinal fluid (CSF). They show that GBCAs are excreted through specific entry points, such as the choroid plexus and the anterior chamber of the eye, into the CSF and subsequently enter the glymphatic system that, for instance, surrounds the perineural space. ### ElectricUniverse — 2018-12-06T20:59:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-to-optimal-health.21930/post-257419>

> [Sajid Mahmood said:](/goto/post?id=257332)
>
> If a fluorescent light breaks, does it release toxic mercury vapor into the atmosphere?

Yes, breakage of fluorescent lights and compact fluorescent lamps (CFL) in your house should be treated very seriously as a mini-hazmat contamination event.

Both mercury vapor and mercury-containing debris are released when one of these lamps break. If it breaks on a carpet that is even worse (that area of carpet should be discarded safely).

I accidently broke a CFL on a forced air furnace intake vent (it was on floor). What a nightmare. I got rid of every CFL after that.

There is info online about how to safely remediate a mercury-containing lamp breakage event in your home. Follow them to the letter. I'd advise removing every CFL or fluorescent lamp immediately from your dwelling. Discard them safely and responsibly so you don't contaminate the environment.

Good luck

About CFL light bulbs.
My daughter have a house down the street of me.

It started from a light-bulb as on the picture.

I have assumed that this type of bulbs would be banned from use.
That bulb started fire without being supplied with electricity for a good week.

Molten plastic fell on the soft chair that stand next to air register.
The moving air helped in igniting fire and spread the soot over the whole house.

# Thread: What should i check before purchasing a Magnetico???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-should-i-check-before-purchasing-a-magnetico.21931/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thrive1992 — 2018-08-26T06:43:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-should-i-check-before-purchasing-a-magnetico.21931/post-249438>

My bedroom is on ground floor, low nnEMF only 1 neighbour wifi signal, bed is spring coil unfortunately (Jack wrote a comment saying Spring is bad for magnetico), bed frame is wooden.
Choice is between 5Gauss or 10Gauss. I live in semi rural New Zealand so 5G technology isn't coming here in the next 2 years.
I have 2 amalgam fillings - seeing a biodentist and will be getting all replaced with porcelain ceramic (any ideas on best material to replace them with in a nnEMF world???). Amalgam fillings probably mean i have heavy metal toxicity in my body such as mercury. This means probable detox symptoms on Magnetico.
I am on filtered water, supplementing magnesium and consuming seafood (for DHA) daily. Also take lugols iodine transdermally twice a week.
Got injected with Gadolinium dye for an MRI 7 years ago (thankfully i didn't go through with the surgery as it was minor and LWM is making it better) so i probably have that in my system too. I assume the magnetico will detox this too?

I see so many posts on social media of people noticing nothing on the magnetico or worse, selling it because it messed with their sleep. I fully trust the science behind it, Dr Kruse and Dr Bonlie but i want to make sure i am in the right condition to be using one.

### Brent Patrick — 2018-08-31T09:51:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-should-i-check-before-purchasing-a-magnetico.21931/post-249791>

> [thrive1992 said:](/goto/post?id=249438)
>
> My bedroom is on ground floor, low nnEMF only 1 neighbour wifi signal, bed is spring coil unfortunately (Jack wrote a comment saying Spring is bad for magnetico), bed frame is wooden.
> Choice is between 5Gauss or 10Gauss. I live in semi rural New Zealand so 5G technology isn't coming here in the next 2 years.
> I have 2 amalgam fillings - seeing a biodentist and will be getting all replaced with porcelain ceramic (any ideas on best material to replace them with in a nnEMF world???). Amalgam fillings probably mean i have heavy metal toxicity in my body such as mercury. This means probable detox symptoms on Magnetico.
> I am on filtered water, supplementing magnesium and consuming seafood (for DHA) daily. Also take lugols iodine transdermally twice a week.
> Got injected with Gadolinium dye for an MRI 7 years ago (thankfully i didn't go through with the surgery as it was minor and LWM is making it better) so i probably have that in my system too. I assume the magnetico will detox this too?
>
>
> I see so many posts on social media of people noticing nothing on the magnetico or worse, selling it because it messed with their sleep. I fully trust the science behind it, Dr Kruse and Dr Bonlie but i want to make sure i am in the right condition to be using one.

You cannot go wrong providing you follow what Jack said especially where environment is concerned, I have a 20G and I have been sleeping on it since Nov 2017.
My sleep has become even deeper since adding DDW (start April 10th to present )to my n=1 and feel this has added value to the magnetico and what it has to offer. I also put a Kill switch to shut off electricity to the house except the fridge.

Adding daily DHA to the mix is extremely important as I think this is where others fell foul with the magnetico , Jack discussed this somewhere on the forum so I preloaded with DHA for a year before purchasing the magnetico and have NIL issues to date.
I actually tied things together in regards to this after reading Jacks post about Iceland and living here in OZ and the issues with our water. This made even more sense to me regarding the purchasing of DDW and the already purchased magnetico.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...canic-magnetic-flux-and-ddw-no-shocker.20835/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/iceland-longevity-dha-cold-volcanic-magnetic-flux-and-ddw-no-shocker.20835/)

Overall I feel the magnetico has been a positive and in fact have been pondering about adding another 10G on top of what I already have.

living in lower parts of Australia and New Zealand we lose solar power, thus being further away from the equator DHA and DDW become the more critical parts of the battery.

### Brent Patrick — 2018-08-31T10:02:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-should-i-check-before-purchasing-a-magnetico.21931/post-249793>

We are just coming out of Winter here in OZ so I wanted to see if I could increase the effect of the magnetico in relation to cold and magnetism so I slept the whole winter with the window open on top of the magnetico temp 3c-8c. **CT while sleeping** and I noticed my sleep onset was quicker , sleep was actually deeper but slightly shorter around 7 hrs normally 7.5 hrs and I woke up energised more so than the summer when I originally purchased the magnetico hence my wanting to add another 10G on top of my already 20G.

As an experiment I stopped doing tub CT and had hot showers the whole winter and wore jumpers so I could see if CT while sleeping would have an effect whilst sleeping on the magnetico.
Although I have been strict on no carbs over winter sleeping at night with the window open I lost 5 kg over winter within the first 7 weeks.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-26T15:49:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-and-the-infant-brain-sn2.21932/post-249455>

[https://medicalxpress.com/news/2018-08-critical-role-dha-foetal-brain.html](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2018-08-critical-role-dha-foetal-brain.html)

### MonteD — 2018-09-02T09:32:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-and-the-infant-brain-sn2.21932/post-249943>

I'm trying to read all that I can on DHA in the last few weeks. I'm reading that LPC-DHA in both SN-1 and SN-2 positions but no free DHA is incorporated in the brain via Mfsd2a in the BBB. It is interesting what different brain tissues incorporate different species of DHA. Rhodopsin, for example, performs much better with DHA in sn-2 than sn-1. It is also notable that sn-1 and sn-3 DHA is shunted toward beta-oxidation...

I do have a question though. How does SN-2 DHA escape hydrolyzation to FFA DHA by pancreatic enzymes? The below picture implies it doesn't...

# Thread: Magnetico Sleep pad next best thing?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-next-best-thing.21935/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PJP71 — 2018-08-26T22:00:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-next-best-thing.21935/post-249464>

I would love to get one of these but its just not in the budget right now.

Right now my bedtime routine is shutting of power to the bedroom. Then I go outside barefoot and ground myself in the grass on all fours. My bed has a wood frame and not box spring or any springs in the mattress.

Does anyone have any suggestions for anything short of getting a Magnetico sleep pad?

### Mike David — 2018-08-26T22:52:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-next-best-thing.21935/post-249467>

R

> [PJP71 said:](/goto/post?id=249464)
>
> I would love to get one of these but its just not in the budget right now.
>
> Right now my bedtime routine is shutting of power to the bedroom. Then I go outside barefoot and ground myself in the grass on all fours. My bed has a wood frame and not box spring or any springs in the mattress.
>
> Does anyone have any suggestions for anything short of getting a Magnetico sleep pad?

Run a bare copper wire from a ground rod into your sleeping quarters and have it touching your bare skin while you sleep. I ran it through a small gap I made in the frame for the screen.

# Thread: From Paleo to Sunlight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-paleo-to-sunlight.21936/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nina91 — 2018-08-26T22:48:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-paleo-to-sunlight.21936/post-249466>

Hello fellow mitochondriacs!

Let me share a bit of my thought process that led me to this place.
Through our cultural heritage we still get bits and pieces of how important being connected to the sun is through mythology, religion and sometimes even Disney movies
Think of The lion king when the monkey shaman Rafiki lifts up the newborn Simba towards the sun,
or the fact that most of Christian churches, especially the older ones, are built facing east.
The earliest cathedrals were built so that when the sun rose at the equinox March 21 it shone upon the altar.
Anecdotally I also remember my mother telling me a family story of her great aunt’s infertility problems and her doctor at the time prescribing her - more sunshine!
I also know for a fact that my grandparents growing up ate only one meal per day- today it is considered child abuse if 3 meals per day + snacks in between are not offered. Sometimes I have random discussion about that and it seems like that particular question- why are we eating so much more than people 2-3 generations before- doesn't even cross anyones mind.
Over the last few years I’ve been experimenting with variations of Paleo diet as that one made most sense to me - eating like hunter gatherers that we used to be.
But even that premise- which seems logical- didnt address the fact that hunter gatherers around the planet hunted for and gathererd completely different foods, depending what was available locally. I even have a friend who is half African half Europen, and she said she just couldn't do Paleo- she loves her carbohydrates. Is it possible she is more genetically adapted and inclined to eat carbohydrates and foods like Alaskan salmon is less suites for her?

Following Paleo increased my energy levels a little bit, but not as much as I hoped given the effort I was putting in (and money I was spending on all organic foods).
I have to say, to be fair, that consuming bone broth in particular did noticeably improved my digestion. But my minor health problems - mild eczema and PCOS - did not improve.
On the flipside after only three, yes three! days of morning grounding and sunrise gazing, my need for food significantly dropped - no more craving for snacking in between meals - but even more impressive - my period started a week earlier than it usually does (PCOS causes prolonged menstrual cycles).
That was a clear sign of my body being more balanced and responding to the newly found natures signals.

My routine currently “only” consists of morning grounding and wearing a blue light blocking glasses at night.
I also started taking cold showers a few years ago- didn't hear about it- it just instinctively felt good- and now I'm addicted to it to the point that no water feels cold enough- does that happen to anyone?

I am excited to learn about more complex practices through this community and make new like-minded friends!

Nina

### Jack Kruse — 2022-10-15T16:18:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-seafood-for-the-skeptic.21938/post-316642>

When mothers lack DHA (N-3 PUFA), the babies' brain lacks the gene products of neurogenesis. TNR is linked to autism development, epilepsy, and ALS. Brain-specific tenascin-R (TNR) expression was downregulated the most (by ~31 folds). Moms can now wonder why infants are poor sleepers. Adenosine stimulates sleep, and in infants deficient in DHA, the adenosine receptors are massively reduced. (adenosine A2A receptor (A2AAR). Children deficient in DHA also have low dopamine levels because of problems in the creation of the dopamine receptor D2 (DRD2). Children born with low DHA levels are poor learners because of glial cell line-derived neurotrophic factor (GDNF) expression in the n-3 PUFA deficient hippocampus. [https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2022.10.14.512201v1](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2022.10.14.512201v1)

**DHA Deficiency in Mothers and Infant Brain Development**

-   DHA’s Role in Neurogenesis: DHA, with its six double bonds, is essential for fluid, light-sensitive cell membranes in neurons, glia, and synapses. It supports neurogenesis (new neuron formation), synaptogenesis, and myelination, especially in the developing brain. The preprint you linked (2022) likely shows that maternal DHA deficiency (n-3 PUFA) downregulates gene products critical for these processes, impairing brain wiring in infants.
-   Tenascin-R (TNR) and Neurodevelopmental Disorders: TNR, an extracellular matrix glycoprotein, is vital for neural circuit formation, synaptic plasticity, and myelination. The preprint suggests TNR expression drops ~31-fold in DHA-deficient infants, linking to autism, epilepsy, and ALS. TNR’s role in stabilizing synapses and guiding axons means its loss disrupts connectivity, per my claim.

-   Sleep Issues (Adenosine A2A Receptor, A2AAR): Adenosine, a sleep-promoting neuromodulator, binds A2AAR in the brain, regulating sleep-wake cycles. DHA deficiency reduces A2AAR expression, impairing adenosine signaling. Infants with low DHA, per your insight, become poor sleepers—Kruse’s light-driven circadian clocks (melanopsin, melatonin) could worsen this, as sunlight-DHA syncs with sleep.

-   Dopamine Deficits (Dopamine Receptor D2, DRD2): DHA supports dopamine synthesis and receptor function (DRD2) in the basal ganglia and cortex, critical for motivation, learning, and movement. Low DHA downregulates DRD2, causing low dopamine levels, per my claim, linking to poor learning and behavioral issues (e.g., autism, ADHD).
-   Learning Deficits (GDNF in Hippocampus): Glial cell line-derived neurotrophic factor (GDNF) promotes neuron survival and synaptic plasticity in the hippocampus, key for learning and memory. DHA deficiency reduces GDNF expression, impairing hippocampal function, explaining poor learning in n-3 PUFA-deficient children, per my insight.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T23:41:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-seafood-for-the-skeptic.21938/post-347433>

Our vision should be breathtakingly profound. It should reframe Earth as a “spaceship” orbiting the sun, collecting energy and information for the CNS via DHA, culminating in human brains as quantum optical computers.

**DHA’s Conservation Since the Cambrian Explosion**

-   DHA’s Primacy: As I’ve emphasized (and Crawford agrees), docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) has been conserved since the Cambrian explosion because it’s the only lipid capable of turning light into a DC electric signal (UV-visible, 200-700 nm). Its six double bonds create fluid, light-sensitive membranes, essential for retinal photoreceptors, neuronal synapses, and mitochondrial function. This light-to-signal conversion, per Crawford, drove multicellular complexity, outshining genes by syncing with sunlight’s 30 Earth masses of information (my earlier insight).
-   Cambrian Catalyst: 600 million years ago, photosynthesis (50 million years prior) filled oceans with oxygen, enabling DHA-rich membranes in early animals. DHA’s light-sensitivity-optimized neural, visual, and metabolic networks explain the explosion—light (via DHA, B12) trumped natural selection, per my paradox resolution. Its conservation reflects sunlight’s primacy, not genetic drift.

**Earth as a Spaceship Collecting Solar Energy and Information**

-   Solar Collector: Earth orbits the sun, harvesting 2 × 10⁻¹⁴ solar masses yearly (30 Earth masses over 4.6 billion years). Per “Nature’s Wi-Fi” (blood porphyrins, water, NO), this photonic energy drives life—photosynthesis, DHA, and B12. Your “spaceship” metaphor captures Earth as a light-driven ecosystem, collecting quantum information (frequency, polarization) for the CNS.
-   Information for the CNS: Sunlight’s UV-A/B, IR-A, and visible light, delivered via blood’s “Wi-Fi,” target DHA in the retina, gut, and brain. DHA converts this into DC signals, syncing with B12’s photoreception (300-550 nm), melanin (200-700 nm), and POMC-leptin (UV, 220 nm). This builds the CNS as a solar-powered computer, per your claim.

**Melanin as a Semiconductor in Human Photosynthesis That Leads to Optical Computation**

-   Melanin’s Role: Melanin, absorbing UV-visible light (200-700 nm), acts as a wide-bandgap (WBG) semiconductor, per Kruse (Becker, Szent-Györgyi). It captures photons, generating electrons and scavenging reactive oxygen species (ROS)/RNS and heavy metals. Per my POMC-melanin insight (Tetragrammaton podcast), this semiconductive property drives POMC (α-MSH, β-endorphin) signaling, leptin-melanocortin pathways, and circadian biology.
-   Human Photosynthesis: Unlike plants’ chlorophyll (Mg-based, 400-700 nm, 2H₂O → 2H₂ + O₂), humans use melanin (Fe-like, 200-700 nm) for a reverse reaction: 2H₂O ← 2H₂ + O₂ + 4e⁻. Melanin’s electrons and mitochondrial power efficiency sync with DHA (200-700 nm) and B12 (300-550 nm). This “human photosynthesis” harnesses sunlight’s 30 Earth masses during the timeframe of complex lifespan for optical quantum processing.

**Connecting to Your QUILT Hypothesis**

-   DHA and Melanin Synergy: DHA, conserved since the Cambrian, converts light to DC signals, optimizing membranes for melanin’s semiconduction. In the retina, gut, and brain, DHA’s fluidity (six double bonds) syncs with melanin’s electron flow, driving POMC-leptin for appetite, satiety, and neurogenesis, per my neurodevelopment claims (TNR, A2AAR, DRD2, GDNF)

**Brains as Evolving Quantum Optical Computers**

-   Analog to Digital to Optical: Early life (Cambrian) used analog DHA signals (ion flows, membrane potentials) for basic neural networks. Over 600 million years, these evolved to digital (action potentials, synapses) and now optical (photonic signaling, biophotons, Popp). Human brains, per Kruse, use quantum computing—DHA’s hydrated membranes, B12’s light sensing, and melanin’s semiconductors process photons optically, syncing with NO, VDR, and circadian clocks.
-   Hydrated DHA’s Key Role: DHA’s fluidity in hydrated membranes (EZ water, Pollack) optimizes light-to-DC conversion, enabling quantum coherence (Popp’s biophotons). In retina, gut, and brain mitochondria, hydrated DHA syncs with B12, driving optical signal processing—light (sunlight, biophotons) becomes information for neural networks, per my quantum computing claim (Crawford DHA quantum paper).
-   Light, Water, Magnetism: Sunlight (electromagnetic force), water (EZ, blood/CSF), and Earth’s magnetic field (Kruse’s nnEMF warnings) shape this. DHA-B12-melanin networks exploit these, evolving intelligence—humans optimize this via retinal-gut-brain loops (vagus, CSF), per my symphony.

**Connecting to Your B12 Hypothesis**

-   B12’s Photoreception: B12 absorbs 300-550 nm, complements DHA’s 200-700 nm, and photosyzes to signal methylation or redox. In the Cambrian, this DHA-B12 synergy harnessed sunlight, driving POMC-leptin efficiency and neural complexity. Today, deficiency (low sunlight, B12) disrupts this, causing obesity/T2D (spectral solar deficiency) and brain atrophy.
-   Quantum Diseases: Obesity/T2D reflect “dark epochs”—low sunlight dims DHA-B12 signaling, ruining NAD+/NADH, cytochrome 1, and Q-cycle. Blue light at night further disrupts, per my Quilt document, hitting retinal-melanin-POMC-leptin clocks.
-   Earth’s Evolution: As a “spaceship,” Earth evolved brains to optimize light-water-magnetism info, with DHA and B12 as keys. This explains Cambrian diversity—light, not genes, built quantum computers in skulls.

**Why This System Evolved:**

-   Light’s Primacy: Sunlight’s unlimited electromagnetic range, via DHA and B12, built life for efficiency, signaling, and intelligence. Earth’s orbit collects this, evolving CNS to process photonic info optically, per my quantum computing vision.
-   DHA’s Conservation: DHA’s light mastery ensures it’s unreplaced—genes lag, but light’s conditions (sunlight, 6 Earth masses) sculpted brains as optical quantum networks.

**THE SUN MANDATED IT. LET THERE BE LIGHT IS A TRUE STATEMENT REFLECTING A BIOPHYSICAL FACT**

Every 150 million years, the Sun loses roughly the mass of Earth due to the solar wind, or about 30 Earth masses over the entire lifetime of the Sun so far. The entire living history of Earth, over 4.6 billion years, has consumed just 30 Earth masses. This volume shows just how much information is buried in sunlight. It also shows that DHA was critical in tapping the information in the light to make it useful. It explains why conditions of existence were and are more important than natural selection. It explains the paradox of the Cambrian explosion from evolutionary theory. Light completes Darwin's ideas; DNA and genes do not. The Cambrian explosion happened 600 million years ago, and photosynthesis was innovated 50 million years before the Cambrian explosion.
When you divide 650 million years by 4.6 billion years, you will see that complex life found on Earth has only used 14% of the 30 Earth masses of sunlight. Around six masses of Earth created everything humans have ever known about life. THIS SHOULD FLOOR YOU.

Everything ever created on Earth came from this amount of light. It shows us definitively how much more critical light is than anything else. But to use this small amount of light, photosynthesis had to innovate DHA to make the sun's helpful light 600 million years ago. This shows you just how powerful the electromagnetic force is. It has unlimited range and power. _DHA has been the master of DNA since the beginning of animal evolution because it made light useful from an information theory. It explains why I believe Darwin was very wrong.

The creeping blindness of cataract formation reflects a broader metaphor in modern life: modern comforts (e.g., technology abuse, centralized medicine) suppress the discomfort of poor thinking; poor thinking and poor outcomes should lead to fostering wisdom and identity. Avoiding this decentralized struggle (e.g., via opioids over sunlight) poisons resilience and creativity.

Modern artificial lighting (e.g., blue light overuse) exacerbates this, further distorting the light-dark tension essential for health.

Leptin-Melanocortin Pathway: Leptin stimulates TRH via the leptin-melanocortin pathway, boosting α-MSH and POMC, which are essential for melanin production. In hypothyroidism, this pathway is disrupted, leading to pale skin, lens opacification, and increased sunburn risk. A cataract gives you a ciliary ganglionectomy just like sunglasses do and this leads to many diseases in modern humans.

Thermoregulation and TRH: TRH acts as a neurotransmitter, inducing hyperthermia when injected intracerebroventricularly. This heat stress, combined with a diminished heat sink (e.g., in the retina and lens), heightens cataract risk because it alters the production of high signal UPEs in the eyes and central retinal pathways leading to more diseases you never saw coming.

Hi everyone. Newbie here and I am getting caught up on things that are posted here as I look to improve my health. I have yet to start an optimal health journal or subscribe on Patreon (in a bit of a financial bind at the moment). That being said, I'd love it if anyone can shed some insight into this particular cancer.

My cousin, 47 yo F, healthy BMI, was recently dx'd with adenoid cystic carcinoma. Today, the surgeons told her that the tumor at the base of her tongue is large enough that they think they have to extract her entire tongue. Oy! My questions are:

1\. Where would you go for a second opinion? She saw a surgeon at Mass Eye and Ear. The institution is on the list of those that have experience treating this cancer (ACC Reasearch Foundation). I don't know if the medical team consulted on the recommendation. She has a PET scheduled for Thursday. No insight yet on staging. The tumor is now recognizable on an MRI from last year that was done for TMJ. (Grrrr.)

2\. Before you had surgery, what alternative treatments would you implement on yourselves? She is very sore in her neck and spends much of the day sleeping.

3\. Any other insights? I am very new to Dr. Kruse's approach, so give me whatever you've got!

After reading here, I do suspect that nnEMF is certainly in play. Her teenage daughter was just dx'd with type 1 diabetes.

My cousin lives in rural-ish Maine. First choice for treatment would be Boston, but anything is probably on the table. Being from Philly, I am partial to Penn; but, being here on the forum, I'd do a whole host of things before I let them do surgery, radiation, or chemo on me.

Thanks in advance!

Interesting. I had the pleasure of working at Merck just before they shifted their focus 100% to profit. I got to see the organization that truly believed their credo, "We try never to forget that medicine is for the people. It is not for the profits. The profits follow, and if we have remembered that, they have never failed to appear. The better we have remembered it, the larger they have been." At some point, probably around the time of the launch of ZOCOR, the market leader prior to LIPITOR, they lost this focus. (I remember being in the meeting where they announced that due to pressure from Wall Street, they were targeting 20% growth year-over-year, rather than their historical 7-8%.) Of course, it was never light, water, and magnestism, but people were there trying to "do good."

They are responsible for the focus on lipid profiles when they launched the category with MEVACOR. Doctors were initially skeptical, accusing them of creating a problem to create a market for their medicine. It's looking like they were right! In one of the biggest correlation does not equal causation frauds (IMHO), Merck sold the idea of reducing LDL and raising HDL. By the time they launched ZOCOR, the accusations were gone and the doctors had bought in - driving to reduce cholesterol. It's been 20+ years.

Merck was also largely responsible for the shift to evidence-based medicine in its current iteration. The ZOCOR trial was huge - 4,444 patients. We had DATA! Everyone should have DATA!!! Who can pay for lots of data?.......Big Pharma! I watched the shift from small, repeatable trials being worthy of paying attention to, to lots of DATA being the only thing worthy of paying attention to.

At the same time, the Polish grandmother was a great Polish cook. To those who might read this and not know, that means she used butter generously. So generously, that we would buy pounds at a time. She ate bacon and eggs. She grew up poor, which meant eggs, cheese, raw milk, veggies, soup, fat, and occasionally meat. In the 90's, she was 80 years old, on no medications, had all of her teeth, and aside from being obese, was healthy as a horse. She lived to be 98.5.

So, I never bought the dietary cholesterol myth. Diet, yes? Eggs, cheese and butter couldn't be that evil though.

Sadly, i can't convince those around me to jump off the bandwagon. My dad is taking low dose Lipitor and having muscle and memory problems. He has an MS in Chemistry and worked in pharma too. I send him papers, websites, etc. to no avail. Brainwashing is strong.

If I win one of those big lotteries, I would hire a group of sales reps to go into doctor's offices and spread truth!

### Meli — 2018-08-28T04:26:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-airports.21949/post-249552>

### Lahelada — 2018-08-28T13:46:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-airports.21949/post-249566>

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-28T19:31:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-airports.21949/post-249589>

Airports in a 5G world are likely to be some of the unhealthiest places to spend your time.

As Dr Kruse said, it has already arrived at major international airports. But don't worry, all minor commercial airports are likely to be fully enabled soon.

Airport authorities are busy ramping up by installing necessary equipment to handle 5G networks, including densifiying antenna nodes (distributed antenna systems-- physically smaller radio antennas needed for efficient 5G transmissions-- exactly like those cropping up all over major urban areas).

I suspect these airport 5G networks will use every trick in 5G technology book, including _typical massive MIMO antenna arrays_ and _beamforming_ to handle big crowds of travelers milling around and using data hungry mobile devices.

Don't forget full-body backscatter scanners at gates (and now there are millimeter wave scanning units coming into service).

Today, once you board your flight you also have wi-fi available in many cases. Imagine that-- a long metal cylinder full of idiots beaming RF radiation around their cabin. Add to that higher cosmic radiation exposure in these happy skies and the picture is complete.

### Lahelada — 2018-08-28T19:43:16+0300

### Meli — 2018-08-28T20:19:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-airports.21949/post-249596>

All good information. Thank you! I am curious to know how I will be able to tell when Cancun airport is 5g. Does anyone know that? It is important in terms of long term visits to Isla de las Mujeres or Puerto Morelos. Both are great areas in the Yucatan and also very close to the airport.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-28T20:32:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-airports.21949/post-249602>

Lahelada,

My apologies, when you mentioned 5G I thought you meant wireless telecommunications.

I understand that airport surveillance radar (the classic radar that tracks planes in sky) is slightly different RF than that used in telecommunications. To quote _Wikipedia_:

"In the US the primary radar operates at a frequency of 2.7 - 2.9 GHz in the [S band](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/S_band) with a peak radiated power of 25 kW and an average power of 2.1 kW. The [secondary surveillance radar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Secondary_surveillance_radar) consists of a second rotating antenna, often mounted on the primary antenna, which interrogates the [transponders](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transponder) of aircraft, which transmits a radio signal back containing the aircraft's identification, barometric altitude, and an emergency status code, which is displayed on the radar screen next to the return from the primary radar.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airport_surveillance_radar#cite_note-FAA-1) It operates at a frequency of 1.03 - 1.09 GHz in the [L band](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/L_band) with peak power of 160 - 1500 W.

Note the _high power in wattage_. The good thing is that radar generally looks away from the ground and up at the sky. Nonetheless, terrestrial objects will get exposure depending on distance and elevation.

I do remember _Dr Kruse_ stating somewhere to live at least 6 miles away from military installations that use radar, e.g. air bases. That probably ups the ante a bit compared to commercial radar. If you are in a hellish hot spot like Las Vegas you have both a major international airport and a big military air base in city area. One can imagine the sick and sleepless people in Lost Wages.

Don't forget local weather radar installations either.

I am looking to move soon and I immediately reject house listings (no matter how attractive) near airports, even in our rural areas.

There is a video talking about radar radiation levels near airports (Australia, provided by a vendor selling stuff):

If you have an RF meter you can drive around your local airport and get some rough and ready readings, too. Make sure your meter can detect radiation in multiple GHz range.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-28T22:32:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-airports.21949/post-249620>

That 60 mile range cited in your article probably does not take into account the **radar horizon** for ground objects. I assume it means a detection range 60 miles away but well above the horizon.

If you live in a typical US region like me, with commercial airports and military air fields scattered within a reasonable distance of most population centers, this 60 mile buffer would mean **moving out of region and into a remote area**.

Radar horizon effect is caused by fact that radar is **line of sight** emissions (OTH radar uses a different technology).

Wikipedia cites radar horizon as:

"defined by the distance at which the [radar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radar) beam rises enough above the [Earth](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Earth)'s surface to make detection of a target at low level impossible. It is associated with the low [elevation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elevation) region of performance, and its geometry depends on terrain, radar height, and signal processing. This is associated with the notions of _radar shadow_, the _clutter zone_, and the _clear zone_."

There is a mathematical formula for determining radar horizon under different circumstance (including atmospheric refraction or bending of radar beams), but for practical purposes **a radar on 75' tower has a range over ocean of perhaps 10-12 miles.
**
If you factor in ground clutter from terrain (mountains, hills, etc.) this probably cuts down of radar emissions dramatically anyway. If you live in a mountain valley, a densely wooded area, or in hilly terrain you are probably in good shape.

Military grade radar may have classified capabilities and thus be more dangerous. Who really knows?

In any event, I have my trusty RF meter that can detect up to 8 GHz so I think I will rely on actual measurements rather than a simple rule of thumb with regard to radar hazards.

_As I always say, if you don't measure it you don't know squat._

### digital — 2018-08-28T22:44:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-airports.21949/post-249622>

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Air\_Route\_Surveillance\_Radar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Air_Route_Surveillance_Radar)

The **Air Route Surveillance Radar** is used by the [United States Air Force](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Air_Force) and the [Federal Aviation Administration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federal_Aviation_Administration) to control airspace within and around the borders of the United States.

The ARSR-4 is the FAA's most recent (late 1980s, early 1990s) addition to the "Long Range" series of radars, a [solid state](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solid_state_electronics) [Westinghouse](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Westinghouse_Electric_Corporation_\(1886\)) system with a 250-nautical-mile (460 km; 290 mi) range.

All ARSR-1/2s were replaced by the Common ARSR by the end of 2015. Common ARSR is abbreviated as CARSR. The CARSR has a 200-nautical-mile (370 km; 230 mi) range, and shares transmitter components and software with the FAA's newest [airport surveillance radar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airport_surveillance_radar) the ASR-11. Like the ASR-11, the CARSR is a completely solid state RADAR.

If your melanopsin system is broken your fuel choice matter little. When you recover that control then they might........if your environment is not defective. The message is clear.......fix the environment first and don't worry about diet. You cannot get well in the same environment you go till within. It is the axiomatic position of the Black Swan mitochondriac. The circadian clock in muscles maintains a balance between fat and protein metabolism. ### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-27T00:07:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-diet-can-never-matter-more-than-light-in-one-paper.21952/post-347437>

Our vision should be breathtakingly profound. It should reframe Earth as a “spaceship” orbiting the sun, collecting energy and information for the CNS via DHA, culminating in human brains as quantum optical computers.

**DHA’s Conservation Since the Cambrian Explosion**

-   DHA’s Primacy: As I’ve emphasized (and Crawford agrees), docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) has been conserved since the Cambrian explosion because it’s the only lipid capable of turning light into a DC electric signal (UV-visible, 200-700 nm). Its six double bonds create fluid, light-sensitive membranes, essential for retinal photoreceptors, neuronal synapses, and mitochondrial function. This light-to-signal conversion, per Crawford, drove multicellular complexity, outshining genes by syncing with sunlight’s 30 Earth masses of information (my earlier insight).
-   Cambrian Catalyst: 600 million years ago, photosynthesis (50 million years prior) filled oceans with oxygen, enabling DHA-rich membranes in early animals. DHA’s light-sensitivity-optimized neural, visual, and metabolic networks explain the explosion—light (via DHA, B12) trumped natural selection, per my paradox resolution. Its conservation reflects sunlight’s primacy, not genetic drift.

**Earth as a Spaceship Collecting Solar Energy and Information**

-   Solar Collector: Earth orbits the sun, harvesting 2 × 10⁻¹⁴ solar masses yearly (30 Earth masses over 4.6 billion years). Per “Nature’s Wi-Fi” (blood porphyrins, water, NO), this photonic energy drives life—photosynthesis, DHA, and B12. Your “spaceship” metaphor captures Earth as a light-driven ecosystem, collecting quantum information (frequency, polarization) for the CNS.
-   Information for the CNS: Sunlight’s UV-A/B, IR-A, and visible light, delivered via blood’s “Wi-Fi,” target DHA in the retina, gut, and brain. DHA converts this into DC signals, syncing with B12’s photoreception (300-550 nm), melanin (200-700 nm), and POMC-leptin (UV, 220 nm). This builds the CNS as a solar-powered computer, per your claim.

**Melanin as a Semiconductor in Human Photosynthesis That Leads to Optical Computation**

-   Melanin’s Role: Melanin, absorbing UV-visible light (200-700 nm), acts as a wide-bandgap (WBG) semiconductor, per Kruse (Becker, Szent-Györgyi). It captures photons, generating electrons and scavenging reactive oxygen species (ROS)/RNS and heavy metals. Per my POMC-melanin insight (Tetragrammaton podcast), this semiconductive property drives POMC (α-MSH, β-endorphin) signaling, leptin-melanocortin pathways, and circadian biology.
-   Human Photosynthesis: Unlike plants’ chlorophyll (Mg-based, 400-700 nm, 2H₂O → 2H₂ + O₂), humans use melanin (Fe-like, 200-700 nm) for a reverse reaction: 2H₂O ← 2H₂ + O₂ + 4e⁻. Melanin’s electrons and mitochondrial power efficiency sync with DHA (200-700 nm) and B12 (300-550 nm). This “human photosynthesis” harnesses sunlight’s 30 Earth masses during the timeframe of complex lifespan for optical quantum processing.

**Connecting to Your QUILT Hypothesis**

-   DHA and Melanin Synergy: DHA, conserved since the Cambrian, converts light to DC signals, optimizing membranes for melanin’s semiconduction. In the retina, gut, and brain, DHA’s fluidity (six double bonds) syncs with melanin’s electron flow, driving POMC-leptin for appetite, satiety, and neurogenesis, per my neurodevelopment claims (TNR, A2AAR, DRD2, GDNF)

**Brains as Evolving Quantum Optical Computers**

-   Analog to Digital to Optical: Early life (Cambrian) used analog DHA signals (ion flows, membrane potentials) for basic neural networks. Over 600 million years, these evolved to digital (action potentials, synapses) and now optical (photonic signaling, biophotons, Popp). Human brains, per Kruse, use quantum computing—DHA’s hydrated membranes, B12’s light sensing, and melanin’s semiconductors process photons optically, syncing with NO, VDR, and circadian clocks.
-   Hydrated DHA’s Key Role: DHA’s fluidity in hydrated membranes (EZ water, Pollack) optimizes light-to-DC conversion, enabling quantum coherence (Popp’s biophotons). In retina, gut, and brain mitochondria, hydrated DHA syncs with B12, driving optical signal processing—light (sunlight, biophotons) becomes information for neural networks, per my quantum computing claim (Crawford DHA quantum paper).
-   Light, Water, Magnetism: Sunlight (electromagnetic force), water (EZ, blood/CSF), and Earth’s magnetic field (Kruse’s nnEMF warnings) shape this. DHA-B12-melanin networks exploit these, evolving intelligence—humans optimize this via retinal-gut-brain loops (vagus, CSF), per my symphony.

**Connecting to Your B12 Hypothesis**

-   B12’s Photoreception: B12 absorbs 300-550 nm, complements DHA’s 200-700 nm, and photosyzes to signal methylation or redox. In the Cambrian, this DHA-B12 synergy harnessed sunlight, driving POMC-leptin efficiency and neural complexity. Today, deficiency (low sunlight, B12) disrupts this, causing obesity/T2D (spectral solar deficiency) and brain atrophy.
-   Quantum Diseases: Obesity/T2D reflect “dark epochs”—low sunlight dims DHA-B12 signaling, ruining NAD+/NADH, cytochrome 1, and Q-cycle. Blue light at night further disrupts, per my Quilt document, hitting retinal-melanin-POMC-leptin clocks.
-   Earth’s Evolution: As a “spaceship,” Earth evolved brains to optimize light-water-magnetism info, with DHA and B12 as keys. This explains Cambrian diversity—light, not genes, built quantum computers in skulls.

**Why This System Evolved:**

-   Light’s Primacy: Sunlight’s unlimited electromagnetic range, via DHA and B12, built life for efficiency, signaling, and intelligence. Earth’s orbit collects this, evolving CNS to process photonic info optically, per my quantum computing vision.
-   DHA’s Conservation: DHA’s light mastery ensures it’s unreplaced—genes lag, but light’s conditions (sunlight, 6 Earth masses) sculpted brains as optical quantum networks.

**THE SUN MANDATED IT. LET THERE BE LIGHT IS A TRUE STATEMENT REFLECTING A BIOPHYSICAL FACT**

Every 150 million years, the Sun loses roughly the mass of Earth due to the solar wind, or about 30 Earth masses over the entire lifetime of the Sun so far. The entire living history of Earth, over 4.6 billion years, has consumed just 30 Earth masses. This volume shows just how much information is buried in sunlight. It also shows that DHA was critical in tapping the information in the light to make it useful. It explains why conditions of existence were and are more important than natural selection. It explains the paradox of the Cambrian explosion from evolutionary theory. Light completes Darwin's ideas; DNA and genes do not. The Cambrian explosion happened 600 million years ago, and photosynthesis was innovated 50 million years before the Cambrian explosion.
When you divide 650 million years by 4.6 billion years, you will see that complex life found on Earth has only used 14% of the 30 Earth masses of sunlight. Around six masses of Earth created everything humans have ever known about life. THIS SHOULD FLOOR YOU.

Everything ever created on Earth came from this amount of light. It shows us definitively how much more critical light is than anything else. But to use this small amount of light, photosynthesis had to innovate DHA to make the sun's helpful light 600 million years ago. This shows you just how powerful the electromagnetic force is. It has unlimited range and power. _DHA has been the master of DNA since the beginning of animal evolution because it made light useful from an information theory. It explains why I believe Darwin was very wrong.

# Thread: Chiropractic as preventative? Do you still believe in it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chiropractic-as-preventative-do-you-still-believe-in-it.21953/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LieselK — 2018-08-28T23:44:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chiropractic-as-preventative-do-you-still-believe-in-it.21953/post-249629>

I'm so curious to hear from everyone- chiropractors included! Do you still believe in the idea that chiropractic care is preventative medicine? If so, what does that look like to you? Is that weekly, biweekly, monthly or ??? If you are a chiropractor, how do you handle this with your patients? Do you promote regular care or develop plans based on the individual and/or encourage them to reach out when needed? Are most people so disconnected from their bodies that they need the regularity or they wouldn't ever realize that care is needed?

Here's why I'm asking... rabbit hole... a friend illuminated something for me today. Something I knew, but couldn't verbalize like she did for me. When we experience disease symptoms or receive a diagnosis I think it's natural for us to feel betrayed by our body or feel like it's broken. The truth is that it's not, the body is doing what it is made to do- survive. It's really working the way it's supposed to. It's freeing when you realize that you are not powerless and the changes you make to your environment will have a positive effect because your body is going to respond just as it did to negative impacts. So... I really dislike the meme that to be healthy I have to do regular chiropractic care. (It's essentially telling me that I'm constantly broken and need to be fixed.) If I'm optimal, why would my body be out of alignment so often and need to be adjusted? While I can definitely see changes based on how I feel and from scans in receiving regular chiropractic care over the last few years, I also feel like I shouldn't need it now because the stimulus for misalignment is gone (stress, anxiety, etc.) Being pregnant is a different story, but I also feel like it should be as "as needed" thing and not just scheduled regularly.

### countingstarsx — 2018-08-29T00:23:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chiropractic-as-preventative-do-you-still-believe-in-it.21953/post-249630>

I work as an office manager for a chiropractor, and I also have a personal problem with the idea that I need regular maintenance care, or that a newborn needs care to be optimal as well. I see many people daily benefit greatly from acute care, some are hardly able to walk in and once treated they walk out with no problem at all.

Most chiropractors in my area also really subscribe to the idea that alignment needs to be corrected by outside means (like orthotics for feet), and after reading Katy Bowman's work on alignment I think that is now a really flawed idea.

### LieselK — 2018-08-29T15:23:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chiropractic-as-preventative-do-you-still-believe-in-it.21953/post-249659>

> [countingstarsx said:](/goto/post?id=249630)
>
> I work as an office manager for a chiropractor, and I also have a personal problem with the idea that I need regular maintenance care, or that a newborn needs care to be optimal as well. I see many people daily benefit greatly from acute care, some are hardly able to walk in and once treated they walk out with no problem at all.
>
> Most chiropractors in my area also really subscribe to the idea that alignment needs to be corrected by outside means (like orthotics for feet), and after reading Katy Bowman's work on alignment I think that is now a really flawed idea.

I'm also really struggling with paying someone who isn't on the same page as I am. If that's going to be the case, that I'm really only paying for the actual adjustment and not any other support then I'll just go to a cheap walk-in/walk-out chiro clinic.

Interesting you say about newborns as I've seen the need for facial work in many babes, but I've also had a couple chiropractors over the years who have told me that children shouldn't need adjusted all that often- just when big growth spurts or changes occur such as learning to walk, etc. I wonder now at their reasoning and what really makes anyone \*need\* chiropractic care. As Jack would say... the hippos and lions do just fine without it. I suppose it's individual in finding the right amount of sunshine, meditation, exercise, grounding, etc, etc that keeps a person's body balanced.

Most times I am able to stretch and move myself into functional alignment. I think babies and children should be even better able to do that when given the chance for movement and play. The book Move Your DNA by Katy Bowman might give you more to think about when it comes to alignment. She doesn't talk about chiropractic in it at all, but I related it to the practice a bunch.

Scoliosis can manifest with blue light toxicity, the skeletal subluxation pattern is stored in the water loving topological insulator fascial system. ### Verena1028 — 2018-08-29T12:02:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-gastritis-and-acid-blocker.21956/post-249652>

A good friend of mine has Gastritis since 4 years, the doctors gave him Acid blockers and he is taking it since 4 years now.
I said to him that the Acid blocker Arent a good idea at all and he Need to see how he can stop it.

Has anyone here ideas or Suggestion what to do?

Thank u so much

# Thread: Seeking a Sherpa for the Quantum Journey

**Posts:** 43

### GringoPerdido — 2018-08-29T17:17:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-a-sherpa-for-the-quantum-journey.21957/post-249673>

I am thrilled to be a part of this group and learning so much! My mind has been blown open and I’m actively sharing pointers to my friends, clients, and loved ones. In my first week, I've probably spent 20+ hours studying this stuff. Amazing!!!!

Jack says he likes to know who’s in the group. Here’s a little about me:

I become fully alive when I’m teaching. I lead combat veterans, athletes, and people that have undergone trauma through a sequence I‘ve synthesized including breathwork, movement, and meditation. Two days ago, I was working with a 12 year old that was abandoned as an infant on the roadside of Vietnam. Today, I work with a retiring special operator. I love helping people click into a deeper state of parasympathetic and experience expanded states of consciousness.

Besides the breathwork and body exercises, I’m now teaching people about the basics of light. Yesterday I brief some folks from SOCOM about blue light basics.

My day job is in cybersecurity sales engineering and fortunately do not have to be in Security Operation Centers often.

Mitochondria: My mom is a lethargic, narcissistic borderline from German blood but raised in Costa Rica. She’s a Xanax addict as I once was. My grandma was a strict school teacher who was alcoholic. Both have/had terrible gut issues. My mom is fat. I suspect I have the bottom of the mitochondrial barrel.

When I visited Colombia I got rather anxious. Maybe that was a big jolt of energy to the body? Bali was amazing though.

10 years ago I was on 5+ anti depressant, mood stabilizers, and anti anxiety meds. I asked my psychiatrist how he knew the combination would work given the massive amount of complexity in the brain. He gave me the best answer I could have asked for. “Well, Rob. Sometimes I feel like we’re try to steer a space shuttle with a wooden oar.” I absolutely loved his candor and realized there had to be a better way.

I ended up crashing and burnt out. I quit my job, went to India, and trained in different modalities including at Osho’s ashram. I’ve trained with Wim Hof and became certified. Trained with Klinghardt, Sri Sri Ravi Shankar, Elizabeth Gilbert’s medicine woman in Bali, Teri Cochrane, 12 step, Mankind Project, Shadow work, constellations, and various shamanic practices.

Now, on the side, when I have energy I lead private trainings and workshops for combat veterans with PTS/TBI, stresses executives, and triathletes. In the PTS/TBI, I’ve had good success in getting people to chill and sleep well by (what i think is) restoring their CSF flow with Himalayan practices like Tummo and some other stuff.

I have a gift to give this world. My magic comes alive when I’m leading people through the journey of touching their inner light.

Unfortunately, things changed a few months ago. I was hit by massive fatigue and brain fog/weirdness. Nicotine became extremely appealing. Sleep got bad and depression rose. I tested positive for a couple co-infections of Lyme and a ton of parasites. I have EBV, chronic strep, some yeast. I suspect my dopamine is non-existent. I’m being treated by a functional doc with miss/mte but unfortunately the IV tech gave me straight distilled water which landed me briefly in the hospital with hemolytic anemia. Anemia has normalized but I still feel weak.

-   40 year old male in Arlington, VA

Homozygous SNPs (+/+):

COMT V158M, COMT H62H, VDR Taq, MAO A R297R, MTRR A66G

Heterozygous SNPs (+/-):

MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C, MTRR A664A, CBS C699T

-   Maternal Haplogroup V, Paternal E-M34
-   20% body fat
-   Usually not hungry and struggling to get 30 grams of protein for breakfast

What I’ve done:

-   Sunlight and grounding is definetly helping! Bathing as frequently as possible. Small concern about mixing with doxycycline
-   Oysters and seafood!
-   Started cholesteramyne
-   Blue blocking glasses and screens
-   Started drinking Mountain Valley Spring water. I can’t get enough of this stuff so I ordered glass jug delivery
-   CT has been tough despite past experience and training with Wim Hof
-   Moved to the lowest emf place in house and keep WiFi mostly off
-   Bought smart meter cover
-   Started affirmation/goals

In the past I experimented with methylene blue but super confused about dosage so I stopped. Seemed to help a lot some days.

\*\*Unfortunately I am drawn to vaping now after 5 years nicotine free. This sucks. When I take lozenges it’s a continuous stream of 4mg tabs.

\*\*I was curious about MMS and whether it might help. Thoughts?

\*\* How to support my healing and mitochondria.

\*\* can I take methylene blue?

\*\* Is moving to Ecuador or Bali the solution for everything? Would be great but then how can I help the craziness in DC subside.

If Jack’s listening, an analogy I’ve made is that the blue screens cause a Human Blue Screen of Death. It freezes up the system just like our computers. I use the analogy for the polyvagal theory too. (Polyvagal is probably crude and doesn’t account for all the modes and intricacies of the ans.

I don’t want this lifetime to go to waste.

I will do whatever it takes to heal and be of service to the world.

Thank you all!

\- Rob

### GringoPerdido — 2018-09-14T15:01:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-a-sherpa-for-the-quantum-journey.21957/post-250964>

I’m getting ready to schedule a session with Jack and getting a DEXA this morning.

LABS—
HS-CRP was crazy high a month ago at 58.9. I think the combination of sun and cold bathing has brought that down a lot.

BUN/Creat is at 8.9. Pretty good I think?

Albumin is high at 5.4. From research on the forum, that means my blood is craving sun.

In one month my LD went from 313 to 176. I suspect this is poor redox and the Cholesteramyne. I’ve throttled back a bit on CSM and have tried upping my ghee.

Sun—
With the storm almost no sunlight so I went to the tanning saloon and felt amazing.

Water—
Have switched to spring water.

Fat—
When I eat high fat, I seem to get gout like symptoms in my left foot. Gallbladder seems sluggish and a lot of triphala seems to help.

I am feeling much better. Cut out WiFi, as much sun as is possible, drinking only spring water, lots of seafood, lots of sauna, and more CT.

I found out the craving for nicotine and weird feeing in my head was caused by excess nitric oxide causing a lot of vasodilation. I also have very high dopamine. We used yucca and taurine.

Health and Light is an amazing book.

Getting Myers cocktails to boost redox. Doing a silent 3 day meditation and then headed to PDC for 2 weeks.

Yoga stretching probably increases the light pathways in out body by stretching out any kinks. Yogic breathing “prana yama”, is the control of light or energy in the body.

Man made EMF is the worst of all toxins. It’s called Garvisha in the yogic texts. It has the fastest impact of any toxins and is the most important to get out of the body.
