### GringoPerdido — 2018-11-27T22:33:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-a-sherpa-for-the-quantum-journey.21957/post-256776>

> [recoen said:](/goto/post?id=256632)
>
> [@RobInDC](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21869/) how do you perform your muscle testing?

[@recoen](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21522/) I have a fellow practitioner, Teri Cochrane, who's a pro at muscle testing and taught me. Also trained under Dr. Klinghardt.

An easy way is to use a lean test. I hold a bottle near my heart and sense if there's a lean forward or backward. I've also used a pendulum. Some may find it to be very woo woo but I think there's a quantum explanation and a subtle sensing of the autonomic nervous system.

I will caveat this with, muscle testing may not work or may give totally inaccurate results if the body is in a closed or switched state. Tends to work better when you're healthy. Maybe better redox? Maybe better coherence or entanglement? My muscle testing works much better when my meditation practice is up.

I spent time with my parents to learn more about my history.

**Mom info:**

My mother was conceived during a very highly stressful time in my grandmother's life. My grandparents had just moved to a farm 7 hours by horseback from San Jose Costa Rica. My grandmother hated the rural environment and felt very much out of place. She was traumatized by the whole experience and so the period during my mother's gestation was rough. My grandmother always had migraines which started during the farm period. She was traumatized by the experience and thereafter was heavy impacted by crippling migraines.

My mother has had esophageal problems like reflux and they were worried about Barret's esophagus. She is obese, never exercises, has a wrinkly face, is a Xanax addict, judgmental, and addicted to religion. She always has a thick coating of makeup and has tattooed makeup on. :/ She is lactose intolerant and has always had significant deep stomach pains. She hated getting up early. My dad loved getting up early.

I was birthed vaginally.

Now, looking back, my father was heavily sunlight deficient. He had terrible allergies and would rage often. He instinctually knew to take cold showers. He's Jewish with AustroHungarian lineage.

# Thread: CT in long light cycles
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mike David — 2018-08-29T21:34:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/post-249700>

So I hope some of the smarter people than me can clear this up for me. Our SCN is entrained to natural light cycles to regulate our body clocks. The cold disconnects the SCN from being yoked to light cycles. I wake up to see the sunrise partly to yoke the body clock to the natural light cycles. When I CT in the summer is the SCN able to reyoke to the light after I CT in long light cycles? If I CT in long light cycles does the SCN just ignore the cold trying to shut it off from being entrained to light? How long after CT does this shunting effect last? Would CT in the summer have its greatest effect right before bed or does it not matter?

### kris90 — 2018-08-30T01:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/post-249726>

I wouldn't overthink it. Basically, when temps fall in the environment, that becomes the dominant stimulus to entrain the circadian rhythm. In summer, light is the dominant stimulus, irregardless of how much CT you do I would imagine. Think of winter like a prolonged night, and think of summer like a prolonged day. In winter, we express eNOS in brown adipose tissue (BAT) to direct bloodflow to fat and skeletal muscle, because we need to expend energy to produce heat. In summer, we use vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) to direct bloodflow to the gut because we eat more (i.e. carbs) in long light cycles. Night time would mimic what winter does (i.e. doing CT).

### Antonis — 2018-08-30T16:32:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/post-249755>

>
> I wouldn't overthink it. Basically, when temps fall in the environment, that becomes the dominant stimulus to entrain the circadian rhythm. In summer, light is the dominant stimulus, irregardless of how much CT you do I would imagine. Think of winter like a prolonged night, and think of summer like a prolonged day. In winter, we express eNOS in brown adipose tissue (BAT) to direct bloodflow to fat and skeletal muscle, because we need to expend energy to produce heat. In summer, we use vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) to direct bloodflow to the gut because we eat more (i.e. carbs) in long light cycles. Night time would mimic what winter does (i.e. doing CT).

that's why in the equator we can do well with the sun only?

### kris90 — 2018-08-30T18:03:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/post-249761>

> [Antonis said:](/goto/post?id=249755)
>
> that's why in the equator we can do well with the sun only?

Precisely, because the sun keeps us tightly coupled. The IR light from the sun heats the water that surrounds our tissues helping to bring deuterium up to the surface, and the UV light puts the pinch on deuterium to complete the human photosynthesis process of making UVC light in blood plasma.

In the cold, we need our mitochondria to release the IR light to do what the sun was supposed to do to bring the deuterium up. In order for this to occur, we needed to express the uncoupling proteins to allow protons to leak.

### Mike David — 2018-08-30T18:12:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/post-249764>

Well doesn’t that make a lot of sense. Thanks for clarifying. So based on where I currently reside CT is completely necessary in the UV winter. In the summer it’s the best of both worlds. I underestimated my fear of the cold. Showers are one thing the tub is a whole other beast to tame. Sometimes my Neolithic mind gets in the way and presents convenient excuses cloaked in comfort and peace of mind. All leading to a broken mind blinded from the ways of nature.

### Evan Leck — 2018-09-04T02:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/post-250094>

With modern life, even our people aren't getting anywhere near the optimal amount IR/UV light exposure. So I would think that CT during summer makes sense as an adjunct to maximize the cellular battery charge, or to overcome some disease state.

I think the bigger issue, in terms of seasonal modalities, are keto folks who miss out on the benefit of carb consumption during summer.

Sunlight can make many anti-inflammatory chemokines and this is why the Vitamin D level is linked to MS link. Food is ONLY important to life when it is disconnected from earth, sun, and water for long stretches. Why? What do you and tree have in common? You're both alive. Does a tree have to eat? No. It makes everything it needs from thin air, water, and sunlight. It uses water as its photosynthetic battery to make sugar from CO2 and sunlight. It can do this because its roots connect it 100% of the time to the Earth's magnetic flux which is powered by the sun via the dynamo and its canopy is 100% connected to the solar radiation based upon its location. It has zero need for food sources. You, however, are not designed to be 100% connected to the magnetic flux or the sun's radiations solely for your energy source. You have a need for food because you have a backup system that allows you to do this called a colony of mitochondria. The amount of time you are disconnected from both sources is the amount of food you need to eat. The other variable that mitochondria give humans is that those at the equator have mitochondria DNA that is tightly coupled meaning that they need less food to make electrons because they have strong solar flux and strong magnetic flux because they tend not to wear shoes.........therefore they don't need to eat. Their food electrons are always high energy and high information bearing electrons unlike the one further from the equator. Those humans who live outside the equatorial zones (most modern humans) have Northern European mtDNA that is loosely coupled. This means their mitochondrial DNA needs more food electrons to uncouple to make more heat to keep them warm and build their EZ in cells to offset this increase disconnection from the sun. It is assumed by Mother Nature, they would still have a connection to the Earth but that is no longer true. As such those outside of the tropics have to eat more (electrons/protons) because they are more disconnected from the sun and the magnetic flux of the dynamo. This decreases the spin rate of the ATPase on a relative basis from those who live inside the tropics. It is not an overgeneralization; it is a quantum fact. Why? All foods are linked directly and indirectly to photosynthetic webs. Most modern humans are divorced from evolutionary theory and from mitochondrial science. If you listen to a food first guru you'll never reverse your Multiple sclerosis autoimmune condition with food. MS is a quantum disease, not a foodborne illness. All autoimmune conditions are diseases where humans live a blue light, an indoor existence where their immune system is re-programmed by the power grid and not properly programmed by the sun and magnetic dynamo as humans were built too. UVA, UVB light, and the VDR all inhibit ECT in mitochondria to simulate fasting effect. Sunlight has 42% IR-A light which increases the ATPase spin rate at the same time while the effect of the UVA, UVB, and the VDR reduce electron flow. The effect of red light on the red light chromophores in the ATPase keep protons flowing from the matrix to the ATPase to make ATP with less food needed. This is the Black Swan wisdom in this paper. # Thread: Deep fried drones
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-fried-drones.21963/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2018-08-31T07:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-fried-drones.21963/post-249788>

I live in a popular tropical surf destination and whilst the go pro is now firmly attached to most learner boards.....the use of the 'flying drone' to capture 'the shot' to try and satiate the insatiable appetite of the narcissism factories of facebook and instagram.....i often ponder what the operators of such are doing to themselves.

They dock their phone into a controller which i am guessing powers up the signal of the phone to allow it to be received by the drone. I was wondering if anyone has measured these devices, when in action with emf meters? I am guessing they are giving themselves the deep fry with lashing of calcium efflux and mito heteroplasmy as a double helping for dessert.

Outside of the idiots droning themselves to death........i wonder what ever happened to actually just sitting silently and contemplating the natural beauty that surrounds them.

### drezy — 2018-08-31T17:09:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-fried-drones.21963/post-249813>

These systems use 2.4GHz or 5.8GHz radios. It won't be that different that holding a modern router in your hands as well as staring into a blue-lit screen for however long.

There are multiple ways to remove them from your sky.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-31T17:53:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-fried-drones.21963/post-249817>

As a would-be drone pilot I can understand the thrill of ersatz flying experience with them using onboard cameras. But using them to take selfies to show off on social media is a bit beyond the pale.

Drones can be very useful tools for observation and surveillance, especially if you have a large property.

Drone controller units are probably like most other remote control gadgets used in toys, R/C hobbies, etc. There are good reasons not to go there with these RF devices.

For die-hard drone fans, no doubt there are ways to mitigate RF exposure using a shield similar to that used in cell phones (e.g. _DefenderShield_ product). Ideally, this way RF beam is deflected mostly away from operator and aimed at drone. But only some diligent RF meter observations could prove how safe it really is.

Let's see if Kruse members can report in with their experiences on this topic.

### drezy — 2018-08-31T19:48:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-fried-drones.21963/post-249828>

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=249817)
>
> For die-hard drone fans, no doubt there are ways to mitigate RF exposure using a shield similar to that used in cell phones (e.g. _DefenderShield_ product). Ideally, this way RF beam is deflected mostly away from operator and aimed at drone. But only some diligent RF meter observations could prove how safe it really is.

I'm sending a heartfelt best of luck with that.

I have day blockers for artificial light, but am waiting to find the best source for a night pair for total blue blocking .. with my prescription .. maybe RA Optics when they are ready, or Specsavers if I can get them to do it the way I instruct them to (they are telling me blue light toxicity is pseudo at the moment, so maybe I'll give them a miss) .. Never concern yourself with what others are thinking/saying .. keep it up with those sunrises, sunsets and as much as you can in between .. as Byron Katie says .. "what other people think of you is none of your business .. "  .. so don't give it headspace. I don't know if I'd go nude .. but I've looked at the Kiniki bikinis .. very tiny, and you can sun right through them (a Kruse recommendation). I would love to meet you one day, so hold that thought .. maybe for when summer hits, and in the meantime, please do let me know of any more products etc you discover, and I can do the same .. feel free to email me too as an alternative [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted]). I don't know anything about Icelandic water .. at the moment I'm restructuring distilled (I have a distiller) .. and I've posted everything I do.
Thanks again, and yes, you are so lucky to be near the beach .. chilly now though  .. I used to live right on Curl Curl .. and that was truly icelandic in winters.
take care

I make mistakes just like everyone else and have certainly made my fair share the most important thing is to not fold like a cheap suitcase (skin in the game) and keep putting one foot forward. Reflecting on my mistakes and correcting them moving forward I feel has helped me focus on whats important.

I eat local oysters and purchase sockeye salmon from overseas.
I have testing done but to be honest I go on how I actually feel especially in terms of sleep I sleep very well,this was not always the case before I joined Jack's forum.
The biggest thing I recommend is to read the blogs and do not make the mistake I did, not reading in numerical order as I missed something fundamental several months ago as connecting dots is far easier.

I have made good gains only because I feel discipline and consistency is key and doing the following everyday and not being concerned of what others think or say.

Morning sunrise grounded
transition to UVA
seasonal eating based on Epi Paleo Diet
Consuming DDW
CT daily in some form either cold showers tub CT or of recent this winter Sleep CT
Watching my light environment
Using UVA Bulbs when unable to be outside for required time

I think the main thing Catherine is to just start and take it from there many Ozzies on the Forum and plenty of good advice from the members on this forum.

# Thread: solved finally gut issues!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solved-finally-gut-issues.21967/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Erin Gaiser — 2018-08-31T11:39:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solved-finally-gut-issues.21967/post-249801>

So basically just by blocking blue light (we did stopped wireless and RF about 1.5 years ago) we were finally able to stop chronic diahrea in my second son. It only took about 2 weeks! Additionally, they were getting as much of the sunrise as possible (hard when a mountain is blocking it) and as much sun as possible.

My first son has had amazing results. He is no longer sensitive to oxalates, he no longer needs the vitamins for HPU, and he has amazing moods constantly now! He is a new person. Additionally, he had problems with undigested food and needing to go to the bathroom 5-7 times a day. That is now down, could be better. He still needs to avoid apples, tomatoes, gluten and dairy. Hope this might change as we keep up with the protocol.

We are just so happy because these solutions are relatively easy and super cheap. I thought I would have to buy them probiotics and all those other supplements forever!

We have so much to be thankful for! Thank you Dr. Kruse!

# Thread: Raw vs Cooked
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-vs-cooked.21968/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alan Jewells — 2018-08-31T12:09:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-vs-cooked.21968/post-249803>

Forgive me, as this has probably been covered elsewhere, but I haven't seen detail and I don't trust the on-line charts. For oysters, the delicate qualities of their nutrition is coming from the DHA, iodine, as well as a massive amount of key minerals and other nutrients like zinc, etc. In looking at most charts, they indicate very little variance when cooked vs raw. I've read where Dr. Kruse mentioned frozen seafood has about a 40% reduction in DHA. So fresh is clearly important. I'm wondering if anyone knows the reduction of DHA, Iodine, or other nutrients when cooked. I had two dozen raw oysters last night, and I'm still learning to "enjoy" them as they are not my favorite food. I would enjoy them much more if I could pan fry them in tons of butter and garlic, but I also want the key benefits, some of which could be totally lost if cooked. I just don't know. This is probably a common question, but appreciate any wisdom around this. Thank you! Alan

### Brent Patrick — 2018-09-01T09:24:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-vs-cooked.21968/post-249873>

Alan you have to overcome your negative bias ,I hated seafood and would never have entertained the thought of eating raw oysters but two years later I eat on average 4 dozen per week plus wild salmon 5 times a week.
I now eat for Optimal function rather than pleasure and the results I have seen since incorporating seafood into my diet has been all positive.

Stay away from fish supplements and Algae oil they are rubbish

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/seafood-for-beginners.21060/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/seafood-for-beginners.21060/)

**When DHA is in the SN-2 position it becomes planar. You need seafood to convert algae's DHA into the SN-2 position. Taking algal DHA is a waste of time. This conversion of DHA to the SN-2 position allows it to be both an N-type and P-type semicinductor........because of the 22 double bonds become flat like a wire. Members will learn much more about why this is big because of two non-linear effects of light on harmonic generations. Reject fake fish oil sources no matter how bad the marketer tries to tell you otherwise**. [https://www.researchgate.net/…/The-planar-structure-of-the-…](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.researchgate.net%2Ffigure%2FThe-planar-structure-of-the-preferred-DHA-conformation-is-a-fundamental-characteristic-of_fig1_263925583&h=AT2nqZszN2D7ikLxIcZ37P4Kj81TdnvYbc38qdMFCnSocgJv9KPec7CLS6AmFGOMAHvi163Bc4hxPo9EdoGgxEWcwKzcX1hn0erl4m3yJYnhkkhOLzXgMD2oedcgF0MCCVoEVnCv_g_AOTZcz9ntYDX3E0QkjQwdhLW37D1vL-YNbIuVFWYeL_iMgw9Wkji8o3_0gJaq_uhRapzfbQczdy8KG5A4RN2dlI9QcpEO8A-XRCD-cz5mo3Ujho_aIXv6IKNlUIhFgNeUuYok-LQMDzGKqisR407QdCdrW8Fy2p_FkrZAJqNobo_4wRyH5dTlv5N3EaVAU8N2YlNd-AxR48Op7mGgdCUa4fseeTa6lF1cuyOKFFBXi2lbEwu5cdYbm5nam2n4bfLH3i3G69pKbiMEBDMyXhxARs4jKVjjhFtauA) JK FB [March 13](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/2073438466053830?__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARBgnO7wH7v63zquRk73QDd3rPqLq7TzEzj8ixt2qr1OETykpiHDnvznlQt9fuauWpGXacCtYCH0dIfG7ILlPrTtlUivWLPzU1Bwn-ARzJ2QWn5-aoC-lRWf8OylKgOYeXF9_BLaPH2qsFTUjBnaEs2dGFQM7-MOc1XZR5S5zB_J4yB2BvgSJQ&__tn__=-R)

# Thread: My Optimal Journal

**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Maureen M MacDonald — 2018-08-31T17:19:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21971/post-249814>

4 years with a functional medicine (IM) doc to deal with menopause. (Age 55) My learning: just taking a bunch of supplements doesn't work and is a huge waste of money and just creates stress to get them all down. (I hate taking pills.) Hormone replacement helps, but these docs are still learning- and know nothing re: methylation cycle issues (COMT++) that can complicate their dosing. My HS-CRP has been high (>4.0) for 4 years despite downing more fish oil than I ever imagined. I finally asked to get a stool/ gut test-- yes leaky gut issues and dysbiosis. More pills to swallow.
So then I found Jack-- had no idea both sun and nnEMF were so important. Love his biology explanations of everything. My stress has been chronically high for at least 10 years (single parent and business owner); working on meditation and just relaxing much more-- not take life so seriously would be good for me. I can think my way to a hot flash or cortisol flush-- training my mind seems more important than any damn supplement I might take.
Leptin Rx makes sense-- very close to Terry Wahls Protocol.
So ready to go with Leptin Rx/ Wahls eating (3cups greens, 3 cups sulphur veggies 3 cups color veggies/berries) -- Paleo Rx at its best.
Last labs: Vit D 45 (had been 27); CRP 5.0; Ferritn 142 (had been 62); HDL 75; LDL 158; Triglycerides 117; Fasting glucose 90; HBA1C 5.3; DHEA 203; Pregnenolone <20. Omega 6:3 ratio in 2014 was 5:1; Reverse T3 11.
Taking desicated thyroid-- last labs: Free T4 1.14; T3 87; Free T3 2.9; Reverse T3 10.
Morning cortisol was high in '15 (8.5) then low in spring '18 (3.3) -- Weight down 10 lbs since 2015-- now 152; BMI 24.0
I get it. Time to reverse all this stuff.
Wish me luck- our modern world and most people's eating habits are not supportive to change; so will look to this group to keep me going. Thanks in advance.

# Thread: Survival Soup on a Budget - The protein shake / soup version
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/survival-soup-on-a-budget-the-protein-shake-soup-version.21973/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### James Lech — 2018-08-31T17:49:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/survival-soup-on-a-budget-the-protein-shake-soup-version.21973/post-249816>

I really found Jack's Survival Soup useful. It is simple.
([https://www.patreon.com/bePatron?c=1083965&rid=1915327&redirect\_uri=/posts/survivors-soup-15707564](https://www.patreon.com/bePatron?c=1083965&rid=1915327&redirect_uri=/posts/survivors-soup-15707564))

But I needed to find a way to make it on a budget. A growing platform but thought the below may be helpful with a ketogenic diet.

The most expensive piece of tech in all of this is a very high powered blender like a Blendtec or Nutribullet Rx.

1.  When using shellfish seafood, the best was hunting for mussels described in my previous thread in the next point.
2.  [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...ering-the-bs-gaining-new-understanding.21516/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/emfis-filtering-the-bs-gaining-new-understanding.21516/)

3.  When not possible, I would look at the supermarket for amazing deals on frozen seafood or thawed "fresh-fish", that is on a sale. Often frozen seafood like shrimp, mussles is pre-cooked. I would use frozen raw or pre-cooked. Throw them into the blender with a little bit of hot water, and blend into a very fine mush. On the blender there would be a soup mix function. Decant and use for later mixing. I would throw-in prawns with shell, head and all. For fish, no need to de-bone as the bones are broken into fine particles.
4.  Add your butter, coconut butter or olive oil depending on your area and season.
5.  Meat and fats. Purchasing bones for cooking is really expensive here per kg. Instead, I found a really good butcher and I use the sawdust. (It is the extract from the cutting machine catch tray). The content is a mixture of all the different meats within the butcher, cuttings from bone and fat. You can cook it with hot water in a pressure cooker or rice cooker for 20-40 minutes. This extracts and melts the fat. I then place this into the blender on soup function. Again, any bone fragments are liquidized. A real bonus is when you get fat sinues etc... P.S. I know the above visually is unappealing but the taste and mitochondria effect is amazing. Decant the mix into a side bowl.
6.  Vegetable soup and herbs. In this, I would place vegetables, especially green (kale is 1/3 the price of spinach here), seaweed/kelp, ginger, spices, herbs, vegetable stock etc.... blend again into a soup function. If you have hearty vegetables like pumpkin or sweet potatoes, I recommend cooking it. Use what works best for your seasonal environment.
7.  Now pour all blended mixes together to get a good consistency.

Now drink/chew drink. The taste is surprisingly great (especially if you use ginger). You get a tone of seafood, fats, proteins and vegetables with water.

I tell people, it is an effective protein shake compared to weigh. You are able to make liters (a gallon/s) of it in under an hour, refrigerate and consume over a couple of days. P.S. don't leave it for too long. It works very well as the big ass breakfast in the morning.

P.S. I re-iterate, the visual, intellectual idea of it is off-putting, but once you understand the science and mito mechanisms, it makes a lot of sense, and again, it is overwhelming how tasty it actually is.

Jack, let me know if you want me to make a quick video post of this?

# Thread: Occasional CT time off + caffeine question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/occasional-ct-time-off-caffeine-question.21978/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Corey Nelson — 2018-09-01T16:47:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/occasional-ct-time-off-caffeine-question.21978/post-249884>

Background: all year I've done 2-5 CT sessions each week, most being in 50-55f water with 30lb bagged ice for 45-60+ minutes. I don't always love it but I know I'm quite cold adapted and sometimes I don't shiver at all and feel hot during the session. It's awesome!

Occasionally I feel like taking some time off (going a week with 0-1 sessions), mostly when it's been a stressful week and I'd end up missing some needed rest time or altering my schedule. I think I've done this twice and other times I've gritted through.

The question: for long time CT users have you found any rules of thumb for this? I know I don't lose my adaptation in that period gauging by physical response but I'm curious if anyone can share their own attitude towards how they view time off. And if you take time off at any particular time of year, for what reasons, and whether you found a limit for retaining adaptation. Anything relevant to this line of thought would be cool. I gather that less time off is better and some mini sessions can be helpful during travel. Is there any situation where time off is considered beneficial?

As a bonus unrelated off topic question that probably doesn't deserve its own thread: how often do you other strict adherents to Kruse protocols consume caffeine? I am definitely no longer psychologically or physically addicted to it but have found it's fun for recreational and cultural reasons about one to two times per month (1-3 cups of strong coffee or tea) , and never out of any feeling of necessity (not to be more productive or combat fatigue).

Thanks in advance.

### kris90 — 2018-09-05T21:10:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/occasional-ct-time-off-caffeine-question.21978/post-250277>

In my experience, it's like muscle memory. The adaptation sticks. As long as you don't use fake light at night and have a good circadian rhythm, you are designed to use BAT to burn off excess calories as heat every night when the leptin receptor is activated. Cold adapted or not, it seems BAT is an important type of metabolic tissue to regulate our weight year round. We probably just express it more when we live in the cold.

# Thread: Laura Cullen-Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laura-cullen-optimal-journal.21982/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Laura C — 2018-09-01T23:41:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laura-cullen-optimal-journal.21982/post-249899>

My Optimal Life Journal for Laura Cullen
Geographic Location: Small town 40 minutes south of Madison, Wisconsin. Planning on moving to Mexico, in two-four years.
Age: 45 years(1972), caucasian female, 166 lbs, 5'7"
Occupation History: Former medic in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, left job to raise my children in 1996
Current Occupation: self employed at home, writer, homemaker, homeschool teacher
Education: Public/Private schools until High School then Homeschooled, some College, EMT Certificate
Kyuki-do martial arts: eligible to test for 1st Dan black belt, Judo- Green Stripe, Juijitsu-rank forthcoming; post black belt test
Parents: Mom(deceased colon cancer aka bowel obstruction secondary to colon cancer) a former RN, later years recovered alcoholic, mitral valve prolapse. German ancestry, immigrated in 1841 to Wisconsin.

Father, alive, former engineer. Abusive, no contact. Super high cholesterol, literally off the charts. Dad's mom, German ancestry. Dad's dad was born in Hungry and came in US in 1930's.

Current problems: menorrhagia(heavy menstrual bleeding during my 3 day cycle), I like to loose about 15 lbs.
Current Supplements: SUN
Current Prescriptions: none, however, I was told the last two physicals to take statins due to my crappy high cholesterol. MY LDL is 211. I suspect my body is making cholesterol to turn into pregnenolone, one for my imbalanced hormones(toxic blue light), and two since I spent the better part of my life living in homes with an abusive dad, then emotionally abusive husband, emotional/physically abusive second husband. So suffice to say, my body was running high on cortisol. Then there is the electrical aspect as well.

Diet/Supplements: Standard american processed food diet until my mid twenties and had children. Proceeded to do an elimination diet for dairy sensitivities. Tried to heal gut via Weston Price food guidelines. After 5 years of examining and living their food dogma, I walked away to seek other answers. I proceeded down Paleo alley, and ended up on the doorstop of Magnesium Morley and his supplement guidelines for the last three years. Yet, again I didn't find the answers, I felt that his approach to supplementing health left me with unanswered questions and I was disenchanted with this all in one approach. I lean towards eating lots of meat, seafood, veggies, and fruits. Grains don't sit well in my stomach as I feel all bloated. I do consume dairy, currently A2 milk from a farm 3 hours north of me. I make my own kefir using A2 milk but usually drink that mostly in winter.

Medical History:
Eyes: high optical pressures reported since I was 7 years old, myopia, near -9.00 in each eye. I wore gas permeable contact wearing between the ages of 15 and 28. Currently wear glasses during day, spend time outdoors without my goggles, and wear blue blockers that I made with BPI tint diamond dye 550, inside when I am looking at tv or computer screens.

Ears: Normal, until recently. Hearing noise at night, maybe early tinnitus(I feel it's nnEMF related). Planning on getting a meter shortly to figure out what nnEMF I need to mitigate.

Heart: Diagnosed with mitral valve prolapse 1996, while pregnant. Experienced palpatations while resting. Short term prescription during pregnancy. No current issues. Mom had mitral valve prolapse too.

Gut health: Misdiganosed with antiperspirant allergy when I was 14, in the 1980's. Later on, a family doctor that diagnosed my firstborn with a dairy sensitivity(she had rashes on her face and creases of her fat folds as a baby, also concurrently diagnosed me with a dairy sensitivity and I stopped dairy consumption for several years during nursing my 1st daughter. My 2nd daughter, almost 17 yrs has a bad gut and can't eat eggs, or hazelnut family ingredients and for a long while just has issues with pain in her belly when she ate. I will add her issues later on and what she is doing in her journal. (look down below for more details on Brooke)

Spine/Skeletal: Spondylolesthesis L3 if memory serves. Chronic pain for 20 years, about 9 rating on a 1-10 pain scale, which I addressed with massive tons of ibuprofen, and chiropractor visits which got me to a 5 on the pain scale. Then I used acupuncture, a sports medicine chiropractor and a sports medicine surgeon who taught me how to use core muscle exercises to strengthen and support my core. I rarely have pain in my lower back now. I spend lots of time outside naked getting morning sun each day and laying out midday exposing my naked body to the sun. I've never had surgery.

Fertility: Moderate, I'd rate it poor(I know I had 4 kids)

Children: 4 (2 girls, 2 boys), short hard labors, vaginal births, 1 miscarriage

I have tracked my fertility through temperatures/cervical position/ an cervical mucus status since I was 20 yrs old.

Daily waking temps below 97.3 regularly in my 20's. Self treated with sea kelp tablets. (another fallout of not getting enough sun and seafood), 5 years spacing between first two children, 3 years before baby #3. Then 9 months spacing before conceiving number 4.

Breastfed all kids, until they weaned themselves at late toddler age. 3 yrs 2 months, 2.5 years, 3 years and 4 years(tandem nursed the boys together).

Menstrual difficulties: Menstruation began at 14 years old. At 37 yrs began having huge heavy clots(size of my hand) and heavier bleeding than normal for me, which is usually heavy. I use a silicone menstrual cup and cloth liners since I am part hippy(minus the weed experience). Vaginal ultrasounds, and doctor consult was negative for any abnormalities or fibroids. The GYN said it was normal for women my age to have heavy periods and that my only option was birth control or a hysterectomy. I chose neither and began to research ways of controlling heavy bleeding on my own. I tried supplements for women like evening primrose oil, vitex(chaste tree berry), magnesium, diffferent magnesium, acupuncture, vit d oil, no eating sugar, or drinking caffeine, using essential oils, eating liver, and nothing worked. Magnesium Morely seemed to think my bleeding was related to my body dumping iron from added iron to processed foods and grains however I haven't eaten breads for over 2 years now and still had the bleeding issue.

### Laura C — 2018-09-01T23:55:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laura-cullen-optimal-journal.21982/post-249900>

January(late) 2018- I was introduced on facebook by a friend who shared one of Dr. Jack's blog posts on deuterium. I went to read the blog post and I just couldn't figure out what deuterium was. I saved the webpage to return to it later. A week later, same friend shared another Dr. Jack post at which point I went to the website and started reading about this Dr. Jack fellow. The website was loaded with information and data and I am a lover of data, so I skimmed many webpages, blogposts and noticed a few guidelines. I ordered Epi-Paleo and Health and Light to be delivered to my local library. I started reading posts on Dr. Jack's facebook page. A couple weeks, later Health and Light by John Nash Ott arrived and I read half the book that morning. The next day, I went outside to get morning sun, in February, in Wisconsin, in my bikini top and my plaid flannel pajama bottoms(no dissing the plaid flannel please). I was surprised to not feel that cold, when the sun's ray were directly on my skin. A few weeks later, we had record high temps of 38 degrees and I was spring cleaning my flower garden in the front yard in shorts and my bikini top and feeling comfortable. I joined as a bronze member since I wanted to read posts on the forum and planned to read every post on the forum by May.

February-2018 Epi-Paleo Rx finally arrived at the library. I started reading it immediately. I was a few chapters into the book and I wanted to scream, cry, shout, throw up the window and yell outside some more at some passerby that I finally, had answers. Instead, I would spout out random paragraphs to whichever child was around to hear me. It was the first time, I felt a medical professional understood the idea behind root cause. It was the first time the puzzle pieces in my head started to make sense.

February 2018- Watch Dr. Jack's Vermont 2017 talk (three times), took notes. Read Light and Health again. Getting outside for morning sun. Read forum posts.
March 2018-Watch Dr. Jack's Vermont 2016 talk, took notes, rewind video, listen again. More notes. Found fish heads at the asian grocery, made bone broth with seafood. Get outside in the morning sun.
April 2018-Ordered Epi Paleo RX from the library, read it again. Ordered BPI tints to make blue blockers.
June 2018-spending more time in the sun, outside. Listening to podcast on Youtube with Dr. Jack while working online at home.
July 2018-Upgraded to gold membership so I can access more podcasts, talks. Ordered kniki swimsuit. Bought Vermont talk 2018, listened 2x. Took notes.
August 2018-I recently listed to Dr. Jack talk about heavy bleeding being a deuterium story in his youtube titled CT Talk 12/17/18 with Jeremy Thomley and Benjamin Smith at 54:30 and that sparked an idea about excess estrogen causing the heavy bleeding.

So to address my heavy bleeding, I eliminate exposure to blue light at all times, cover my skin(thyroid especially) if in stores. Wear blue blockers when exposed to tv, computer work at home, indoor lighting at other locations. My home has amber incandescence, or red lights. I usually don't need the light at night since I go to bed shortly after sunset. I don't eat ice(I was concerned with it being heavy in deuterium). I did eat ice a lot the last two years since I enjoying chewing it. I have sex to orgasm which offloads deuterium, every night. I aim for 2-3 15-20 second orgasms each night as the second and third seem to be more intense so I feel it is more effective at offloading deuterium. And oh hey, since I can't pee standing up I might as well enjoy the multiple orgasm gift. So sex isn't all about deuterium, that would be a lie. I wake just before sunrise and go expose my naked body to the rising sun for about 10-20 minutes. I eat within 30 minutes of waking, usually seafood bone broth(which I make from fish heads) with loads of shrimp with shells/tails on, clams, oysters, seaweed, sea lettuce. Some days I eat eggs and bacon(I'm having issues finding pork belly straight up) and other days if I run out of seafood bone broth I eat raw salmon. Lunch, I drink some A2 milk or flaxmilk or having more seafood broth. Supper is meat, and veggies. I then head to the bathroom and do CT after breakfast, fill up the tub with straight cold water and some ice, I can only get the temp down to 73.8 so I am looking at sticking my chest freezer in the basement for cooler CT. I've been spending time outside with no shoes and in my kniki suit for 2-3 hours per day, as I stain our cedar fence, pull weeds from the flower beds and yard. I did this for 3 weeks straight and noticed that I don't like being inside, I feel anxious and interested in coming up with reasons to be outside the house. I suspect nnEMF is causing my issues or maybe I just feel I need more sun medicine. I plan to purchase a trifield or cornette meter next month to see the areas I will need to mitigate nnEMF.

### countingstarsx — 2018-09-02T20:48:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laura-cullen-optimal-journal.21982/post-249988>

Welcome Laura! I found Morley in my early quest for healing too -- even purchased a consult with him. I was taking so much magnesium a day and after a month my body crashed. For the last 3 years I haven't been able to supplement with mag at all because it dehydrates me and causes extreme anxiety/depersonalization. Morley had no answers, and that when I knew it really wasn't the root cause. Dr. Kruse's answers have really resonated with me, and brought a lot of improvement. I'm happy you've found us!

### Laura C — 2018-12-16T16:36:11+0200

Thank you for the "welcomes" Dr. Jack, Jenny, Counting Stars, and for the welcome and B-day wishes Phosphene! I will be posting a more detailed update here soon. Yesterday, I was listening to some Schumann resonance in music and wow, that was amazing to feel in my brain. I think I am going to play that during homeschool time and when we aren't hanging outdoors. I'm deep in the throes of researching a DIY mattress since my daughter needs a full bed. Mattresses aren't made nowadays to be long lasting(another fallout of demand for cheap crap) So I am aiming for 9 inches of rubber latex over magnetico pad, zipped in a wool cover special ordered from diymattress or rubber latex 3 inches over pocket coil springs also zipped in wool custom cover. Since her bedroom is on a floor 1/2 up(we live in a bilevel home where the basement is only half in the ground, 3 bedrooms up, 2 down), I wanted to increase the geomagnetic field for her while she sleeps. Oysters are back for the holiday, so I stocked up on those. The sun has been out consistently in the morning for a few hours during these two blackout months(Nov 30-Jan30) in Wisconsin, so I have been outside during that time since late September and October was so overcast.

And since it is my birthday today, I am now 46 years young, we are eating out at the seafood buffet(they have lots of oysters and sushi for me and other fare for other people) for brunch and then come home and play cards... usually, Sheepshead, Florida Rummy or Zioncheck, all good German pastime activities.

# Thread: It's been 3 years
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-been-3-years.21983/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aerose91 — 2018-09-02T00:48:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-been-3-years.21983/post-249907>

October 2015 i couldn't take how sick i was anymore. I got rid of everything i owned, bought a tent and a few new pieces of clothes, remediated my truck and drove out to the desert to live off the grid and start my doctor's protocols as well as Dr Kruse's.
Due to an untimely sand storm my camp got ruined but there was no way i was giving up only a few months in. Since then i have been living in my truck, relocating all around the country based on altitude, sun spectrum, air quality, EMF levels and any unknown factors that may be effecting me. I just know what areas feel right when i get there.
I have lived in 26 different places in these years, returning to some that felt the best as the seasons allow (Borrego Springs CA, Jenner CA, Seaside FL, Outer Banks).

My circadian rhythms are completely fixed and dead on now. I haven't missed a single sunrise or sunset while being grounded and drinking a glass of spring water in 3 years. Just the other day i was at a gas station and took my shoes off and laid in a little patch of grass by the curb so i could be grounded for sunset.

It took me about a year to build enough tolerance to the sun to not have regular crashes from it, but i did. I'm still at my worst mid day when the sun is strongest and it is hottest but i can tolerate it. Evening, about 2 hours prior to sunset is by far the best time of day for me.

At first cold was a killer- crashed me hard at the slightest amount. I can tolerate small amounts now like a cold dip in the ocean or river mid day on a hot day. Too long in there is still a problem, though. Hot and humid days are by far the worst for me.

My diet has always been good. I keep the seafood high and always have some seaweed and other marine foods around.

I drink only spring water. I have dozens of springs mapped out around the country that i fill up at whenever i pass by.

The only time i wear shoes is to go into a store. I'm on average grounded 8-10 hours a day and when i was camping, i stuck a copper wire in the sand and wrapped the other end around my toe to stay grounded while sleeping.

I'm only on my phone when grounding and preferably in the sun. I have a red light filter on it at all times.

I have a new doctor ive been seeing who just re-did a bunch of labs i had done just before leaving for the desert 3 years ago. My reverse T3 and TSH have been cut in half, my potassium is up and my bun/creatinine ratio went from 16 to 10.5. My manganese and molybdenum went from non existant to normal. My bilirubin for the first time ever is in normal range.

Good signs!

But...

I don't feel any better. Honestly nothing symptom wise has changed. I still have very thick depersonalization/derealization, my exercise intolerance is as bad as it ever was, I'm still equally sensitive to foods and environmental factors. Muscle weakness is unchanged and my memory & confusion issues are the same as they always were since getting encephalitis. I still border on psychosis often and small things like a full moon or over doing it can drop me into it. It's honestly very difficult to live like this.

I think I'm solidly in the lyme/CIRS category. I have tested positive for bartonella but both herbal and antibiotic treatments have failed, though they produced plenty of die off. I did a heavy methylation protocol while out in the desert for over a year and got nowhere with it. I will do infared saunas by zipping up my tent mid day and laying in there till i get a good sweat but i must be careful- if i sweat too much and lose too many electrolytes there will be hell to pay.

My current diagnoses are; toxic encephalopathy, bartonella encephalopathy, mast cell activation, secondary adrenal insufficiency, dysautonomia.

I'm on a bunch of antihistamines and bartonella abx with my current doc but they're not helping much. He suspects i may have a protozoan infection that has never come up on any tests but that is just speculation.

I sort of feel like I'm almost out of ideas now. The effects of everything I've done over the past 3 years haven't been cumulative at all. I can say my chemical sensitivites are noticeably better, but that's really all i got.

I'm not sure what to do next. I feel like there probably isn't a huge, major protocol that I'm missing but I'm all ears for any and all suggestions regardless. Thank you for your time if you've read this far.

[@Aerose91](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/10465/) While my situation isn't as severe as yours, I understand the frustration that comes from having issues with mast cell activation and histamine intolerance. Since 2014 I have had issues with nocturnal itching. The summer of 2016 I made a big turn around from following Kruse's recommendations. But then I went away to school, and my symptoms came back twice as bad as they ever were. There was a good 15 months where my sleep was absolute trash: itching, waking up in the middle of the night, being anxious about going to bed, etc.

Now, the itchiness is less of a problem, but I think I have some neuro-degeneration from some of the things I did to keep myself going. I'm supposed to be looking for professional work now, but I feel so far away from having the capability to work an intellectual job that I've considered options like moving to North Dakota to work as a fracker.

While my situation is bad, I have improved a lot thanks to some guidance from a guy named Ryan Frisinger. He has an impressive knowledge base that includes biophysics, genetics, mold toxicity, homeopathics, functional medicine, and traumatic therapy.

In my experience, mold is devastating if you have the genetic susceptibility to it and it can't be ameliorated with the typical biophysical modalities.

### Sean Waters — 2018-09-05T17:48:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-been-3-years.21983/post-250245>

Ohhhh wow.... Yes, you guys all need to head to my Journal:

Mast Cell/ Histamine issues have been the topic of the last 3 or 4 pages: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/page-10](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/page-10)

In short:
NREM Sleep is the key to your nocturnal flare ups of histamine from my n=1. It completely overrides that histamine problem for some reason. NREM sleep is vital in T-Cell Clonal Selection.... you know how our immune cells are supposed to NOT attack our tissues? Crazy right, considering how that seems to be the norm these days.

Well NREM sleep has been shown to be key to that process. It stops that Mast Cell degranulation and starts recognising tissues as normal and stops the overreactions.

So most NREM sleep occurs before midnight. So the old wives tale: Every hour before 12 is worth 2. Well, that's actually true from what I've found.

To get to sleep that early, I made myself wake up earlier to increase the Adenosine build up @ 8pm (makes you tired) - but also, completely cut out all inflammatory factors. You need to dramatically lower Cortisol in the latter part of the day to stop any Wakefulness due to a high cortisol. That's a fairly simple task: Eat your carbs in the AM, and Zero carb in the PM with lots of Omega 3 (think fillet of fish with Ghee or coconut oil). This is basically the Leptin Rx and Epi Paleo Rx. "no eating after 5pm etc.".

Sunsets really help with lowering cortisol. Daylight UV exposure lowers Adrenaline. Which, if you have shitty cortisol receptors (inflamed for any reason) and low cortisol you will feel tired in the sun. The Leptin Reset + Epi Paleo Rx are key for this I've found.

But everything you are doing should target lowering inflamm = lowering cortisol = sleeping better. Sleep is 1/3 of every day of our lives, it is so underrated it is a fucking joke. It is where all the Immune Problems stem from in my view (I believe from n=1, not a fact).

I've started taking 2-3000mg of Curcumin, expensive yes @ £100 a month, but it has lowered my inflamm so much that my sleep is just incredible. Libido and Vivid Dreams will come flooding in and Histamine issues will just cease to happen when you get it right.

Message me for any Q's. My journal is a gold mine for this shit. Check it out

Honestly, I've been hacking SSKI for the past month and the results have been mind blowing (Brain Gut 12) - I would have both of those on hand to mitigate any infections that might come your way with the 5G - it sounds like mitophagy might still be \*off\* in your case If it wasn't, you could handle the cold - the methylene blue would help with the mitophagy - since you are doing some amount of fasting, you are making DDW when you burn your own fat - a water fast is my new go to suggestion for anyone - I go 12 hours at night ant 6-8 between breakfast and dinner - that seems to work great for me - IMHO - and really, who am I - the DDW might be a great move for you - it would at least answer the question if you were loaded with deuterium or not - probably you are - and - I think I vaguely remember anyway - you only have to do it for a couple of months - but I think it's $700.00 for those months - but janz is the go to guy for DDW and Scompy has played with it a bit - also, remember, it's been 3 years, but you've experimented with a lot of protocols and if they involved supplements, might not have been the way forward - sometimes, when I experiment I find myself taking 2 steps backward... when you were in these places, how long did you stay? Maybe you didn't stay long enough? The deuterium thing is huge - seafood/eggs/mushrooms (chaga/lions mane for the brain as it crosses the BBB) deplete deuterium -

the other thing is you might be sporting more heteroplasmy/deuterium/inflammation than other people because of your mom's heteroplasmy and her mom's heteroplasmy - like are they of northern european descent? I.e., it might take you a little longer to heal as you might have more deuterium than most - for Lyme, if you still think it's that, you might take cholestyramine which will clear the toxins that are stored in your fat - unless fasting does that too...

Also, I would get some l-serine - it will deplete deuterium and improve your mind - at least it did mine - you could do bone broths - but grass fed bones around here are $18.00 for 6 bones... R they nuts! Adding l-serine I can now remember my dreams, sweat at the proper times and recall stuff that astounds me - not to mention, if you take 3 grams prior to sleep, you have sleep onset within 15 minutes - and sleep is what repairs you - the stuff \*really\* takes the edge off -

The baking soda increases the potential of hydrogen - basically it knocks it up to a higher orbital - thus taking it out of ground state - this does a few things - it increases the bonding - creates an EZ and if you then add UV light, then you increase the size of the EZ - kinda like IR light is a direct hit to the ass end of the mitochondria and so it makes water, UV light then increases the PH of that water - something to that effect anyway - they have done studies with 4th stage kidney disease and all they do is 1tsp of baking soda 3 times a day and they've had reversals - I think this is also why ozone with UV would work better because it pops up the electrons to a higher orbital -

Take care and thanks for dropping by

When I was done (after about an hour) I felt almost nauseous, which I chaulked up to bad ground currents.

# Thread: Low dopamine state person wants to get better through nature...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-dopamine-state-person-wants-to-get-better-through-nature.21985/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tropicalislandman — 2018-09-02T06:19:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-dopamine-state-person-wants-to-get-better-through-nature.21985/post-249928>

Hi guys,

This one is an interesting post for quantum biohackers to sink their teeth into. A real opportunity to help someone close to me.

Been following Dr Kruse’s work for about 10months now. I’ve listened to most of his podcasts at least twice. I’m currently listening to the audiobook Life on the Edge by Jim Khalili.

I’m also a nurse on a remote island in the tropics of Capricorn, so my understanding of biology is decent. It is also worth noting that the island is an alcohol and drug free island.

I have a cousin who is dear to me, he’s in his late 30s, been in a low dopamine state since his teens. He recreationally resorts to the use of substances such as methamphetamine and marijuana. He is a high level manager in a tech company. Smokes approx 10 cigarettes a day, loves drinking alcohol and high sugar drinks.

He loves nature and feels as though getting out of the big city and getting in touch with the land would do him good. We’ve had an extensive chat about quantum biology and how biophysics is what we lack in our modern medical paradigms. At the end of our chat, he said “You’re abit of a mad scientist, can I be your first first subject?”, I explained to him how he’s likely in a low dopamine state which is influences his thinking and his actions. He added that a lifetime of tobacco, alcohol and recreational drugs have probably contributed to this. He is an articulate intellectual, was captain of his debating team in university. He recognises that he is depressed. He is staunchly against anti-depressants, his family doctor has tried many times to give it another go.

He is approx 5ft 10inch, a slender 58kg. As most users are slender. Genetically half south east asian and half caucasian.

I’ve been digging around the forums and through my own feeble knowledge. I wanted to keep his quantum care plan as simple as possible, so as to not over load him. He too is fascinated by quantum biology and looks forward to testing each hypothesis.

1) At least 30mins of sun in the morning approx 0700, shoes off grounded. Difficult to do the sphinx position on the island as there are no areas of privacy.

2) At least 60mins of of sun near the time of sunset, grounded. He loves the sun and this part won’t be hard for him.

3) At least 1000mL of water per day. There is a fresh water spring near my place. This one is going to be hard for him, he is water averse and loves sugar filled drinks. However, after an indepth discussion on the mammallian battery he agreed to drink water, but that he might have to add a teaspoon of sugar for taste.

4) He plans to keep busy during the day with fishing and exploring the island. Also playing basketball with the locals, and teaching the sport of rugby to the kids.

5) Large focus on returning circadian rhythms back to baseline. Minimal light after sunset and blue blockers if watching a screen.
6) I really want to do some baseline labs, but on the island we don’t have many of the labs that the forum goes by. Is there an alternative to BUN measurements? Our pathology services can get Urea levels, but not nitrogen. I have access to Urinalysis sticks? Can get vitamin D levels but at the same unit of measurement as what you guys mention here. I can do full blood count, standard electrolytes urea and creatinine, but the unit of measurement is likely to be different. Our pathology results get flown to Australia. Can also do venous blood gases. Any clinicians out there? How long should I wait to retest his labs?

I’m looking for any other suggestions towards his quantum care plan. If anyone could direct me to any blog posts I should know indepth. I know that CT should be on there, but I think it would be asking for too much as I know for a fact that his threshold for cold would be zilch. I don’t want to overwhelm him with too many daily ‘To Do’s’, I do plan on introducing it at a later stage. Maybe after a month of sunlight, spring water and good sleep. DHA will hopefully come from him eating fish that he catches. He is big a believer in grounding, even before I mentioned Einstein’s Photo-electric effect. What am I missing?

Thank you all in advance, love you all

### Jack Kruse — 2018-09-02T17:05:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-dopamine-state-person-wants-to-get-better-through-nature.21985/post-249959>

Leather soles on his shoes. When he can connect to earth direct DO IT.

### tropicalislandman — 2018-09-06T12:36:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-dopamine-state-person-wants-to-get-better-through-nature.21985/post-250343>

Noted, can't believe I got a reply from the man himself

He's a big believer in earthing, he walks barefoot most places he goes anyway.

Thank you Captain Kruse!

# Thread: DEPRESSION and nnEMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-and-nnemf.21987/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-09-02T15:21:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-and-nnemf.21987/post-249950>

This disease has a big stigma attached to it. This is the reason why many avoid physician care and default to religious leaders for guidance.

Physicians tend to opt for biochemical and Big Pharma answers for depression. Religious leaders default to what they know best and tend to only seek the spiritual answers. While this is admirable for some, it often ignores the fact that bio-physics has practical answers as well for this condition

A few of the most practical pieces of advice for the public when dealing with depression are as follows -

1\. Eliminate blue light exposure after dark. The reason is that melatonin production is mediated by the specific frequencies of light we expose ourselves to. Quality of sleep is critical to our body's natural healing processes.

Get blue-blocker glasses. I know it's difficult for folks who have to be up late studying and preparing for messages, meetings, and events.

2\. Turn wifi & cell phones off at night at least 3 hours before bed. Hormones and neurotransmitters regulate our mood and other biological functions. Leptin and melatonin need 2-4 hours of dark to operate under the cover of darkness. The release of hormones and neurotransmitters is determined by endogenous as well as environmental voltage levels caused by electric fields at specific frequencies. Here's some research to back this point up.

Microwave frequency electromagnetic fields (EMFs) produce widespread neuropsychiatric effects including depression---
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26300312](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26300312)

Again, difficult for shift working folks... but people do still use ethernet cords to connect their computer to the internet.

3\. See every sunrise and sunset the rest of your life and this will eliminate the need for man's pills for symptoms. Put your feet on the earth barefoot for 45-60min a day and get sunlight. It's a natural antioxidant. We're electrical beings. Wearing rubber on the bottom of our feet Blocks the subtle electric exchange between our body and the earth we've had for thousands of years. Sunlight is our most natural, efficient and healthiest source of Vitamin D.

When you get your downtime, don't just sit in front of the TV/laptop or stay inside. Make yourself get outside away from man's creation and immersed in Nature's creation.

4\. Stop eating highly processed and refined foods not built by photosynthetic webs. It's easy as a modern human to eat out all the time these days. Stop snacking and consider how leptin works. When the sun rises you eat like a King, lunch like a prince and dine as a pauper. As the sun's light ages every day your food intake should drop. Using food fuels incorrectly with sunlight is what feeds mitochondrial disease epidemics. Eat more seafood when possible, as it's our most natural, efficient, and healthiest source of DHA and EPA. Our body metabolizes seafood. Humans are incapable of using DHA in a processed supplement.

This is how change begins

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=249950)
>
> This disease has a big stigma attached to it. This is the reason why many avoid physician care and default to religious leaders for guidance.
>
> Physicians tend to opt for biochemical and Big Pharma answers for depression. Religious leaders default to what they know best and tend to only seek the spiritual answers. While this is admirable for some, it often ignores the fact that bio-physics has practical answers as well for this condition
>
> A few of the most practical pieces of advice for the public when dealing with depression are as follows -
>
> 1\. Eliminate blue light exposure after dark. The reason is that melatonin production is mediated by the specific frequencies of light we expose ourselves to. Quality of sleep is critical to our body's natural healing processes.
>
> Get blue-blocker glasses. I know it's difficult for folks who have to be up late studying and preparing for messages, meetings, and events.
>
> 2\. Turn wifi & cell phones off at night at least 3 hours before bed. Hormones and neurotransmitters regulate our mood and other biological functions. Leptin and melatonin need 2-4 hours of dark to operate under the cover of darkness. The release of hormones and neurotransmitters is determined by endogenous as well as environmental voltage levels caused by electric fields at specific frequencies. Here's some research to back this point up.
>
> Microwave frequency electromagnetic fields (EMFs) produce widespread neuropsychiatric effects including depression---
> [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26300312](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26300312)
>
> Again, difficult for shift working folks... but people do still use ethernet cords to connect their computer to the internet.
>
> 3\. See every sunrise and sunset the rest of your life and this will eliminate the need for man's pills for symptoms. Put your feet on the earth barefoot for 45-60min a day and get sunlight. It's a natural antioxidant. We're electrical beings. Wearing rubber on the bottom of our feet Blocks the subtle electric exchange between our body and the earth we've had for thousands of years. Sunlight is our most natural, efficient and healthiest source of Vitamin D.
>
> When you get your downtime, don't just sit in front of the TV/laptop or stay inside. Make yourself get outside away from man's creation and immersed in Nature's creation.
>
> 4\. Stop eating highly processed and refined foods not built by photosynthetic webs. It's easy as a modern human to eat out all the time these days. Stop snacking and consider how leptin works. When the sun rises you eat like a King, lunch like a prince and dine as a pauper. As the sun's light ages every day your food intake should drop. Using food fuels incorrectly with sunlight is what feeds mitochondrial disease epidemics. Eat more seafood when possible, as it's our most natural, efficient, and healthiest source of DHA and EPA. Our body metabolizes seafood. Humans are incapable of using DHA in a processed supplement.
>
> This is how change begins

Great summary of the foundational points... whether dealing with depression or just about anything else.

# Thread: My Journey...too few electrons
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-too-few-electrons.21988/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matt Resinger — 2018-09-02T17:26:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-too-few-electrons.21988/post-249962>

Hi Folks,

44M in 41st latitude with an H6a1 haplotype

I became more interested in the material here after waking up about 3 years ago. I have worked in hospitals and nursing homes my entire life. I have worked with 1000’s of patients and have the luxury to observe lifestyles, morbidity and results. I frequently observe western medicine fails in terms of prevention and treatment. I have noticed the most success in improving lives with reducing unnecessary medications and found a niche in geropsychiatry, where the field encourages such.

My health started tanking in my 30’s after one too many double shifts, chasing overtime dollars. At first, it was a subtle decline. My GI absorption started to fail. I lost weight despite being thin. Then my L4 herniated without any severe trauma. I developed peripheral neuropathy, widespread muscle fasciculations and Sjogren’s disease. I chased it the only way I knew how. More tests, supplements and expert opinion. Rheum consult found HLA B27 +. GI consult workup/EGD/colonoscopy showed widespread “villous blunting, ulceration at terminal ileum”. MRCP without further help or diagnosis and labeled catch all dx of “IBS”. I agreed to Zenpep and fat-soluble vitamins, at the time.

My younger son was born and suffered a peculiar illness in which I could predict his fevers every month. His pediatrician insisted it was “daycare”. I knew better. After showing up with a spread sheet of fever tracking, I was able to predict the day his fever would spike. After, watching him have multiple seizures, they finally accepted my working diagnosis of PFAPA. Tonsil removal would help him out and I ultimately declined the steroids they offered. To add fuel to the fire, my father, who was extremely health conscious and fit, died after a very brief battle with prostate cancer. He wasn’t a blue light gym rat, but didn’t have the knowledge we do today in terms of circadian mismatch/arrythmia.

What was my initial approach? I read the quilt and I experimented. In my ignorance I started with food and then tackled environment. After a year of AM sardines, improved sleep and my energy retuned. My Sjogren’s reversed. I had a microdiscectomy for L4 disc herniation and central canal stenosis, which addressed my L leg radiculopathy (not so much the central back pain). I started upping my vitamin d via solar exposure and vit d supplement (not the best idea, but desperate).

I’m currently off supplements. I have a way to go, but feel 80% better. I blue block, airplane mode and minimize nnEMF when/where possible while remaining gainfully employed. Basically, when one mimic the dogs waking hours and routine and you are half way there. I read Becker’s book (fantastic). I’m currently reviewing webinars from 2016 and enjoy the dopamine spike of learning again.

My goals include:

Further hacking my immediate environment to maximize family health

Reverse fat absorption maintain weight and bring up cholesterol to normal range, along with vitamin d levels. Currently at 41. This will be a challenge with my latitude. Jet Blue points accumulating though.

Eventually integrate some of this knowledge with my patients with dementia and psychiatric illness to improve their quality of life, while staying within the guidelines of practice (which change like the wind)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-09-02T18:55:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-too-few-electrons.21988/post-249971>

Too few electrons is not a problem when you have the right amount of sunlight. In fact, UVA, UVB and the VDR all slow ECT flow while promoting proton flow via the ATPase.

### kris90 — 2018-09-02T19:42:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-too-few-electrons.21988/post-249973>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=249971)
>
> Too few electrons is not a problem when you have the right amount of sunlight. In fact, UVA, UVB and the VDR all slow ECT flow while promoting proton flow via the ATPase.

I'm getting curious about understanding the whole concept of sunlight slowing ETC. UVA/UVB and VDR slow ETC while the 42% of red light from the sun maintains the ATPase? Essentially the work is done for us from our environment and mitochondria work less?

When UVA/UVB is low in environment at high latitudes, we need higher electron flow in mitochondria to pump protons out of the matrix to get the same effect? Mitochondria release more heat when there is a steeper temperature gradient (i.e. winter/cold) and that heat keeps the ATPase going?

### Inna — 2018-09-02T20:03:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-too-few-electrons.21988/post-249980>

Welcome, Matt,
Am very happy to see another doc who follows Jack and understand the great value of his teaching for health. It is wonderful that you are applying Jacks protocols (connection to nature / disconnection from technology) gaining more electrons and teaching your patients to do the same. I practice pulmonary medicine, and am convinced that western medicine is doing more harm than good for health.
It is quite challenging for me to “stay within the guidelines of practice” while trying to improve quality of life for my patients.
My only hope that guidelines of practice would change with time to incorporate connection to nature disconnection from technology quantum health principles.
My mom suffered from dementia for the last 5-6 years of her life and I learned a lot while taking care of her. She was physically fairly healthy apart from dementia. We were able to keep her at her home till she died at 99 (only went to hospital for last 3 days for ‘comfort care’). I did my best to keep her away from medical establishment, as much as possible, stopped most of her meds (including) Lipitor, when she was in her 70s (probably too late).
Pfapa syndrome sound like autoimmune disease, perhaps your younger son is a canary in the mine, pointing to problem in the environment.
You might consider platinum membership with Jack, it is so worth it + you’d be supporting a great cause!
My daughter is also psychiatrist, on her last year of residency, however unlike you she is a firm believer in evidence (eminence) based medicine, not a mitochondriac. Fortunately she has my mitochondria and hence great health so far.
Best wishes for you and your family!
Inna

### Matt Resinger — 2018-09-02T21:59:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-too-few-electrons.21988/post-249991>

Hi Inna,

Ty for the encouragement. I am not a doctor, I am board certified as a family nurse practitioner and a psychiatric family nurse practitioner (PMHNP). I agree with everything you said. The problem here is that once you see Jack's material and learn it,...you can't un-see it. I did member up.

Your daughter will eventually come along. Once she sees the docs giving pharma talks for medications that are no better than placebo at 12 weeks (Nuplazid for example) and realizes that the only reason her colleagues are doing these pharma talks is for the $1500 speaking fee, she will come around.

As a new grad, it would be hard for your daughter to be awake yet and recognize the system we are trapped in. I think it takes a few years to notice that most of what exist within the 4 walls of the pharmacy can be quite harmful. Some exceptions include those with schizophrenia and schizoaffective disorder, for example. Outside of that, many want a quick fix without understanding the long term side effects.

It has been the story for ages......I showed slides the last two years in Vermont that laid it out......two ways.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-too-few-electrons.21988/post-250021>

Welcome Matt,
With deep appreciation for medical professionals like you and Inna working to put patient wellness where it should belong - at the forefront with the 'system' following, for efficient administration and for protection of patient and professional.
